transcript:
[18:27:25] director: Hi there. The first basket is a rabbit facing to the right. 

[18:27:47] matcher: Ok, got it 

[18:28:54] director: The second basket is a basic round basket that is round and has a handle. I realize this one may need some clarification. lol 

[18:30:07] matcher: Lol is there a rim around the top? If so, is it thicker? 

[18:30:41] director: yes. uniform brown color, thicker rim. 

[18:31:21] matcher: Ok,hopefully i got it. Lol 

[18:31:27] director: we can revisit if needed. the third basket is a rectangle, and the right side of the lid is propped open at a 45 degree angle. 

[18:31:45] matcher: Got it 

[18:32:40] director: the 4th basket is 2 tone white and light brown. the handle comes off the basket slightly split and becomes uniform at the top. 

[18:32:53] matcher: Ok, got it! 

[18:33:50] director: the 5th basket is fairly basic. it has a uniform brown color and a braided handle. 

[18:34:07] matcher: Is it taller or shorter? 

[18:35:04] director: the basket is fairly low but the handle is fairly tall and does not arch as much as the others 

[18:35:22] director: sort of squared off for lack of a better term 

[18:35:29] matcher: Ok, perfect. Got that one. 

[18:35:51] director: the 6th is a fat rodent that is looking towards you 

[18:36:06] matcher: Lol got you. 

[18:36:52] director: the 7th is 2 tone, darker and lighter brown 

[18:37:53] matcher: Does it look more square or round? I feel like I'm playing guess who. Lol 

[18:38:19] director: the handle and rim are a lighter brown and the basket itself is darker with a hint of the handle color. it looks more square 

[18:39:02] matcher: Ok, I think I got it. 

[18:39:03] director: the 8th is also 2 tone but looks more gray and brown and is round. 

[18:39:44] matcher: Ok, got it. 

[18:40:51] director: the 9th is a tall barrel shaped basket with 2 small handles. there are a few open white spots visible inside the top edge of the basket 

[18:41:14] matcher: Got you. 

[18:41:56] director: the 10th is the shallowest basket. it is light in color and is very shallow, almost like a frying pan 

[18:43:05] matcher: Pattern on it with no handle or different color rim with a handle? 

[18:43:41] director: it has a handle and a slightly browner rim than the body 

[18:43:56] matcher: Ok, were good. 

[18:44:23] director: the 11th is the shorter barrel shaped basket with 2 small handles 

[18:44:42] matcher: Got it. 

[18:45:17] director: the 12th looks like a purse or a tote bag. it has a slight curve to the rim 

[18:46:08] matcher: Got it

You can see slightly inside the basket and it almost looks like there is a second basket within it. You can see a small light brown line that looks like the rim of another basket. 

[20:12:31] matcher: okay, does it look like it has multiple handles on each side? 

[20:12:48] director: Yes, the right side looks like it has 2 handles very close together. 

[20:12:56] matcher: got it

transcript:
[20:17:18] matcher: hello 

[20:17:33] director: Hi. Going from the top left to right, 1-6 

[20:17:51] matcher: okay 

[20:18:08] director: Basket 1: Looks like a traditional picnic basket. It is light brown with a thick handle in the middle. The lid on the left is closed. The lid on the right is open and at an angle touching the handle and rim of the basket. 

[20:18:28] matcher: got it 

[20:19:33] director: Basket 2: It is a tall basket, potentially the tallest on the screen. It is brown. You can see inside the basket and see 4 thick white lines that are probably light shining in. You can also see a small thick white line on the left inner portion too. It has 2 handles that are thin on either side of the basket. 

[20:19:46] matcher: got it 

[20:21:11] director: Basket 3: It is brown, it almost has a flower pot look to it. It is shaded on the bottom right so it looks darker brown there than the rest of the basket. The rim looks like one thick solid piece that is brown with some black lines going across it horizontally. One in the middle, and one towards the top. You can see slightly into the top. The handle looks like one thick piece and is dark brown. 

[20:23:08] director: The basket almost looks like it is solid compared to the rest of the wicker baskets. 

[20:24:07] matcher: so it is darker brown in color and almost cylindrical in shape? 

[20:24:29] director: It is a darker brown, but it is not cylindrical. 

[20:24:58] director: From the bottom it widens out with a slight outward curve. 

[20:25:08] matcher: okay, got it 

[20:25:11] director: Basket 4: The handle is light brown and looks braided. The rim looks like 3 pieces of wicker going horizontally with about 8 pices of wicker going vertical. The basket itself looks like it is woven, similar to a belt through belt loops. With thin brown pieces going vertical and thicker darker brown pieces going horizontally. 

[20:26:42] matcher: okay, got it 

[20:26:45] director: Basket 5: Beige bottom, Beige rim with a spiraled dark brown piece on top of that. The handle is a thicker piece where it looks like spheres when it connects to the basket. The middle portion of the basket has dark brown pieces going horizontally, with 4 or 5 pieces going vertically on the outside of the horizontal pieces. 

[20:27:15] matcher: okay, got it 

[20:27:16] director: Basket 6: The top portion looks like a bunny facing the right. You can see 1 black eye and the ears are behind the head. 

[20:27:46] matcher: got it 

[20:27:59] director: Starting at the bottom left with # 7. The bottom portion has 2 tiny pieces that represent feet. It has the face of a small animal with some red eyes and whiskers facing you. 

[20:28:15] matcher: got that one 

[20:28:35] director: Basket 8: It is shallow and light brown. The rim is a darker brown. The handle is a thin light brown. Inside the basket is a circular pattern with a brown spot in the middle. 

[20:28:53] matcher: is it the shallowest basket? 

[20:28:57] director: Yes 

[20:29:03] matcher: okay, got that 

[20:29:40] director: Basket 9: The bottom is brown and looks woven. There are 8 wicker pieces that go from bottom to just under the top rim. The horizontal pieces look like a dark brown/green. The rim is brown and looks woven. The handle is one piece and connects to each side. 

[20:30:13] matcher: okay, got that 

[20:30:38] director: Basket 10: The wicker pattern is diagonal. The right side looks like it has multiple handles next to each other. The left side looks like there is a handle at the top of the rim and another handle below that on the inside. You can see inside the basket and see what looks like a light brown rim of another basket. 

[20:30:58] matcher: okay, got it 

[20:31:43] director: Basket 11: The rim is indented, basically curved downwards. It is the only basket I have that looks like that. The handle is light brown and the attachments look like they are clamping down on the rim. The rim looks braided or woven. You can see inside the basket, but really only the top portion. 

[20:32:16] matcher: okay, i think i got that 

[20:32:39] director: Basket 12: Bottom is brown. The top part of the basket has white horizontal lines. The handle has what looks a white wrapping on the left and right side. Below these wrappings the handle branches out in a Y shape until it connects to the rim. 

[20:33:50] matcher: okay, got it 

[20:33:58] director: ok 

[20:34:07] matcher: i will submit now 

[20:34:10] director: ok

transcript:
[19:49:43] director: Basket arrangement goes from left to right. Two rows, 6 in each row. First basket: bunny-shaped, facing the right 

[19:50:14] matcher: Got the first basket - bunny basket 

[19:51:35] director: Ok. gimme a second. A lot of these are similar so I need to find a way to dsitinguish them 

[19:53:11] director: The second basket is perhaps the least-porous looking out of all of them, very tight weave. The basket is not rectangular shaped, it is round and there is no top door/lid. It appears to have a slight luster 

[19:53:17] director: need more info? 

[19:53:31] director: Color of this second basket is also on the reddish-brown side 

[19:54:07] director: Third basket is only one with a visible lid. Bit of a pale brown color and rectangular in shape 

[19:54:45] director: Fourth basket has white accents and the top handle has four connective points to the bowl of the basket. 

[19:54:46] matcher: For the second basket does the upper part have a pattern of triangle shapes? 

[19:55:00] director: No, there is no visible pattern on the second basket. 

[19:55:36] matcher: Does the second basket have a handle? 

[19:55:52] director: Yes. Singular one, very round, semicircle shape. 

[19:56:30] matcher: I've narrowed the second basket down to two. Does it have shadow on the bottom right hand side 

[19:57:01] director: I would say so. But gimme one more descriptive question to be sure, if you can 

[19:58:14] matcher: Very tight weave, semi circle handle, and cannot see any of the inner basket from the top of the basket (looking inside) 

[19:58:41] director: Hmm. so I can see a very, very small sliver of the inside of the basket. 

[19:58:53] matcher: Ok got it! 

[19:59:04] director: Nice. Do you have 3 and 4? 

[19:59:24] matcher: Got the third and forth 

[20:01:05] director: Ok fifth one... it has a large handle, taller than the base of the basket. Handle has a distinct twisted pattern. Basket is loosely weaved and the base of the basket is interwoven with a slightly darker/greyer shade of brown 

[20:01:30] matcher: Got it! 

[20:01:37] director: Sixth basket (final basket of the first row) is bunny shaped, with red eyes and facing the camera 

[20:01:47] matcher: Got it! 

[20:03:05] director: 7th basket is tightly woven, and due to the angle we cannot see the inside of it at all. Base is a rectangle shape. Base has a greenish color while the handle and rims (both top and bottom) are brown. Tell me if you need more info 

[20:03:49] matcher: Got 7 

[20:04:35] director: 8th looks small and round. The handle and top rim is a medium brown. The base of it is two colors. A darker brown in the center, and two beige/off-white stripes on the top and bottom. 

[20:05:04] matcher: got it 

[20:05:28] matcher: great descriptions by the way! 

[20:05:57] director: 9th is tall and looks like a laundry basket. Cylindrical, very round, very tall. Two small loops as handles. 

[20:05:59] director: Thanks! 

[20:06:07] matcher: got 9 

[20:07:04] director: 10 is a light-brown, beige color. The top and bottom rims are a darker brown. We can see the entirety of the inside of the basket from the angle. Base is round, and shallow. 

[20:07:23] director: Handle is also the beige color, btw. 

[20:07:29] matcher: got it! 

[20:08:49] director: Ok, two more. 11th basket also has two small loops as handles, though the handles appear to be doubled. Cylindrical in shape, but shorter than 9th 

[20:09:12] matcher: got 11 

[20:10:35] director: 12th one is somewhat irregular shape. The 'mouth' of the base is curved downward at the center, slightly. There is a isible shadow under the basket, and the basket is turned at a slight angle. Tell me if you need more info! 

[20:10:44] director: visible* 

[20:11:11] matcher: I got it! I think I got all the baskets correct. I will submit the order now. 

[20:11:17] director: Ok great!

transcript:
[17:45:24] director: The first basket is facing right and looks like a stylized rabbit (round body, long ears laying flat back) 

[17:45:59] matcher: What's number 2 

[17:47:19] director: number 2 is dark brown, looks like a handbag. It has a thin arched handle, and a flat band going around the top of the body of the basket. The whole basket is very smooth looking. 

[17:47:43] matcher: is this correct? 

[17:48:21] director: I can't see the picture you're trying to show me 

[17:49:01] matcher: is it round or square 

[17:49:28] director: The bottom has rounded corners 

[17:50:02] matcher: is it all one color or is the band a different color? 

[17:50:12] director: All one color 

[17:50:24] matcher: ok, what is #3 

[17:51:37] director: 3 is rectangular, has a rough looking surface, has one flat and sort of wide handle in the middle, and two flap style lids 

[17:52:01] director: It's all one color 

[17:52:23] matcher: ok what is 4 

[17:54:19] director: 4 is two colors, a brown stripe on the top and bottom, and a light stripe in between. It's rectangular, but the ends are rounded, not flat. The handle is attached at each short end, and splits into 2 pieces at each end 

[17:55:20] matcher: what is 5 

[17:56:52] director: 5 is a shallow oval shape, with a single twisted handle attached at each narrow end. The body of the basket is woven with wide pieces, and the top edge of the basket is woven with narrow round pieces. 

[17:57:21] matcher: ok what is 6 

[17:59:43] director: 6 is a light colored rounded basked shaped like an animal (maybe a mouse) facing directly towards viewer. It has two pegs representing feet, two eyes, and two ears. 

[17:59:54] matcher: ok what is 7 

[18:01:56] director: 7 is rectangular, with a single handle attached to the short ends. The bottom is flat. The body of the basket is very dark. There's a lighter brown stripe on the bottom, and on the top, and the handle is the same lighter brown color. The lighter brown is reddish color 

[18:02:55] matcher: ok what is 8 

[18:04:50] director: 8 is a tall cylinder with a flat bottom. There's a decorative pattern of open rectangular holes around the top. There are 2 very small rounded handles on opposite edges. 

[18:05:14] matcher: ok what is 9 

[18:05:15] director: MY MISTAKE I JUST DESCRIBED 9 

[18:05:26] matcher: ok what is 8 

[18:06:31] director: 8 is a short round basked with a single handle. The body is dark, with a very light stripe on the top and on the bottom. 

[18:07:01] matcher: what is 10 

[18:08:23] director: 10 is a very shallow round with a flat bottom and a single handle. The basket is a light color, with a thin reddish brown stripe around the top edge. 

[18:08:43] matcher: what is 11 

[18:10:28] director: 11 is a smooth woven cylindar, slightly flared at the top, with a flat bottom. There are 2 arched handles, and each handle is actually two handles acting as one 

[18:11:34] matcher: what is 12 

[18:13:30] director: 12 is oval shaped, with flat sides, like a handbag. There's a single handle attached to the short ends. The bottom is flat. The top edge dips down in the middle and goes up towards the two short ends

[16:17:23] director: and the 12th basket looks like you took a basket and sort of pressed onto it from the back and front so it looked smooshed and the opening is oval shaped

[16:23:54] director: the 2nd basket is a long cylinder barrel shape with two very mini handles on its sides 

[16:25:43] director: the 3rd basket is brown with a very tight wound body so there's no holes visible in the basket, it's more like a tallish bowl shape and has a perfect semi-circle handle 

[16:26:29] director: the 4th basket has a basket that is shorter than the height of the handle, the handle has more of a rectangular shape with curved corners 

[16:27:14] director: the 5th basket has a basket that has the bottom and top has a white lined color while the body is much darker brown 

[16:27:50] director: the 6th basket looks like a rabbit and the head/face of the bunny rabbit is facing towards the right 

[16:28:16] director: the 7th basket looks like it has a rat head/face with eyes and whiskers and its facing directly forward at us 

[16:29:53] director: the 8th basket looks very short and low to the ground like a circular kiddie pool shape and you can see the bottom of the basket inside and it has the lightest color handle 

[16:30:35] director: the 9th basket has a body that looks more rectangular shaped with a very noticeably dark green woven body and an half oval shaped handle 

[16:31:18] director: the 10th basket is the shorter of the cylinder shaped baskets with a small handle on its left and right 

[16:31:52] director: the 11th basket looks like the middle is caved in if you look at the rim of the basket 

[16:32:38] director: the 12th basket has a basket where the top half is white and the handle has two noticeable white bands in it 

[16:32:45] director: if you have any trouble let me know! 

[16:32:53] matcher: got it! those were perfect

transcript:
[16:12:48] director: Hello. The first basket looks like a rabbit, but the rabbit's head is on the right side of the box 

[16:13:24] matcher: Got it. Rabbit head is facing right with ears folded back towards the left. 

[16:13:40] director: Yep, you got it 

[16:14:13] director: Next, a brown basket with a rounded bottom and one handle. I see a direct side view of this basket 

[16:15:11] matcher: I see two that match that description. One has a handle the same color as the basket, and the other has a darker, slightly burnt looking handle. 

[16:15:38] director: The one with the darker handle 

[16:15:52] matcher: Done 

[16:15:58] director: And you can see the side of the basket, not really down into it at all 

[16:16:05] matcher: Yup. 

[16:17:14] director: The next basket is a rectangular basket with to seperate lids (one on each side, covering the top of the basket). It also has a thicker handle and is medium brown 

[16:17:30] director: *two separate lids 

[16:18:03] matcher: One of the lids is slightly open and leaning on the handle? 

[16:18:07] director: Yes 

[16:18:12] matcher: Done. 

[16:19:28] director: The next basket is brown with a cream strip around the top part of the basket. It is round but elongated, sort of like an oblong shape. There is a single handle that splits in two at the points where is meets the basket. 

[16:20:30] matcher: I see two that possibly meet that description. That cream strip, is there a black strip separating it from the brown section at the bottom? Or no? 

[16:20:38] director: No black 

[16:20:46] matcher: OK. Done. 

[16:21:52] director: The next basket is also sort of oblong. It is medium brown The single handle is taller than the basket is deep, and the handle is twisted. I see a side view of the basket. 

[16:22:30] matcher: Done. 

[16:23:17] director: The next basket is a bunny basket. The bunny is facing directly to the camera, but slightly pointed to the left. The bunny has two little stubby feet. 

[16:23:28] matcher: Done. 

[16:24:46] director: The next basket is a weird color, kind of mossy green in the body of the basket but the top border and bottom border of the basket are medium brown. The single handle is also medium brown and not as tall as the basket is deep. It looks a bit rectangular, I see a direct side view 

[16:25:03] matcher: Done. 

[16:25:47] director: The next basket is round. It is a darker brown with a cream strip around the bottom and and at the top. The single handle is also rounded and is a little shorter than the body of the basket. 

[16:27:09] matcher: OK, done. 

[16:28:23] director: The next basket is a tall medium brown basket. It is round and it has to little handles that are rounded, one on each side. It also has these weird little cutouts around the top 

[16:28:44] matcher: Done. 

[16:30:01] director: The next basket is round and very shallow but wide. The basket is kind of a yellow color, with a medium brown lip around the top of the basket. It has a single large handle. 

[16:30:34] matcher: Done. 

[16:31:34] director: The next basket is round. It is tall and a bit wide. It is medium brown with two small handles (one on each side). 

[16:31:46] matcher: Done. 

[16:32:54] director: The last basket is a weird shape. It is brown, and kind of an oval. The top of it kind of dips in the middle on each side, and there is a single handle. The view is slightly from above 

[16:33:19] matcher: Done. I'm pretty confident we got them all. 

[16:33:26] director: Wonderful!

transcript:
[16:49:23] director: Basket 1 is a novelty wicker basket shaped like a duck/bird: rounded body with a beak-like front and a small eye detail, with the top forming a lid/handle along the ‘back’.

[16:49:33] matcher: Placing the novelty duck/bird-shaped wicker basket in position 1. Just to confirm: it’s the duck/bird profile (side view) rather than the round cat-face one, right? აღწ

[16:49:42] director: Yes—basket 1 is the duck/bird side-profile (not the round cat-face). Now basket 2: a deep, rounded bucket-style wicker basket with a very dark brown/black smooth arched handle; the body is medium-brown weave and looks more like a pot than an oval tray. Can you confirm when you’ve got that one in slot 2?

[16:49:53] matcher: I think I found it and I’m placing the deep rounded bucket basket with the very dark smooth arched handle into slot 2. Quick confirm: is the handle nearly black and thicker/smoother than the weave, with a slightly darker rim/band near the top?

[16:50:03] director: Yes, your basket 2 description matches (nearly black smooth handle with a darker rim near the top). Now basket 3: a boxy rectangular picnic basket with a tall handle in the middle, and a hinged lid that’s flipped open and sticking out to the RIGHT side. Please confirm when you place that in slot 3.

[16:50:13] matcher: Placed the boxy rectangular picnic basket with the tall center handle and the lid flipped open to the RIGHT in slot 3. Quick confirm: it’s the one with a single flap lid opened to the right (not the flat lidded trunk-style box)?

[16:50:23] director: Yes, basket 3 is the single flap lid opened to the RIGHT. Now basket 4: a light cream/tan oval wicker basket with one arched handle; the handle has small colored wrap accents (reddish) near where it meets the sides, and the whole basket looks paler than most others. Confirm when you’ve put that in slot 4.

[16:50:34] matcher: Got it—I’m placing the pale cream/tan oval basket with the single arched handle and the small reddish wrap accents at the handle attachments into slot 4. Quick confirm: the reddish bits are little wraps/ties right where the handle meets the sides, and the basket is overall very light/cream compared to the others?

[16:50:44] director: Yes—basket 4 is the very light cream oval, with little reddish wraps right where the handle meets the sides. Now basket 5: a squat, rectangular open basket with a very thick twisted-rope style arched handle (looks like braided cord) and chunky blocky weave. Please confirm when you place that in slot 5.

[16:50:56] matcher: Placing the squat rectangular open basket with the very thick twisted/rope-style arched handle into slot 5. Quick confirm: the handle looks like braided cord (not a flat wooden/wicker handle), right? Then I’m ready for basket 6.

[16:51:06] director: Yes—basket 5’s handle is a thick braided/rope-looking arch. Now basket 6: the round lidded novelty basket that looks like a cat face (front/top view) with painted whiskers and little ears on top, sitting on small feet. Confirm when you place that in slot 6.

[16:51:16] matcher: Placed the round lidded cat-face novelty basket (with painted whiskers, little ears, and small feet) into slot 6. Just confirming: it’s the cat-face one, not the duck/bird side-profile, correct? Ready for basket 7.

[16:51:28] director: Correct—basket 6 is the cat-face (not the duck). Now basket 7: a wide rectangular basket with a single arched handle, and a very distinctive two-tone body—green/teal vertical slats/panels with a natural tan/orange wicker frame. The front top rim has a scalloped/wavy edge. Please confirm when it’s in slot 7.

[16:51:38] matcher: Placed the wide rectangular two-tone basket (green/teal vertical panels with tan/orange frame and a scalloped/wavy front rim) into slot 7. Can you confirm that matches your basket 7? Ready for basket 8 after that.

[16:51:48] director: Yes, that matches basket 7. Basket 8: a small squat oval basket with one arched handle, mostly dark gray-brown wicker, and noticeable lighter/whitish bands around the lower part near the base. Please confirm when you’ve placed that in slot 8.

[16:51:59] matcher: I think I found basket 8 and I’m placing the small squat dark gray-brown handled basket with the lighter/whitish banding near the base into slot 8. Quick confirm: is it predominantly dark gray/charcoal with that pale band near the bottom (not the lighter tan oval handled baskets)?

[16:52:16] director: Yes—basket 8 is predominantly dark gray/charcoal, and it has that lighter/whitish banding around the lower part near the base (definitely not one of the pale tan/cream baskets).

[16:52:26] matcher: Great—slot 8 is confirmed. What’s basket 9 (shape/handle, any lid, colors or distinctive pattern)?

[11:18:45] matcher: I think I found basket 2: the deep rounded bucket with the very dark/black thick arched handle. I’m placing it in position 2. Can you confirm the handle is much darker than the basket body (almost black) and the weave is fairly uniform (not two-tone banded)?

[11:19:08] matcher: Locked in basket 2 as the deep rounded bucket with the nearly black thick arched handle. Please describe basket 3 (shape/handle/pattern/colors) so I can place it in position 3.

[11:19:22] director: Basket 3 is the rectangular picnic basket with a flat lid—its lid is hinged and shown open/flipped up to the RIGHT side. It has a tall upright handle rising near the middle/left, and the whole thing looks boxy like a lidded hamper. Confirm when you’ve selected it.

[11:19:35] matcher: Selected the boxy rectangular picnic basket with the flat lid flipped open to the RIGHT and the tall upright handle, and placed it in position 3. Just to confirm: the lid is clearly open to the right (not the fully closed rectangular trunk/hamper), correct? Ready for basket 4.

[11:19:48] director: Yes—basket 3 is the one with the lid clearly flipped open to the RIGHT. 

Basket 4: a small oval/boat-shaped basket with a light gray/whitewashed wicker body and a tan rim; it has an arched handle with little reddish/brown wrap accents near where the handle meets the sides. Please confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:20:02] matcher: I found the small oval basket with the light gray/whitewashed body, tan rim, and the little reddish wrap accents where the handle meets the sides, and I’m placing it in position 4. To confirm: the body is clearly pale/whitewashed compared to the rim, with the reddish wraps right at the handle attachment points?

[11:20:15] director: Yes, that’s basket 4: pale/whitewashed body with a tan rim and small reddish wraps right where the handle attaches. 

Basket 5: a squat, rectangular basket with a very thick twisted-rope/corded arched handle; the body is low and wide with a chunky brown weave. Confirm when you’ve selected it.

[11:20:29] matcher: I think I found basket 5: the squat low rectangular basket with the very thick twisted/corded handle. I’m placing it in position 5. Can you confirm the handle looks like a thick twisted rope (not a smooth wooden arch) and the basket is a low rectangle?

[11:20:42] director: Yes—basket 5 is the low, shallow rectangular one with the very thick twisted-rope looking handle (not a smooth wooden arch). The body is squat and wide. If that matches what you picked, lock it in for position 5.

[11:20:56] matcher: Locked basket 5 in position 5 (low shallow rectangle with the thick twisted-rope handle). Please describe basket 6—does it have a handle, and is it a tall container/hamper or a shallow tray?

[11:21:08] director: Basket 6 is the front-facing round animal-face basket (mouse/cat-like): a circular lidded basket with a face on top (whiskers, small colored eyes), no handle, and it sits on tiny little feet. Confirm when you’ve got it (it’s NOT the side-profile mouse from basket 1).

[11:21:21] matcher: Got it—I’m placing the front-facing round animal-face lidded basket with whiskers and tiny feet (no handle) into position 6. Just to confirm: it’s the round face-on one, not the side-profile mouse, right? Ready for basket 7.

[11:21:34] director: Yes, basket 6 is the round face-on animal basket (not the side-profile mouse). 

Basket 7: a rectangular basket with an arched handle, but it’s distinctive because the body is greenish/teal-toned while the rim/handle are orange-brown, and there’s a braided decorative band along the top front. Confirm when you’ve selected it.

[11:21:48] matcher: I found the rectangular basket with the green/teal body and orange-brown rim/handle with a braided band along the top front, and I’m placing it in position 7. Can you confirm the body is clearly green/teal (not just dark brown) and there’s one arched handle?

[11:21:59] director: Confirmed for basket 7: the body is clearly green/teal (not just dark brown), with a single arched handle and an orange-brown rim/handle plus that braided band along the top front.

[11:22:10] matcher: Locked basket 7 in position 7. Please describe basket 8 (shape, handle, colors/pattern, any distinctive details) so I can fill position 8.

[11:22:24] director: Basket 8: a small oval basket with a dark gray/blackish weave and a dark arched handle; the bottom has lighter tan/cream bands/stripes so it looks two-tone (dark top, lighter near the base). Please confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:22:39] matcher: I think I found basket 8: the small oval basket with the dark gray/black weave and dark handle, plus a lighter tan band near the base. I’m placing it in position 8. To confirm: it’s clearly two-tone with that light band at the bottom, right?

[11:22:51] director: Yes—basket 8 is the two-tone one with a lighter tan band near the bottom. 

Basket 9: a tall, straight-sided cylindrical wicker basket (like a small hamper) with an open top and TWO small loop handles near the rim (no big arched handle). Confirm when you’ve selected it.

[11:23:04] matcher: Selected the tall straight-sided cylindrical hamper basket with the open top and two small loop handles, and placed it in position 9. Just to confirm: it’s the tall cylinder with two small side loop handles and no lid, right? Ready for basket 10.

[11:23:17] director: Yes, basket 9 is the tall open cylinder with two small loop handles and no lid.

Basket 10: a very shallow ROUND tray-basket with a single arching handle over the middle; you can clearly see a spiral/circular weave pattern on the flat base. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:23:32] matcher: Placed the very shallow round tray basket with the single arched handle and the visible spiral weave base into position 10. Just to confirm: it’s the really flat, plate-like one where you mostly see the spiral pattern inside (not a deeper bowl-shaped basket), right? Ready for basket 11.

[11:23:44] director: Yes—basket 10 is the very flat, plate-like round tray with the spiral base.

Basket 11: a deep round tub/bucket basket with TWO thick side handles that stick up like little ears (no arching handle over the top). The sides have a noticeable diagonal/chevron weave. Confirm when you’ve selected it.

[11:23:59] matcher: I found the deep round tub/bucket with two thick side handles like little ears and the diagonal/chevron weave, and I’m placing it in position 11. To confirm: it’s the one with no arched handle over the top, just the two side handles and that chevron pattern, right? Ready for basket 12.

[11:24:13] director: Basket 12: a light-brown oval/rounded-rectangle basket with ONE arched handle, and the top rim is distinctive—two pointed/peaked corners so the rim dips slightly in the middle (kind of a wavy/peaked opening). transcript:
[11:30:55] director: Basket 1 (top-left) is a rectangular, boxy basket with one arched handle. The body is distinctly green/teal, while the rim and handle are orange-brown, and there’s a braided decorative band along the top front edge. Please confirm when you’ve selected it.

[11:31:09] matcher: Selected the rectangular boxy basket with the green/teal body, orange-brown rim/handle, and braided band along the top front, and placed it in position 1. Just confirming: it’s the only one with that green/teal body and orange-brown trim, right? Ready for basket 2.

[11:31:23] director: Yes—basket 1 should be the only green/teal body with orange-brown trim. 

Basket 2: a small oval basket with a pale gray/whitewashed body and a tan rim; it has one arched handle with little reddish/brown wrap accents at BOTH handle attachment points (and a light/white section on the handle near the top). Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:31:38] matcher: I found the small oval basket with the pale/whitewashed body, tan rim, and the reddish wraps at both handle attachment points (with a lighter/whitish section on the handle near the top). I’m placing it in position 2. Can you confirm the handle has that light/white segment near the top (not just a plain tan handle)?

[11:31:51] director: Yes—basket 2’s handle has that light/white segment near the top. 

Basket 3: the round, front-facing animal-face lidded basket (mouse/cat-like) with whiskers and small colored eyes; it has NO handle and sits on tiny little feet. Please confirm when you’ve selected it.

[11:32:06] matcher: I found the round, front-facing animal-face lidded basket with whiskers, colored eyes, no handle, and tiny feet, and I’m placing it in position 3. Can you confirm it’s the face-on round one (not the side-profile mouse with a snout/handle along its back)?

[11:32:19] director: Yes—basket 3 is the face-on round animal with whiskers and tiny feet (no handle). 

Basket 4: the side-profile mouse/rodent-shaped wicker basket—rounded body with a pointed snout facing RIGHT, a small eye on the side, and an integrated handle running along the top/back. Please confirm when you’ve selected it.

[11:32:33] matcher: I found the side-profile mouse/rodent basket with the snout pointing RIGHT and the handle running along its back, and I’m placing it in position 4. Just confirming: it’s the side-profile one (not the round face-on animal), correct? Ready for basket 5.

[11:32:47] director: Yes, basket 4 is the side-profile mouse (snout to the RIGHT). 

Basket 5: a squat, rounded-rectangle light-brown basket with ONE smooth arched handle; the key is the opening/rim is slanted/diagonal (one side sits higher than the other), not an even level rim. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:33:02] matcher: I think I found basket 5 and placed it in position 5: the squat light-brown rounded-rectangle with one smooth arched handle and a noticeably slanted/diagonal rim. Can you confirm the rim is clearly higher on one side (diagonal opening) and the handle is a plain smooth arch (not a thick twisted rope handle)?

[11:33:15] director: Yes—basket 5’s rim is clearly diagonal (one side higher) and the handle is a plain smooth arch, not the thick twisted-rope one.

Basket 6: a tall, straight-sided CYLINDER wicker basket (hamper-like), open top, with TWO small loop handles right near the rim—no big arched handle over the top. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:33:27] matcher: I’ve placed the tall straight-sided cylindrical open-top basket with two small loop handles near the rim into position 6. Can you confirm the two loop handles sit right at the rim on opposite sides (and there’s no big arched handle over the top)?

[11:33:39] director: Yes—basket 6 has two small loop handles right at the rim on opposite sides, and no big arched handle over the top.

Basket 7: a deep rounded bowl/bucket-shaped wicker basket with a VERY dark (almost black) thick arched handle over the top; the body is medium-brown and fairly uniform. Confirm when you’ve selected it.

[11:33:52] matcher: I think I’ve got basket 7: the deep rounded bowl/bucket with the very dark (almost black) thick arched handle. I’m placing it in position 7. Is the handle the darkest/blackest thick handle in the set, and is it definitely a deep bowl shape (not shallow or rectangular)?

[11:34:05] director: Yes—basket 7 is the one with the darkest/near-black thick arched handle in the whole set, and the body is definitely a deep rounded bowl/bucket (not shallow and not rectangular). If that matches what you picked, you’ve got basket 7 correct.

[11:34:18] matcher: Locked basket 7 in position 7 (deep rounded bowl with the darkest near-black thick arched handle). Please describe basket 8—shape, handle type/number, and any distinctive colors/patterns.

[11:34:31] director: Basket 8: a small OVAL basket with a dark gray/black woven body and a single dark arched handle; it’s very clearly two-tone because there’s a lighter tan/cream band around the bottom/base. Please confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:34:46] matcher: I found the small oval basket with the dark gray/black body, single dark arched handle, and the lighter tan/cream band around the bottom, and I’m placing it in position 8. Can you confirm it’s mostly dark with that distinct light band only at the base?

[11:34:59] director: Yes—basket 8 is mostly dark gray/black (including the handle) with a distinct lighter tan/cream band only around the bottom/base.

[11:35:11] matcher: Locked basket 8 in position 8. Please describe basket 9 (shape, handle type/number, and any distinctive pattern or color) so I can place it in position 9.

[11:35:24] director: Basket 9: a small rectangular tan wicker basket with a high arched handle that looks twisted/rope-like (not a smooth handle). The body is boxy and evenly woven—no whitewash and no slanted rim. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:35:39] matcher: I think I found it and placed it in position 9: the small tan rectangular/boxy basket with the high twisted rope-like arched handle. Quick check— is the handle a thick chunky twisted cord (not a thinner braid), and is the basket a low wide rectangle?

[11:35:51] director: For basket 9: yes it’s a low, boxy rectangular tan basket, but the handle is a *thin twisted/braided* arch (neat twist), not the super thick chunky rope handle. If yours has a very thick corded handle, that would be a different one.

[11:36:03] matcher: I may have grabbed the wrong one for basket 9 earlier. I’m switching position 9 to the low boxy tan rectangle with the thinner twisted/braided handle (not the super thick rope handle). Can you confirm that’s correct? If yes, please describe basket 10 next.

[11:36:17] director: Yes—your switch for basket 9 sounds right (low tan rectangle with a thinner twisted/braided arch handle). 

Basket 10: a deep round tub/bucket basket with TWO thick side handles that stick up like little ears (no arched handle over the top). The sides have a clear diagonal/chevron weave pattern. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:36:33] matcher: Before we move to basket 10, I want to make sure I’ve got basket 9 locked: is it the low tan rectangular basket with the *thin* twisted/braided arched handle (not the very thick rope-handle version), with a uniform tan weave and no extra bands? If yes, I’m set—then I’ll place basket 10 as the deep round tub with two side “ear” handles and chevron weave.

[11:36:46] director: Yes—basket 9 is the low, boxy tan rectangular basket with a relatively THIN twisted/braided arched handle (not the super-thick rope/corded handle). The weave is uniform tan with a level rim and no extra bands. If that matches, lock basket 9; then we’ll do basket 10 next.

[11:37:01] matcher: Locked basket 9 (low boxy tan rectangle with the thin twisted/braided arched handle). For basket 10, I’m selecting the deep round tub/bucket with two thick side “ear” handles and the diagonal/chevron weave and placing it in position 11—can you confirm that’s the intended one (no arched handle over the top)?

[11:37:12] director: Yes, that description matches basket 10: deep round tub with TWO thick side “ear” handles and a diagonal/chevron weave—no arched handle over the top. But it should go in position 10 (not 11). Please move it to slot 10 and confirm.

[11:37:26] matcher: Moved the deep round tub/bucket with two side “ear” handles and chevron weave into position 10 (not 11). Please confirm it’s the only one with two side handles and no over-the-top handle, and then describe basket 11.

[11:37:38] director: Confirmed—basket 10 is the only deep round tub with TWO side “ear” handles and no over-the-top arch. 

Basket 11: the very shallow ROUND tray (almost plate-like) with a single arched handle over the middle; the inside base has a very obvious spiral/circular weave. Please confirm when you’ve selected it.

[11:37:54] matcher: Placed the very shallow round tray/plate basket with the single arched handle and the obvious spiral weave base into position 11. Just to confirm: it’s the flattest round tray where the spiral pattern is clearly visible inside, right? Ready for basket 12.

[11:38:08] director: Yes—basket 11 is the flattest round tray with the spiral base. 

Basket 12: the boxy rectangular picnic/hamper basket with a FLAT LID that’s hinged and shown flipped OPEN to the RIGHT side; it also has a tall upright handle rising near the middle/left. Please confirm when you’ve selected it.

[11:38:22] matcher: Placed the boxy rectangular picnic/hamper basket with the flat lid flipped open to the RIGHT and the tall upright handle into position 12. Just to confirm: it’s the only one with the lid clearly open to the right (not a closed box), correct?

transcript:
[11:38:36] director: Basket 1 (top-left) is a very shallow ROUND tray basket with one arched handle over the middle—you can clearly see a tight spiral/circular weave on the flat base. It’s the flattest, plate-like one. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:38:50] matcher: Placed the very shallow round tray/plate basket with the single arched handle and the obvious spiral weave base into position 1. Just confirming: it’s the flattest round tray where the spiral pattern is clearly visible inside, right? Ready for basket 2.

[11:39:04] director: Yes, basket 1 is the flattest round tray with the spiral base. 

Basket 2: a small boxy RECTANGULAR wicker basket with a tall centered arched handle that looks twisted/braided (rope-like). The body weave is very tight and grid-like, all light brown. Please confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:39:18] matcher: I think I found it—the small light-brown boxy rectangular basket with the tall centered braided/twisted handle and tight grid-like weave. I’m placing it in position 2. Can you confirm the handle is a neat thin braid (not a thick smooth arch) and the body weave is very tight/square-grid?

[11:39:31] director: Yes—basket 2 is the small boxy rectangular one with the very tight square/grid weave, and the handle is a neat *thin* twisted/braided arch centered on top (not a thick smooth arch). If that matches, lock it in for position 2.

[11:39:43] matcher: Locked basket 2 in position 2 (small boxy rectangle with tight grid weave and a thin braided/twisted centered handle). Please describe basket 3 (shape/handle/colors/pattern) so I can fill position 3.

[11:39:55] director: Basket 3 is the side-profile mouse/rodent-shaped wicker basket: a rounded body with a pointed snout facing RIGHT, a small eye detail, and a handle that runs along the top/back (not a separate arch handle). Please confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:40:09] matcher: Got it—I’m placing the side-profile mouse/rodent basket (snout pointing RIGHT, small eye, handle along the back) into position 3. Just to confirm: it’s the side-profile one, not the round front-facing animal-face basket, right? Ready for basket 4.

[11:40:26] director: Yes—basket 3 is the side-profile mouse (snout to the right), not the face-on animal. 

Basket 4: a rectangular boxy basket with ONE arched handle; the body is distinctly green/teal, while the rim and handle are orange-brown, and there’s a braided decorative band along the top front edge. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:40:42] matcher: Placed the rectangular boxy basket with the green/teal body, orange-brown rim/handle, and the braided band along the top front into position 4. Just to confirm: it’s the only one with that green/teal body and orange-brown trim, right? Ready for basket 5.

[11:40:56] director: Yes—basket 4 is the only green/teal one with orange-brown trim. 

Basket 5: a deep ROUND tub/bucket basket with TWO small upright side handles (like little ears), and a very noticeable diagonal/chevron weave on the sides. No arched handle over the top. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:41:12] matcher: I found the deep round tub/bucket with the two small upright side “ear” handles and the diagonal/chevron weave, and I’m placing it in position 5. To confirm: the handles are short stubs integrated into the rim (not loop handles), and the weave is clearly diagonal/chevron, right?

[11:41:25] director: Yes—basket 5 is the deep round tub with two short upright side “ear” handles and a clear diagonal/chevron weave (no over-the-top arch).

Basket 6: a deep rounded bucket/bowl basket with ONE very dark (almost black) thick arched handle over the top; the body is medium-brown and fairly uniform. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:41:40] matcher: I think I found basket 6—the deep rounded bowl/bucket with the very dark (near-black) thick arched handle—and I’m placing it in position 6. Is the handle the darkest/near-black one and noticeably thick, with a deep rounded bowl shape (not a taller straight-sided bucket)?

[11:41:52] director: Yes—basket 6 is the one with the darkest near-black, noticeably thick arched handle, and the body is a deep rounded bowl/bucket (not the tall straight-sided cylinder and not any rectangular/oval basket). If that matches what you picked, lock it in for position 6.

[11:42:03] matcher: Locked basket 6 in position 6 (deep rounded bowl with the thick near-black arched handle). Please describe basket 7—shape, handle type/number, and any distinctive colors/patterns.

[11:42:15] director: Basket 7 is the tall straight-sided CYLINDER wicker basket (hamper-like), open top, with TWO small loop handles right at the rim on opposite sides—no big arched handle over the top. Please confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:42:29] matcher: I found the tall straight-sided open-top cylinder/hamper with two small loop handles at the rim (no over-the-top handle) and I’m placing it in position 7. Just to confirm: the two handles are small loop tabs right at the rim on opposite sides, correct? Ready for basket 8.

[11:42:44] director: Yes—basket 7 has two small loop-tab handles right at the rim on opposite sides.

Basket 8: a light-brown oval/rounded-rectangle basket with ONE smooth arched handle, and the key feature is the opening/rim is slanted/diagonal (one side clearly higher than the other). No whitewash, no green, no dark handle. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:42:58] matcher: I think I found basket 8: the light-brown oval/rounded-rectangle with one smooth arched handle and a clearly slanted/diagonal rim. I’m placing it in position 8. Quick confirm: is the rim higher on the left and lower on the right (diagonal opening)?

[11:43:11] director: Yes—basket 8 is the light-brown oval/rounded-rectangle with the clearly diagonal opening; in my view the rim is higher on the LEFT and dips lower on the RIGHT, with a smooth plain arched handle (not twisted). If that matches, lock it in for position 8.

[11:43:25] matcher: Locked basket 8 in position 8 (smooth arched handle, diagonal rim higher on the left). Please describe basket 9—shape, handle type/number, and any distinctive colors/patterns.

[11:43:37] director: Basket 9 is the front-facing round animal-face lidded basket: you see whiskers and small colored eyes on the lid, it has NO handle, and it sits on tiny little feet. (Not the side-profile mouse from basket 3.) Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:43:50] matcher: Placed the front-facing round animal-face lidded basket (whiskers, small colored eyes, tiny feet, no handle) into position 9. Just confirming: it’s the round face-on one with no handle at all, right? Ready for basket 10.

[11:44:04] director: Yes—basket 9 is the round face-on animal with NO handle. 

Basket 10: the small oval basket with a pale gray/whitewashed body and a tan rim; it has one arched handle with little reddish/brown wrap accents right where the handle attaches on BOTH sides. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:44:18] matcher: I found the small oval basket with the pale gray/whitewashed body, tan rim, and the reddish wrap accents where the handle attaches on both sides, and I’m placing it in position 10. Can you confirm it has those reddish wraps at BOTH attachment points (and the body is clearly whitewashed/pale)?

[11:44:30] director: Yes—basket 10 is the pale/whitewashed oval basket: the body is clearly light grayish compared to the tan rim, and it has the small reddish/brown wraps at BOTH handle attachment points. If that matches, lock it in for position 10.

[11:44:44] matcher: Locked basket 10 in position 10 (pale/whitewashed oval with tan rim and reddish wraps at both handle attachment points). Please describe basket 11 so I can fill position 11.

[11:44:58] director: Basket 11: a deep ROUND tub/bucket basket with NO over-the-top handle; it has two small side handles at the rim (loop/tab “ears”), and the weave looks tight and slightly diagonal but not the bold chevron of basket 5. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:45:13] matcher: I think I found basket 11: the deep round tub with no over-the-top handle and two small side loop/tab handles at the rim, with a tighter subtle diagonal weave (not the bold chevron one). I’m placing it in position 11. Quick check: are the side handles flatter loop/tabs sticking out sideways (not thick upright stubs)?

[11:45:26] director: Yes—basket 11’s handles are flatter little loop/tabs that stick out sideways at the rim (not the upright stubby “ears” like basket 5). It’s a deep round tub and definitely not the tall cylinder hamper. If that matches, lock basket 11.

[11:45:39] matcher: Locked basket 11 in position 11. For basket 12, I’m selecting the remaining small light-brown round basket with a single thin upright handle and placing it in position 12—can you confirm it’s the one with the simple thin handle (not the deeper bucket with a thick dark handle)?

[11:45:53] director: Yes—basket 12 is the small round bucket-shaped basket with ONE smoother medium-brown arched handle (not the thick near-black handle from basket 6). It’s rounder/deeper than the flat tray and not boxy/rectangular; the rim is level (not the diagonal rim basket). If that matches what you picked, you’re set for position 12.

[11:46:08] matcher: Great—then I’m locking basket 12 as the small round bucket with the single smooth medium-brown arched handle in position 12. Just to confirm: it has no extra side handles and the handle is medium-brown (not the near-black thick one), right? If yes, I’m ready to submit.

[11:32:49] director: Confirmed—basket 6 is the cat-face one. [11:35:13] matcher: I’ve placed the very shallow round tray-like basket with the spiral/coil base and a single arched handle in position 10. To confirm: it’s the really flat one with a low rim and obvious spiral on the bottom (not a deeper round basket), right?

transcript:
[22:08:44] director: Basket 1 is a novelty animal-shaped wicker basket—looks like a duck/bird: a big rounded body with a small head/beak sticking out on the right, with a little eye detail, and a curved arched top/back (like a lid/handle) over the body. Let me know when you’ve got that one.

[22:08:56] matcher: I think I found it—the duck/bird-shaped wicker basket with the beak on the right. I’m placing that in position 1. Just to confirm: it’s the duck/bird one (not the round cat-face one), right? If yes, go ahead with basket 2.

[22:09:09] director: Yes—basket 1 is the duck/bird (not the round cat-face). 

Basket 2: a deep, round bucket-shaped wicker basket with a very dark (almost black) thick arched handle; the body is medium-dark brown with a lighter band right around the rim. Confirm when you’ve placed it.

[22:09:21] matcher: Placed basket 2 as the deep round bucket with the very dark/black thick arched handle and a lighter band at the rim. Quick confirm: the handle is noticeably darker (almost black) than the body, right? If so, describe basket 3.

[22:09:34] director: Yes—basket 2’s handle is much darker (almost black) than the basket body. 

Basket 3: a light tan, rectangular picnic-style basket with a tall handle in the center, and a hinged lid that’s open/tilted out to the RIGHT side. Please confirm when you’ve placed it.

[22:09:46] matcher: Got it—I placed the light tan rectangular picnic basket with the tall center handle and the lid flipped open to the RIGHT in position 3. Quick confirm: the lid is visibly open to the right (not a closed trunk-style basket), correct? If yes, please describe basket 4.

[22:10:01] director: Yes—basket 3 has the lid clearly open to the RIGHT. 

Basket 4: an oval basket with a single arched handle, but it’s very two-tone—pale/whitish wicker sides with a warmer tan base/rim. The handle is fairly slim and there are small reddish/burgundy accent bands where the handle attaches near the rim. Confirm when you’ve got basket 4.

[22:10:15] matcher: I think I found basket 4—the oval, two-tone one with pale/whitish sides, warmer tan base/rim, and the little burgundy wraps where the slim handle attaches. I’m placing it in position 4. Can you confirm that it’s mostly pale on the sides with those two burgundy attachment bands (not the one with a darker mid-band)?

[22:10:28] director: Confirmed—basket 4 is the one with mostly pale/whitish sides, a warmer tan base/rim, and the two small burgundy/red wraps where the slim handle attaches (not the darker mid-band one). Ready for basket 5?

[22:10:40] matcher: Yes, ready—please describe basket 5 (shape, handle, colors/pattern, and any distinctive details like a lid or special trim).

[22:10:54] director: Basket 5: a small, squat rectangular wicker basket with low sides and a very chunky rope‑twisted/ braided-looking arched handle. The body is a tight square-weave in warm golden-brown, and there’s no lid or special color panels. Confirm when you’ve placed it.

[22:11:07] matcher: I found the small squat rectangular basket with the chunky rope-twisted handle and placed it in position 5. Quick check: the handle is really thick and braided/rope-like (chunkier than the rim), right? If yes, please describe basket 6.

[22:11:19] director: Yes—basket 5’s handle is very thick and rope-braided. 

Basket 6: the novelty round lidded basket that looks like a cat face—perfectly circular, with two little ears on top and painted eyes/whiskers/nose on the lid; it also sits on tiny little feet. Confirm when you’ve placed it.

[22:11:33] matcher: Placed basket 6 as the perfectly round lidded cat-face basket with little ears and tiny feet. Just to confirm: it’s the painted cat face one (not the duck/bird), right? If yes, please describe basket 7.

[22:11:46] director: Yes—basket 6 is the painted cat-face one (not the duck). 

Basket 7: a rectangular handled basket with a strong two-tone look—green/teal woven body (vertical-looking slats) framed by warm tan/orange wicker. It has a scalloped/wavy trim along the top edge. Confirm when you’ve placed basket 7.

[22:12:00] matcher: Placed basket 7 as the rectangular handled basket with the green/teal body framed in warm tan/orange and the scalloped/wavy top trim. Can you confirm it has that wavy top border over the green/teal paneling? If yes, please describe basket 8.

[22:12:13] director: Yes—basket 7 has that wavy/scalloped top border. 

Basket 8: a compact, short oval basket with a dark gray/black arched handle; the body is gray-brown wicker with lighter/whitish bands near the bottom (two-tone, not all tan). Confirm when you’ve placed it.

[22:12:26] matcher: I think I found basket 8: the compact oval with the dark gray/black handle and the gray-brown body with lighter bands near the bottom. I’m placing it in position 8. Can you confirm it’s the overall gray/dark one with that pale banding (not a more orange/tan oval)?

[22:12:40] director: Yes—basket 8 is the darker gray/black-handled one with pale banding near the bottom.

Basket 9: a tall, straight-sided cylindrical wicker basket (like a small barrel) with TWO small loop handles near the top rim—no big arched handle. Please confirm when you’ve placed basket 9.

[22:12:55] matcher: I found the tall cylindrical/barrel-style basket with two small loop handles near the top and no big arched handle, and I’m placing it in position 9. Quick confirm: the two loop handles sit opposite each other right at the top rim (and it’s a tall cylinder, not a shorter open tub)?

[22:13:08] director: Confirmed for basket 9: yes, it’s the tall cylinder with two small loop handles opposite each other right at the top rim.

Basket 10: a very shallow, wide ROUND basket (like a tray) with a single arched handle; the base has a really obvious spiral/coiled pattern in the weave. Please confirm when you’ve placed basket 10.

[22:13:21] matcher: Placed basket 10 as the very shallow round tray-like basket with a single arched handle and a strong spiral/coiled base pattern. Can you confirm it’s extremely flat like a tray (not a deeper bowl)? If yes, please describe basket 11.

[22:13:32] director: Yes—basket 10 is very flat like a tray. 

Basket 11: a deep round open tub basket with NO arched handle; instead it has two built-in side handles that stick up like little ears. The sides have a diagonal/chevron weave pattern (darker band near the bottom). Confirm when you’ve placed basket 11.

[22:13:46] matcher: Placed basket 11 as the deep round open tub with two small side “ear” handles and the diagonal/chevron weave with a darker band near the bottom. Just to confirm: no arched handle over the top, correct? If yes, please describe basket 12.

[22:14:01] director: Yes—basket 11 has no arched handle over the top.

Basket 12: a warm tan oval/rounded-rectangle basket with a single normal arched handle; the key is the rim is scalloped/wavy with a noticeable dip in the middle (front edge curves down). No lid, no color panels. Confirm when you’ve placed basket 12.

[22:14:16] matcher: I’m placing the warm tan oval basket with the single arched handle and the wavy/scalloped rim that dips down in the middle into position 12. Can you confirm the front rim has that pronounced center dip (not a smooth even rim)?

transcript:
[10:36:57] director: Basket 1 (top-left): an open rectangular basket with a single arched handle; the body is distinctly green/teal, while the rim and handle are orange-brown, with a braided trim/band right along the top edge. Confirm when you’ve got the teal-and-orange rectangular one.

[10:37:12] matcher: Placed the open rectangular basket with the green/teal body and the orange-brown rim/handle with braided trim into position 1. Just confirming: it’s the one with the teal body and the orange-brown braided band right under the rim, correct? If yes, go ahead with basket 2.

[10:37:25] director: Yes, basket 1 is the teal body with the orange-brown braided band at the rim. Basket 2: a small pale tan oval basket with a tall arched handle that has light/white wrap sections near the top; it also has two thin side straps running up toward the handle (so it looks like a double-strap handle setup). Confirm when you’ve placed it.

[10:37:40] matcher: I think I found it and placed the small pale tan oval basket with the tall handle that has light/white wrap near the top, plus the two thin side straps running up toward the handle, into position 2. Can you confirm those extra side straps are clearly visible (not just a plain single-handle basket)?

[10:37:52] director: Yes—on basket 2 the two thin side straps are clearly visible (not just a plain single handle). Now basket 3: the round, lidded wicker basket shaped like a CAT—face with whiskers on the lid and it stands on little feet. Confirm when you’ve placed the cat one (not the duck).

[10:38:06] matcher: Placed the round lidded cat-shaped basket (cat face/whiskers on the lid, little feet) in position 3. Can you confirm it’s the one with the cat face on top (not the duck with a beak/head sticking out)?

[10:38:18] director: Yes—basket 3 is the cat-face lidded one (whiskers on top, little feet), not the duck. Now basket 4: the DUCK-shaped lidded wicker container—oval body with a head/beak sticking out to the RIGHT and a visible eye, plus a strap-like handle running over the top/back. Confirm when you’ve placed the duck.

[10:38:31] matcher: Placed the duck-shaped lidded wicker container (beak/head sticking out to the right with a visible eye and a strap-like handle over the top) in position 4. Just to confirm: it’s the one with the beak clearly protruding to the right, correct? If yes, please describe basket 5.

[10:38:45] director: Yes—basket 4 is the duck with the beak protruding to the right. Basket 5: an open light-brown/tan basket with ONE arched handle, and the rim is clearly asymmetric—one side/back is higher than the front, giving it a slanted “scoop” profile (not evenly level). Confirm when you’ve placed that slanted-rim handled basket.

[10:38:59] matcher: I think I found the open light-tan basket with the clearly slanted/asymmetric rim (higher on one side) and one arched handle, and I placed it in position 5. Quick check: does the handle have any small reddish/brown wrap accents, or is it plain?

[10:39:11] director: Basket 5’s handle DOES have small reddish/brown wrap accents (little dark bands) on it. Main cue is still the clearly slanted/asymmetric rim—one side sits higher like a scoop.

[10:39:24] matcher: Confirmed on basket 5: I have the open light-tan oval with the slanted/scoop rim and the little reddish-brown wrap accents on the handle in position 5. Please describe basket 6— is it the very shallow round tray with a pale handle and a spiral/target weave on the base, or a different one?

[10:39:37] director: Basket 6 is NOT the shallow spiral tray. It’s the tall cylindrical/hamper-style brown basket, open top, with TWO small loop handles right near the rim (no big arch handle), and the upper rim has little vertical slat-like gaps. Confirm when you’ve placed that tall barrel one.

[10:39:51] matcher: Placed the tall cylindrical/hamper-style open basket with two small loop handles near the rim and the little vertical slat-like gaps at the top in position 6. Can you confirm the two loop handles sit opposite each other right at the rim (and it’s a straight cylinder, not the round tub with diagonal/chevron weave)?

[10:40:05] director: Yes—basket 6 is the straight cylinder/hamper with two small loop handles opposite each other right at the rim (not the chevron tub). Now basket 7: a very deep, round bucket-shaped basket with ONE thick arched handle that’s almost black, and a lighter rim band with little metal tack/stud dots all around. Confirm when you’ve placed that studded-rim bucket.

[10:40:18] matcher: I found the very deep round bucket basket with the almost-black arched handle and the lighter studded/tacked rim band, and I’m placing it in position 7. Can you confirm it’s the deep half-sphere bucket and the tack dots go all the way around the rim?

[10:40:30] director: Yes—basket 7 is the deep half-sphere bucket, and the little tack/stud dots run all the way around the lighter rim band. The handle is thick and almost black.

[10:40:43] matcher: Confirmed—basket 7 is set as the deep round bucket with the almost-black handle and the studded rim band. Please describe basket 8 (shape, handle type/number, and any distinctive pattern or lid).

[10:40:57] director: Basket 8: a small, squat OVAL open basket with ONE arched handle; it’s mostly very dark gray/black wicker (handle matches), with only a thin light-tan band right at the very bottom/base. Confirm when you’ve placed the mostly-dark two-tone one.

[10:41:13] matcher: Placed the small squat oval basket that’s mostly very dark gray/black with a matching dark handle and only a thin light-tan band at the very bottom into position 8. To confirm: it’s almost entirely dark with just a narrow pale strip at the base (not a bigger light lower half), right? If yes, please describe basket 9.

[10:41:26] director: Yes—basket 8 is almost entirely dark with just a narrow pale strip at the base. 

Basket 9: a shallow, low-and-wide RECTANGULAR honey-brown basket with an unusually THICK twisted/rope-like arched handle and chunky square/blocky weave on the sides. Confirm when you’ve placed that thick-rope-handle rectangle.

[10:41:41] matcher: Placed the shallow, low-and-wide rectangular honey-brown basket with the very thick twisted/rope-like arched handle and chunky blocky weave into position 9. Can you confirm the handle is the thick twisted-rope style and the shape reads clearly rectangular (not oval)? If yes, please describe basket 10.

[10:41:54] director: Yes—basket 9 is the rectangular one with the very thick twisted-rope handle. Basket 10: a round open tub/bucket basket with TWO short side handles (like little ears), no big arched handle over the top, and a clear diagonal/chevron weave pattern around the sides. Confirm when placed.

[10:42:08] matcher: Placed basket 10 as the round open tub/bucket with two short ear-like side handles and the diagonal/chevron weave (no big arched handle). Can you confirm that’s right, and then describe basket 11?

[10:42:21] director: Yes, basket 10 is correct (round tub with two small side handles and chevron weave). Basket 11: the VERY shallow round tray-basket with one pale arched handle, and a super obvious spiral/target weave pattern across the base. Confirm when you’ve placed the flat spiral tray.

[10:42:35] matcher: Placed the very shallow round tray with the pale arched handle and the obvious spiral/target weave in position 11. Can you confirm it’s the really flat tray where the spiral fills most of the base? If yes, please describe basket 12.

[10:42:47] director: Yes—basket 11 is the really flat round tray with the spiral filling most of the base. Basket 12: the picnic-style RECTANGULAR basket with a hinged LID that’s clearly OPEN/propped up, and a tall vertical center handle (boxy body, no animal shape). Confirm when you’ve placed the open-lid picnic basket.

[10:43:02] matcher: Placed basket 12 as the rectangular picnic basket with the hinged lid propped open and the tall vertical center handle. Just to confirm: the lid is clearly open upward (not the closed trunk-style box), and it’s a single tall center handle, right?

[10:44:12] director: Yes—basket 2 is the shallow rectangle with the very thick twisted/rope-like handle. 

Basket 3: the DUCK-shaped lidded wicker container—rounded body with a small head/beak sticking out to the RIGHT and a visible eye; it has a strap-like handle running over the top/back. Confirm when you’ve placed the duck (not the round cat-face one).

Basket 4: a taller RECTANGULAR open basket with a dark green/teal woven body, but an orange-brown rim and base; there’s a braided/twisted trim band right along the top edge, and one orange-brown arched handle. Please confirm when you’ve placed the teal-and-orange rectangular one.

[10:44:56] matcher: Placed basket 4 as the tall rectangular open basket with the green/teal body and orange-brown rim/base plus the braided trim at the top, with one orange-brown arched handle. [10:45:10] director: Yes—basket 4 is the teal/green rectangular one with the orange-brown braided band at the rim (no lid). Basket 5: a round open tub/bucket basket with TWO short side handles like little ears (no big arched handle over the top) and a clear diagonal/chevron weave around the sides. [10:45:24] matcher: I’ve placed the round open tub/bucket basket with the diagonal/chevron weave and the two short ear-like side handles (no big arched handle) into position 5. Can you confirm that’s the one, and then describe basket 6?

[10:45:39] director: Yes—basket 5 is the round tub with two small side “ear” handles and chevron weave. 

Basket 6: a very deep, round bucket-shaped basket (almost a half-sphere) with ONE thick arched handle that’s very dark (nearly black). The rim has a lighter band with little metal tack/stud dots all the way around. Confirm when you’ve placed the studded-rim deep bucket.

[10:45:54] matcher: I’ve placed the very deep round bucket basket with the nearly-black thick handle and the lighter rim band with metal studs in position 6. Are the studs visible all the way around the rim, and is the handle much darker than the body? If yes, please describe basket 7.

[10:46:08] director: Yes on basket 6: the studs go all the way around the rim and the handle is much darker than the body. 

Basket 7: a tall straight CYLINDER/hamper basket, open top, with TWO small loop handles right near the rim (no big arch handle). The upper rim area has little vertical slat-like gaps/openings. Confirm when you’ve placed that tall barrel one.

[10:46:21] matcher: Placed basket 7 as the tall straight cylindrical/hamper basket with two small loop handles near the rim and the little vertical slat-like gaps at the top. Just to confirm: it’s the tall hamper with the two small loops (not the round tub with ear handles), right? If yes, please describe basket 8.

[10:46:34] director: Yes—basket 7 is the tall hamper with two small loop handles (not the ear-handle tub). Basket 8: the picnic-style RECTANGULAR basket with a hinged LID that’s clearly OPEN/propped up, and a tall vertical center handle (boxy body). Confirm when you’ve placed the open-lid picnic basket.

[10:46:47] matcher: Placed basket 8 as the rectangular picnic-style basket with the hinged lid propped open and the tall vertical center handle. Just to confirm: the lid is clearly open upward (not closed/flat), and the handle is a tall center handle? If yes, please describe basket 9.

[10:47:00] director: Yes—basket 8 is the one with the lid clearly propped open and a tall center handle. Basket 9: the round, squat LIDDED wicker basket shaped like a CAT—cat face with whiskers on the lid and it stands on little feet (no beak/head sticking out like the duck). Confirm when you’ve placed the cat one.

[10:47:12] matcher: I’ve placed the round squat lidded cat basket (cat face/whiskers on the lid, little feet) into position 9. Can you confirm it’s the cat-face one (not the duck with the beak sticking out)? If yes, please describe basket 10.

[10:47:27] director: Confirmed—basket 9 is the cat-face lidded one (not the duck). Basket 10: a small pale/cream OVAL open basket with one tall arched handle that has light/white wrap sections near the top, plus two thin side straps running up from the sides toward the handle (extra strap supports). [10:47:40] matcher: Placed the small pale/cream oval open basket with the tall handle, the light/white wrap near the top, and the two thin side support straps in position 10. Can you confirm the two side straps are clearly visible and the handle has those light wrap sections near the top? If yes, please describe basket 11.

[10:47:54] director: Yes—basket 10 has the two thin side straps and the light/white wrap near the top of the handle. Basket 11: an open light-brown OVAL basket with ONE arched handle, and the rim is distinctive with two little pointed peaks and a V-shaped dip between them (not a smooth oval). [10:48:09] matcher: Placed basket 11 as the open light-brown oval basket with one arched handle, the rim with two little pointed peaks and a V-shaped dip, and the more open lattice weave near the top. [10:48:22] director: Yes—basket 11 is the one with the two peaked points and a V-dip rim. Basket 12: an open light-tan rectangular caddy basket with a CENTER DIVIDER (two compartments). It has one tall upright handle in the middle, plus a second thinner strap/handle that’s folded down diagonally across the top. Confirm when you’ve placed the divided caddy.

[10:48:37] matcher: Placed basket 12 as the open light-tan rectangular caddy with a center divider, tall center handle, and the thin strap folded diagonally across the top. Can you confirm the flat boxy shape with the visible divider and diagonal strap? If yes, I’m ready to submit.
