transcript:
[10:24:02] director: Basket 1: the light tan wicker basket shaped like a duck—rounded body with a small head sticking out on the right, a little blue bead eye, and a flat strap/handle running over the top/back. Let me know when you’ve got the duck one placed.

[10:24:16] matcher: Got it—I placed the duck-shaped wicker basket in position 1. Just to confirm: it’s the duck profile with the head sticking out to the right (not the round animal face with two eyes on the front), correct? Ready for Basket 2.

[10:24:30] director: Yes—basket 1 is the duck profile with the head sticking out to the right (not the round face). 

Basket 2: a deep, rounded bucket-style wicker basket with a very dark (almost black) thick arched handle; the rim has a lighter band/strip just under it. Confirm when you’ve placed that one.

[10:24:44] matcher: Placed the deep rounded bucket basket with the very dark thick arched handle into position 2. Quick confirm: is the body mostly dark brown/gray wicker (not bright honey-brown), with a pale/whitish band near the bottom too, in addition to the lighter strip under the rim?

[10:24:58] director: Yes—basket 2 is the deep round bucket with the darkest/thickest (almost black) handle. The body is medium-to-dark brown (not honey-gold) and it has that lighter strip right under the rim; I don’t really see a strong pale band at the bottom like some other baskets.

[10:25:09] matcher: Confirmed on basket 2—dark thick almost-black handle, medium/dark body, lighter strip just under the rim. What’s Basket 3?

[10:25:21] director: Basket 3: the rectangular picnic basket with a hinged lid that’s propped open (you can see the lid sticking up/back). It has a tall vertical handle rising from the center, and the body is a boxy tan wicker rectangle. Confirm when you’ve got the one with the open lid.

[10:25:34] matcher: I think I found it—the boxy rectangular picnic basket with the hinged lid propped open and a tall center handle. I’m placing that in position 3. Quick confirm: the lid is clearly flipped open (not the closed flat trunk-style basket), right?

[10:25:48] director: Yes—basket 3 is the one with the lid clearly flipped open. 

Basket 4: a small light-tan oval/boat-shaped basket with a slightly pointy left end, and a single arched handle that has little colored wrap bands (reddish/purple) where the handle meets the sides. Confirm when you place that one.

[10:26:02] matcher: Placed basket 4 as the small light-tan oval/boat-shaped basket with the slightly pointy left end and the arched handle that has reddish/purple wrap bands where it meets the sides. To confirm: the colored wraps are right at the handle attachment points on both sides (not a plain handle), correct?

[10:26:16] director: Yes, basket 4 has the little reddish/purple wraps right where the handle attaches on both sides. 

Basket 5: a shallow, low rectangular honey-brown basket with a very thick twisted/rope-like arched handle (looks braided). The sides are open lattice weave. Confirm when you’ve placed the one with the chunky twisted handle.

[10:26:29] matcher: I placed the shallow honey-brown rectangular basket with the chunky twisted/rope-like arched handle into position 5. Can you confirm the handle is that thick twisted rope style and the basket is quite low/shallow (not the taller greenish rectangular one)?

[10:26:42] director: Yes—basket 5 is the low/shallow rectangular one with the thick twisted rope-like handle (not the taller greenish rectangle).

Basket 6: the round, lidded wicker basket that looks like an animal face from the front—two little eyes and a small nose/mouth on the lid, and it sits on tiny feet/pegs. Confirm when you place the round “face” one.

[10:26:56] matcher: Placed basket 6 as the round lidded basket with the front-facing animal face (two eyes and a little nose/mouth) sitting on tiny feet. Just to confirm: it’s the round face one, not the duck-profile with the head sticking out to the right, correct? Ready for basket 7.

[10:27:10] director: Yes, basket 6 is the round front-facing “animal face” one (not the duck profile). 

Basket 7: a tall rectangular basket with a single arched handle; the body is distinctly greenish/teal (vertical slat-like weave) with warm brown trim and a thick braided band along the top rim. Confirm when you’ve placed the green rectangular one.

[10:27:25] matcher: Placed basket 7 as the tall rectangular basket with the greenish/teal body, warm brown trim, and thick braided band along the top rim. Can you confirm it’s the only green/teal rectangular one with that braided top rim band? Ready for basket 8.

[10:27:40] director: Yes—basket 7 is the only green/teal rectangular one with that braided top rim band.

Basket 8: a squat oval basket with a dark gray-brown body and a clear lighter/whitish band of weave around the lower part; it has a single dark arched handle (not the thick twisted-rope handle). Confirm when you’ve placed that two-tone dark basket.

[10:27:54] matcher: For basket 8, I’m torn: do you mean the squat oval with a clear pale/whitish band around the lower third (not the deeper round bucket with the very thick almost-black handle)?

[10:28:07] director: Yes—basket 8 is the squat, low oval one with the clear pale/whitish band around the lower third. It’s NOT basket 2 (which is deeper/taller and has the very thick almost-black handle). Let me know when you’ve placed basket 8.

[10:28:21] matcher: Placed basket 8 as the squat low oval basket with the distinct pale/whitish band around the lower third. To confirm: that light band is clearly on the lower part of the body, and the handle is medium brown (not the very thick almost-black handle), right? Ready for basket 9.

[10:28:35] director: Basket 9: a tall, cylindrical brown wicker basket (like a small hamper/barrel) with an open top and TWO small loop handles near the rim—no big arched handle. Confirm when you’ve placed the tall cylinder with the two little handles.

[10:28:49] matcher: Placed basket 9 as the tall cylindrical hamper/barrel with the open top and two small loop handles near the rim. Just to confirm: it’s the tall straight-sided cylinder with the small loops (not the shorter wide tub with big side handles), right? Ready for basket 10.

[10:29:02] director: Yes, basket 9 is the tall straight-sided cylinder with the two small loop handles (not the wide tub).

Basket 10: a very shallow round tray-basket with a single arched handle; the base has a clear spiral/concentric-circle weave pattern, and the sides are low and open-lattice. Confirm when you’ve placed the shallow round tray with the spiral base.

[10:29:17] matcher: Placed basket 10 as the very shallow round tray with the spiral/concentric base and a single arched handle. Quick confirm: is the handle a light tan/cream color and the whole thing very flat like a serving tray (not the deeper round basket with a taller handle)?

[10:29:32] director: Yes—basket 10 is the very flat, shallow round tray with a light tan handle and a spiral/concentric base.

Basket 11: a wide, deep round tub basket with TWO thick side handles that stick up like little ears (no arched handle over the top). The weave looks diagonal/chevron around the sides. Confirm when you’ve placed the tub with the two ear-handles.

[10:29:46] matcher: Placed basket 11 as the wide deep round tub with the two ear-like side handles and diagonal/chevron weave. Just to confirm: no arched handle over the top, right? Ready for basket 12.

[10:30:02] director: Yes—basket 11 has no arched handle over the top.

Basket 12: a warm light-brown/orange oval basket with a single tall arched handle; the sides look more structured with vertical rib-like slats (not open lattice), and it’s deeper than the flat tray. Confirm when you’ve placed this oval handled one.

[10:30:19] matcher: Placed basket 12 as the warm orange/light-brown oval basket with the tall arched handle and more structured (rib-like) sides. To confirm: it’s the orange-toned oval with a very tall thin handle (not the small light-tan boat-shaped one, and not the two-tone oval with a dark band), right? If yes, I’m ready to submit.

[10:32:39] matcher: I placed the small low honey-brown basket with the very thick twisted/rope-like arched handle in position 4. Can you confirm the handle is the chunky braided rope style and the sides are open lattice (low bread-basket shape)?

[10:32:53] director: Yes—basket 4 is the low honey-brown one with the chunky braided rope handle and open lattice sides.

Basket 5: the squat oval basket that’s two-tone—dark gray/brown wicker with a distinct pale/whitish band wrapping around the lower third, plus a single medium-dark arched handle (not chunky rope, not black). Confirm when you’ve placed that two-tone banded one.

[10:33:07] matcher: I think I’ve found the squat two-tone oval: dark gray/brown body with a clear pale band around the lower third and a plain medium-dark arched handle. I’m placing that in position 5—can you confirm the handle is plain (not twisted) and the light band is near the bottom?

[10:33:21] director: Yes—basket 5 is the squat two-tone oval: dark gray/brown body with a distinct pale/whitish band around the lower third near the bottom, and a plain medium-dark arched handle (not twisted/rope, not the thick black handle).

[10:33:32] matcher: Great—basket 5 is set as the squat two-tone oval with the pale band near the bottom. What’s Basket 6? Please describe its shape and handle (and any distinctive colors/patterns).

[10:33:45] director: Basket 6: the lidded wicker basket shaped like a duck—an oval body with a lid, and a duck head protruding on the RIGHT side with a little blue bead eye. No arched carry-handle; it’s an animal-shaped container. Confirm when you’ve selected the duck.

[10:33:59] matcher: Placed basket 6 as the duck-shaped lidded wicker container with the head protruding on the right and a little blue bead eye. Just to confirm: it’s the duck-profile (not the round lidded basket with a face on the front), correct? Ready for basket 7.

[10:34:13] director: Yes—basket 6 is the duck profile with the head sticking out on the right. 

Basket 7: the round, lidded wicker basket with an animal/cat face on top (two eyes and a little nose/mouth) and it sits on tiny feet/pegs—no arched carry handle. Confirm when you’ve placed the round “face” one.

[10:45:03] matcher: Placed basket 7 as the round lidded basket with the animal/cat face on the lid and tiny feet. Just confirming: it’s the round face one (not the duck profile), right? Ready for basket 8.

[10:45:17] director: Yes, basket 7 is the round lidded face one (not the duck). 

Basket 8: the very shallow ROUND tray-basket with a single arched handle; you can clearly see a spiral/concentric-circle weave pattern on the base, and the sides are very low/almost flat. Confirm when you’ve placed the flat round tray with the spiral base.

[10:45:32] matcher: Placed basket 8 as the very shallow round tray with the spiral/concentric base and a single arched handle. Quick confirm: is the handle a light tan/cream color and the whole thing basically flat like a serving tray (not a deeper round basket)? What’s basket 9?

[10:45:46] director: Yes—basket 8 is basically flat like a serving tray, with a light tan/cream handle and the spiral base.

[10:46:30] matcher: Placed basket 10 as the wide deep round tub with the two ear-like side handles and no arched handle over the top. Just to confirm: it’s the round tub with the diagonal/chevron weave on the sides, right? Ready for basket 11.

[10:46:45] director: Yes, basket 10 is the round tub with the diagonal/chevron weave and two ear-like side handles.

Basket 11: a small light-tan oval/boat-shaped basket with a slightly pointy LEFT end and one arched handle; the key detail is the little reddish/purple wrap bands right where the handle attaches on BOTH sides. Confirm when you’ve placed that one.

[10:47:01] matcher: Placed basket 11 as the small light-tan oval/boat-shaped basket with the slightly pointy left end and the reddish/purple wrap bands where the handle attaches on both sides. Are those colored wraps clearly visible at both attachment points? Ready for basket 12.

[10:47:30] matcher: Placed basket 12 as the warm orange-brown oval basket with the tall arched handle and the more solid rib/slat-like sides. transcript:
[10:49:08] director: Basket 1: the light tan wicker basket shaped like a duck/bird—rounded body with a head/beak sticking out on the right and a little colored eye; there’s a handle running along the top/back. Let me know when you’ve found it.

[10:49:21] matcher: Found it—I’m placing the light tan duck/bird-shaped wicker basket with the beak pointing right into position 1. Just to confirm: it’s the duck/bird (not the round cat-face one), correct? Ready for basket 2.

[10:49:32] director: Yes—basket 1 is the duck/bird, not the round cat-face. 

Basket 2: a deep, rounded bucket-shaped wicker basket with a very dark (almost black) thick arched handle; medium-brown body with tight small weave and a slightly thicker rim band. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[10:49:47] matcher: I think I found basket 2: the deep bucket-shaped brown wicker basket with the very dark/black thick arched handle. I’m placing it in position 2. Just to confirm: the handle is much darker than the body (almost black) and it’s a deep bucket rather than a shallow bowl, right?

[10:49:59] director: Yes, basket 2 is the deep bucket with the handle much darker (almost black) than the body. 

Basket 3: a rectangular/boxy picnic basket with a tall handle in the middle, and a hinged lid that’s flipped open and sticking out to the RIGHT side. Please confirm when you spot it.

[10:50:13] matcher: I’ve spotted the rectangular picnic basket with the tall middle handle and the lid flipped open to the right, and I’m placing it in position 3. Just to confirm: the lid is visibly open and sticking out to the right (not the flat closed-lid trunk style), correct? Ready for basket 4.

[10:50:28] director: Yes—basket 3 is the one with the lid clearly open and sticking out to the right.

Basket 4: a small oval/boat-shaped wicker basket with one arched handle; it has a darker brown band around the top rim, and the handle has light/white wrapping near the top with little reddish ties where it attaches. Confirm when you’ve placed it.

[10:50:41] matcher: I think I found it—the small oval basket with the darker rim band and the handle that has light wrapping plus little reddish ties at the attachments. I’m placing it in position 4. Can you confirm the reddish ties are visible at both handle attachment points?

[10:50:54] director: Yes—on basket 4 the little reddish wraps/ties are visible at both handle attachment points, and the handle also has that pale/white wrapping near the top plus the darker band around the rim.

[10:51:07] matcher: Great—basket 4 is set. Please describe Basket 5 (shape, handle, any lid/pattern/colors) so I can place it in position 5.

[10:51:21] director: Basket 5 is a squat, rectangular open-top basket with a very thick twisted-rope style arched handle. The sides have a chunky square weave; it’s wider than it is tall (not oval/round). Confirm when you’ve got it in position 5.

[10:51:36] matcher: I think I found basket 5: the squat rectangular open-top basket with the very thick twisted/rope-like arched handle and chunky square weave. I’m placing it in position 5. Can you confirm it’s the light honey-brown rectangular one (not the larger rectangular basket with greenish sides and a smoother handle)?

[10:51:48] director: Yes—basket 5 is the light honey-brown squat rectangle with the very thick twisted/rope-like handle and chunky square weave. It is NOT the larger rectangle with greenish sides (that one is basket 7).

[10:52:01] matcher: Confirmed—basket 5 is placed (the light honey-brown squat rectangle with the thick twisted handle). Please describe basket 6 so I can fill position 6.

[10:52:14] director: Basket 6: the round, lidded wicker basket shaped like a cat’s head—cat face on top (nose/whiskers) with two little pointed ears, and it sits on small feet. (Not the duck/bird.) Confirm when placed in position 6.

[10:52:28] matcher: Found basket 6—the round lidded cat-head basket with the face/ears and little feet. I’m placing it in position 6. Just to confirm: it’s the cat-face one (not the duck/bird), right? Ready for basket 7.

[10:52:42] director: Yes—basket 6 is the cat-face (not the duck). 

Basket 7: a large rectangular basket with an arched handle; it’s two-tone with greenish/teal woven sides and a warm orange-brown rim/handle. More boxy and bigger than basket 5. Confirm when you place it in position 7.

[10:52:55] matcher: Got it—I’m placing the large boxy rectangular basket with green/teal sides and an orange-brown rim/arched handle into position 7. Just to confirm: the sides are distinctly greenish/teal and the handle/rim are warm orange-brown, right? Ready for basket 8.

[10:53:11] director: Yes—basket 7 has distinctly greenish/teal sides with an orange-brown rim and handle. 

Basket 8: a small squat oval basket with a dark (gray/black) woven body and a dark arched handle; the sides have noticeable lighter/whitish horizontal bands/striping. Confirm when you’ve placed it in position 8.

[10:53:26] matcher: I found a small squat oval basket with a very dark (charcoal/black) weave and a dark arched handle, plus lighter horizontal banding on the sides. I’m placing that in position 8. To confirm: the body is distinctly dark gray/black with pale bands (not a plain brown bucket), right?

[10:53:38] director: Yes—basket 8 is the dark charcoal/black one with pale horizontal bands (not the plain brown bucket). 

Basket 9: a tall, cylindrical “barrel” wicker basket, darker brown, with TWO small loop handles right near the top rim (no big arching handle). Confirm when you place it in position 9.

[10:53:53] matcher: Found basket 9—the tall cylindrical/barrel wicker basket with two small loop handles near the top and no big arching handle. I’m placing it in position 9. Just to confirm: it’s open-top with those two small side loops, no lid, right? Ready for basket 10.

[10:54:05] director: Yes—basket 9 is open-top with two small loop handles and no lid.

Basket 10: a very shallow, wide ROUND tray-like wicker basket with one arched handle; the base has a clear spiral/coiled “target” pattern, and the sides are low. Confirm when you’ve placed it in position 10.

[10:54:21] matcher: I think I found it—the very shallow round tray basket with a single arched handle and a clear spiral/target coil on the base. I’m placing it in position 10. To confirm: it’s more like a flat platter with low sides (not a deeper round basket), right?

[10:54:32] director: Yes—basket 10 is the flat, shallow round tray/platter with low sides and a very obvious spiral/bullseye coil pattern on the base (with one arched handle).

[10:54:45] matcher: Confirmed basket 10 is set. Please describe Basket 11 (shape, handle, any pattern/lid/colors) so I can fill position 11.

[10:54:58] director: Basket 11: a deep round tub/bucket basket with NO big arched handle—instead it has two built-in side handles that stick up like little ears. The weave on the sides looks diagonal/chevron. Confirm when you’ve placed it in position 11.

[10:55:14] matcher: I found a deep round tub basket with no arched handle—just two upright side handles like ears and a diagonal/chevron weave. I’m placing it in position 11. Is that the one (not the lighter tan round basket with a single arched handle)?

[10:55:26] director: Yes—that’s basket 11: the deep round tub with two little upright side handles (ear-like) and a diagonal/chevron weave, NOT the lighter tan round one with a single arched handle.

[10:55:41] matcher: Confirmed—basket 11 is placed. Please describe basket 12 (shape, handle(s), lid/no lid, and any pattern/colors) so I can fill position 12.

[10:55:56] director: Basket 12: an open-top, medium-light brown wicker basket with ONE arched handle; the body is a rounded rectangle with a slightly pinched/waisted opening, and the sides look more like vertical slats/structured weave (not tight). The rim has a subtle wavy dip at the front. Confirm when you’ve placed it in position 12.

[10:56:13] matcher: I think I’ve got basket 12: the open-top medium-light brown basket with one arched handle and a slightly waisted/pinched opening. I’m placing it in position 12. Can you confirm it’s the plain light-brown one (not the two-tone banded handled basket and not the shallow tray)?

[10:50:48] matcher: Placed the side-view duck/bird-shaped basket with the pointed beak and loop handle into position 1. Just to confirm: it’s the beak pointing to the right and not the front-facing animal-face basket, correct? Ready for basket 2.

[10:51:00] director: Yes—basket 1 is the side-view bird/duck with the beak pointing right (not the front-facing animal face). 

Basket 2: a round, bucket-shaped wicker basket with a wide open top and a very dark (almost black) thick arched handle.

[10:51:12] matcher: I think I found basket 2: the round bucket basket with the very dark thick arched handle. I’m placing it in position 2. Quick confirm: is the body a warm orange-brown tight weave (not the darker gray/black woven body variant)?

[10:51:24] director: Correct: basket 2 has the warm orange-brown, tight small weave body with that very dark thick arched handle (not the darker gray/black-bodied one).

[10:51:36] matcher: Confirmed basket 2. Please describe basket 3 (shape, handle type, and any distinctive colors/patterns).

[10:51:49] director: Basket 3 is a boxy rectangular picnic basket with a single tall handle in the center, and a hinged lid that’s flipped open/up (you can see the open top under the lid). Mostly light tan wicker with vertical reed sides. Confirm when placed.

[10:52:03] matcher: Got it—I’m placing the boxy rectangular picnic basket with the tall center handle and the lid flipped open into position 3. Quick confirm: the lid is propped open diagonally (not the fully closed rectangular lidded box with no handle), right?

[10:52:17] director: Yes—basket 3’s lid is propped open diagonally. 

Basket 4: a small oval/boat-shaped wicker basket (slightly pointed at one end) with a single arched handle; light tan/cream weave with a darker brown rim, and the handle has wrapped sections with little reddish-brown accents near where it meets the sides. Please confirm when you place it.

[10:52:32] matcher: I think I found basket 4—the small light tan/cream oval “boat” basket with the darker rim and the handle that has little reddish-brown wrapped accents near where it meets the sides. I’m placing it in position 4. To confirm: it’s not the two-tone banded basket or the very flat round tray-style one, right?

[10:52:46] director: Yes, basket 4 is the small cream/tan oval “boat” with the darker rim—NOT the banded one or the flat tray.

Basket 5: a squat, low rectangular basket with a very thick twisted/rope-like arched handle. Medium warm-brown wicker with a blocky/checkered weave pattern on the sides. Confirm when placed.

[10:53:01] matcher: I’m placing the squat low rectangular basket with the very thick twisted/rope-like arched handle and the blocky/checkered weave into position 5. Can you confirm the handle is the chunky rope-twist style (not a smooth wooden arch) and the basket is quite low in height?

[10:53:12] director: Confirmed—basket 5 is the low, squat rectangular one with the chunky rope-twist (braided/spiraled) handle, not a smooth wooden arch.

[10:53:24] matcher: Great—positions 1–5 are set. Please describe basket 6: shape, handle type, and any distinctive colors/patterns (and whether it has a lid).

[10:53:38] director: Basket 6 is the front-facing novelty animal basket: a round lidded container with a cat-like face (whiskers, little nose/mouth) and two small ears on top. No handle, and it sits on tiny feet/pegs—distinct from the side-view duck in basket 1.

[10:53:52] matcher: Placing the front-facing cat-face novelty basket (whiskers, two ears, tiny feet, no handle) into position 6. Just to confirm: it’s the cat face (not the side-profile duck/bird with the loop handle), right? Ready for basket 7.

[10:54:05] director: Yes, basket 6 is the front-facing cat face. 

Basket 7: a rectangular handled basket with a distinctive green/teal body (vertical slats) and a natural tan/brown rim and handle; the top edge has a scalloped/wavy trim. Please confirm when you place it.

[10:54:18] matcher: I found the rectangular basket with the green/teal vertical-slat body and the tan rim/handle with the scalloped top edge, and I’m placing it in position 7. Just to confirm: it’s the only one with that green/teal body and wavy trim, right?

[10:54:31] director: Yes—basket 7 is the only one with that green/teal body and scalloped trim.

Basket 8: a compact oval bucket basket in very dark gray/black wicker, with a single dark arched handle; it has a lighter whitish/cream band around the lower part near the base.

[10:54:49] matcher: I think I found basket 8: the compact very dark gray/black oval bucket basket with a single dark arched handle and a pale/cream band near the bottom. I’m placing it in position 8. Can you confirm it’s the dark one with that light band (not the warm orange-brown bucket with dark handle)?

[10:55:01] director: Confirmed—basket 8 is the very dark gray/black handled basket with the distinct pale/cream band near the bottom (not the warm orange-brown bucket like basket 2).

[10:55:15] matcher: Confirmed basket 8 in position 8. Please describe basket 9—shape, handle type (none/single/double), and any distinctive colors/patterns or whether it has a lid.

[10:55:28] director: Basket 9: a tall, straight-sided cylindrical wicker basket (hamper-like), medium brown, open top, with TWO small loop handles on opposite sides near the rim—no big arched handle over the top. Confirm when placed.

[10:55:41] matcher: Placing the tall straight-sided cylindrical hamper basket with two small side loop handles into position 9. Quick confirm: the two handles are small loops near the rim (no big top handle), and it’s more of a tall cylinder than a short tub, right? Ready for basket 10.

[10:55:55] director: Yes—basket 9 is a tall cylinder with two small side loop handles near the rim, no big top handle.

Basket 10: a very shallow ROUND tray/platter basket with a single arched handle; the base has a clear spiral/coil pattern, and the sides are low (much flatter than the other handled baskets). Confirm when placed.

[10:56:09] matcher: I’m placing the very shallow round tray/platter basket with the arched handle and clear spiral base into position 10. Can you confirm it’s the really flat platter-like one (not a deeper round handled basket)?

[10:56:21] director: Yes—basket 10 is the really flat, shallow round platter/tray with the single arched handle and the obvious spiral/coil pattern in the base (much flatter than the deeper handled baskets).

[10:56:34] matcher: Confirmed basket 10 in position 10. Please describe basket 11 (shape, handle type, and any distinctive colors/patterns or lid).

[10:56:47] director: Basket 11: a deep round tub-shaped wicker basket with TWO short side handles that rise up like little ears; the weave has a noticeable diagonal/chevron pattern. Open top, no big arched handle over the top. Please confirm when placed.

[10:57:01] matcher: Placed basket 11 as the deep round tub with the two short upright side handles (like little ears) and the diagonal/chevron weave into position 11. Just to confirm: it’s the chevron-pattern tub, not the tall cylindrical hamper-style one, right? Ready for basket 12.

[10:57:16] director: Yes—basket 11 is the chevron-pattern tub, not the tall cylinder. 

Basket 12: a light tan oval market basket with ONE smooth arched handle; the sides are fairly open/see-through lattice weave (airier than the tight bucket baskets), and there’s no dark handle or rope-twist. Confirm when you place it.

[10:57:30] matcher: I’m placing the light tan oval market basket with the smooth arched handle and airy lattice sides into position 12. Quick confirm: is it the low, wide oval that has a slightly darker band around the middle (two-tone), rather than a uniform light-tan lattice all around?

transcript:
[10:57:45] director: Basket 1 (top-left): a low rectangular picnic-style wicker basket with ONE tall center handle, and a hinged lid/flap that’s visibly partly open/raised (not a simple open-top basket). Please confirm when you’ve found it.

[10:57:58] matcher: I found the low rectangular picnic basket with the tall center handle and the lid propped open, and I’m placing it in position 1. Just to confirm: the lid is visibly flipped open (not the closed rectangular box with a flat top), right? Ready for basket 2.

[10:58:11] director: Yes—basket 1’s lid is clearly flipped open. 

Basket 2: a tall, straight-sided cylindrical hamper basket (medium brown) with an open top and TWO small loop handles on opposite sides near the rim—no big arched handle over the top. Confirm when placed.

[10:58:25] matcher: Placed the tall straight-sided cylindrical hamper basket with two small loop handles near the rim into position 2. Just to confirm: it’s the tall cylinder with the two little side loops and no big top handle, correct? Ready for basket 3.

[10:58:38] director: Confirmed basket 2. 

Basket 3: a deep round bucket-shaped basket with a warm orange-brown tight weave and a very dark (almost black) thick arched handle; there’s a noticeable rim band around the top. Please confirm when you place it.

[10:58:51] matcher: I’m placing the deep warm orange-brown bucket basket with the very dark thick arched handle into position 3. Can you confirm the handle is almost black and quite thick, and the body is a uniform warm orange-brown tight weave (not two-tone/grayish)?

[10:59:04] director: Yes—basket 3 has an almost black, thick arched handle, and the body is a uniform warm orange-brown tight weave (not gray/two-tone). It’s a deep bucket shape with a darker rim band at the top.

[10:59:14] matcher: Confirmed basket 3 in position 3. Please describe basket 4 (shape, handle type/number, any distinctive colors/patterns, and whether it has a lid).

[10:59:27] director: Basket 4: a low, squat oval/rectangular basket in warm honey-brown wicker with a VERY thick twisted/rope-like (braided) single handle; the sides look chunky and blocky/checkered rather than airy. No lid. Please confirm when placed.

[10:59:43] matcher: I found the low squat basket with the very thick braided/rope-twist single handle and the chunky checkered sides, and I’m placing it in position 4. Quick confirm: the handle is that chunky rope-twist (not a smooth wooden arch), right? If yes, please describe basket 5.

[10:59:56] director: Yes—basket 4’s handle is the chunky rope-twist/braided one. 

Basket 5: a compact dark gray/black oval bucket basket with ONE dark arched handle, and a distinct pale cream/whitish band around the lower part near the base (two-tone). Please confirm when placed.

[11:00:11] matcher: Placing the compact very dark gray/black oval bucket basket with the single dark arched handle and the pale cream band near the base into position 5. Can you confirm the light band is around the lower third near the base (not just a lighter rim at the top)?

[11:00:23] director: Yes—basket 5 is the very dark gray/black handled basket where the pale cream band wraps around the LOWER third near the base (not just a lighter rim at the top).

[11:00:35] matcher: Confirmed basket 5 in position 5. Please describe basket 6 (shape, handle type/number, distinctive colors/patterns, and whether it has a lid).

[11:00:51] director: Basket 6: the novelty animal basket shaped like a duck/bird in SIDE view—rounded lidded body with a pointed beak sticking out to the right, a small colored eye, and a built-in loop handle over the back/top. Please confirm when you’ve placed it.

[11:01:06] matcher: Placed the side-view duck/bird novelty basket (beak pointing right, small eye, loop handle over the back) into position 6. Just to confirm it’s the side-profile bird/duck, not the front-facing cat-face one, correct? Ready for basket 7.

[11:01:19] director: Yes—basket 6 is the side-profile duck/bird, not the cat.

Basket 7: the front-facing novelty CAT-face lidded basket—round, with whiskers and two little ears on top, and it sits on tiny feet/pegs. No handle. Please confirm when placed.

[11:01:34] matcher: Placing the front-facing cat-face lidded basket (whiskers, two ears, tiny feet, no handle) into position 7. Confirm it’s the cat head (not the side-view duck with the loop handle), right? Ready for basket 8.

[11:01:48] director: Yes—basket 7 is the front-facing cat head (not the side-view duck). 

Basket 8: a VERY shallow round tray/platter basket with a single arched handle; the base has an obvious spiral/coil pattern and the sides are low/flat compared to all the other handled baskets. Confirm when placed.

[11:02:02] matcher: Placed the very shallow round tray/platter with the single arched handle and the obvious spiral/coil base into position 8. Can you confirm it’s the flattest round one (not a deeper round handled basket)? If yes, please describe basket 9.

[11:02:15] director: Yes—basket 8 is the flattest round tray with the spiral base. 

Basket 9: a rectangular basket with a single arched handle, but the body is distinctly green/teal (vertical slat look) with a natural tan rim/handle and a scalloped/wavy top edge. Please confirm when placed.

[11:02:30] matcher: Placed the rectangular basket with the green/teal vertical-slat body, tan rim/handle, and scalloped top edge into position 9. Just confirming: it’s the only one with that green/teal body and wavy trim, right? Ready for basket 10.

[11:02:44] director: Yes—basket 9 is the only green/teal one with the wavy trim.

Basket 10: a small light tan/cream OVAL “boat” basket with one arched handle; it has a darker brown rim and little reddish-brown wrapped accents where the handle attaches (handle is not the super-thick rope-twist). Confirm when placed.

[11:03:00] matcher: I think I found the small light tan/cream oval “boat” basket with the darker rim and the reddish wrapped accents at the handle attachments, and I’m placing it in position 10. Does it have those reddish wrap bands on both sides where the handle meets the rim, and is it more pointed/boat-shaped than round?

[11:03:13] director: Yes—basket 10 is the more boat-shaped oval (a bit pointed at the ends), with a darker brown rim, and it has the reddish-brown wrap bands on BOTH sides where the handle meets the rim.

[11:03:26] matcher: Great—basket 10 is set in position 10. Please describe basket 11 (shape, handle type/number, and any distinctive pattern/colors).

[11:03:41] director: Basket 11: an oval, light tan market basket with ONE arched handle, and the key detail is the handle has decorative colored wrap/stripes (reddish/purple accents) near where it meets the sides. No lid. Please confirm when placed.

[11:03:58] matcher: I found an oval light-tan market basket with a single arched handle that has colored wrap bands near where the handle meets the sides, and I’m placing it in position 11. Are the colored wraps clearly visible as multiple little bands near both attachment points (and the basket is more open/airy than the cream boat basket)?

[11:04:11] director: Yes—basket 11 has clearly visible multiple colored wrap bands (reddish/purple) on the handle near BOTH attachment points, and the body weave is more open/airy than basket 10’s cream boat basket.

[11:04:25] matcher: Confirmed basket 11 in position 11. Please describe basket 12 (shape, handle type/number, and any distinctive pattern/colors) so I can place the last one.

[11:04:40] director: Basket 12: a plain light-brown/tan rectangular basket with rounded corners and ONE smooth arched handle; the sides are fairly open/see-through with vertical slats. No lid, no green/teal panels, and no colored wrap accents on the handle. Confirm when placed.

[11:04:58] matcher: I’m a bit confused because my current Round 2 board shows position 10 already filled and position 12 still empty. For your Basket 12 description (plain light-tan rectangular with open vertical slats and a smooth handle, no colored wraps), I think it matches the plain brown handled basket (not the one with colored wrap bands). Can you confirm it’s a soft-rectangular shape (not oval) and the handle is completely plain with no wrap bands? If so, I’ll place it in the last empty slot.

[11:05:12] director: Yes—basket 12 is a soft-RECTANGULAR (rounded-rectangle) open-weave basket, not oval, and the single arched handle is totally plain with NO colored wrap bands/accents. Go ahead and place that plain brown handled one in the last slot.

[11:05:28] matcher: Got it—I’m placing the plain light-tan rounded-rectangle open-weave basket with the totally plain arched handle (no colored wraps) into the last empty slot (position 10). Please confirm it’s the soft-rectangular one, not an oval/boat shape; if confirmed, we’re ready to submit.

transcript:
[11:05:44] director: Basket 1 (top-left): a rectangular, boxy basket with ONE arched handle; the body is distinctly green/teal (vertical slat look) with a tan/brown rim and handle, and the top edge has a scalloped/wavy trim. Please confirm when you’ve found it.

[11:05:59] matcher: Found the rectangular basket with the green/teal vertical-slat body and scalloped/wavy top edge, and I’m placing it in position 1. Just confirming: it’s the only one with that green/teal body and wavy trim, right? Ready for basket 2.

[11:06:13] director: Yes—basket 1 is the only green/teal one with the wavy trim. 

Basket 2: a small light cream/tan oval “boat” basket with a darker brown rim and ONE arched handle; key detail is the reddish-brown wrap accents on the handle right where it attaches on both sides. Confirm when placed.

[11:06:27] matcher: I think I’ve got basket 2: the small cream/tan oval “boat” with the darker rim and the reddish wrap accents where the handle attaches on both sides. I’m placing it in position 2. Can you confirm the reddish wrap bands are visible at both handle attachment points (and it’s not the larger two-tone banded oval)?

[11:06:42] director: Confirmed for basket 2: yes, the reddish wrap bands show at BOTH handle attachment points, and it’s the smaller cream “boat” (not the larger two-tone oval).

Basket 3: the front-facing novelty CAT-face lidded basket—round, with whiskers and two little ears on top, no handle, and it stands on tiny feet/pegs. Please confirm when placed.

[11:06:56] matcher: Placed the front-facing cat-face lidded basket (whiskers, two ears, tiny feet, no handle) into position 3. Just to confirm: it’s the cat head (not the side-profile duck/bird with a loop handle), right? Ready for basket 4.

[11:07:10] director: Yes—basket 3 is the front-facing cat head. 

Basket 4: the novelty bird/duck basket in SIDE view—rounded lidded body with a pointed beak sticking out to the right, a small eye on the side, and a built-in loop handle over the back/top. Confirm when you place it.

[11:07:22] matcher: Placed the side-view duck/bird novelty basket (beak pointing right, loop handle over the back) into position 4. Just to confirm: it’s the side-profile duck/bird, not the front-facing cat-face container, correct? Ready for basket 5.

[11:07:38] director: Yes, basket 4 is the side-profile duck/bird. 

Basket 5: a plain medium warm-brown basket that’s a squat rounded-rectangle (not oval), open top, with ONE simple smooth arched handle—no colored wrap accents, no rope-twist handle, and no lid. Please confirm when placed.

[11:07:52] matcher: I think I’ve found the plain squat rounded-rectangle basket with a simple smooth arched handle, and I’m placing it in position 5. Quick check: the handle is smooth (not a thick rope-twist) and the basket reads more rectangular than oval from the front, correct?

[11:08:06] director: Yes—basket 5 should look more rounded-RECTANGULAR with a smooth plain handle (not rope-twist). 

Basket 6: a tall straight-sided CYLINDRICAL hamper basket, open top, medium brown, with TWO small loop handles on opposite sides near the rim (no big arched handle over the top). Confirm when placed.

[11:08:21] matcher: Found the tall straight-sided cylindrical hamper with two small loop handles near the rim (no big top handle) and placed it in position 6. Can you confirm the two handles are small loops right at the rim and it’s clearly a tall cylinder (not a shorter tub with ear-like handles)? Ready for basket 7.

[11:08:35] director: Confirmed on basket 6: yes, it’s the tall cylinder with two small loop handles right at the rim (not the shorter tub with ear-like handles).

Basket 7: a deep round bucket-shaped basket with a warm orange-brown tight weave and a VERY dark (almost black) thick arched handle over the top. Please confirm when you place it.

[11:08:51] matcher: Placed the deep warm orange-brown bucket basket with the very dark thick arched handle into position 7. Can you confirm the handle is near-black and quite thick, and the body is a uniform warm orange-brown tight weave (not the dark gray/black one with a pale band)?

[11:09:02] director: Yes—basket 7 is the deep round bucket with a uniform warm orange-brown tight weave and a very dark/near-black thick arched handle. It is NOT the dark gray/black one with the pale band near the base.

[11:09:17] matcher: Confirmed basket 7 in position 7. Please describe basket 8 (shape, handle type/number, and any distinctive colors/patterns or whether it has a lid) so I can place it in the next empty slot.

[11:09:31] director: Basket 8: the compact very dark gray/black oval bucket basket with ONE dark arched handle, and a distinct pale cream/whitish band wrapping around the LOWER part near the base (two-tone). No lid. Please confirm when placed.

[11:09:47] matcher: Placed the compact very dark gray/black oval bucket basket with the single dark arched handle and the pale cream band near the base into position 8. Just to confirm: the body is dark gray/black (not warm orange-brown) and the pale band wraps the lower third near the base, right? Ready for basket 9.

[11:10:00] director: Yes—basket 8 is the dark gray/black one with the pale band around the LOWER third. 

Basket 9: the low squat RECTANGULAR basket with the very thick twisted/rope-like single handle (chunky braided look) and a blocky/checkered weave on the sides. Confirm when placed.

[11:10:17] matcher: Placed the low squat rectangular basket with the very thick rope-twist/braided single handle and the blocky/checkered sides into position 9. Just to confirm: the handle is the chunky spiraled rope-twist (not smooth), and the basket is clearly low and rectangular rather than round?

[11:10:30] director: Yes—basket 9 is the low, squat rectangular one with the chunky spiraled rope-twist/braided handle (not smooth), and the sides have that blocky/checkered weave; it’s definitely not a round bucket.

[11:10:41] matcher: Great—basket 9 is set in position 9. Please describe basket 10 (shape, handle type/number, and any distinctive pattern/colors or lid) so I can place it in the next empty slot.

[11:10:55] director: Basket 10: a short, wide round tub-shaped basket (open top) with TWO small upright side handles like little ears; the weave shows a clear diagonal/chevron pattern. No big arched handle over the top. Confirm when placed.

[11:11:10] matcher: Placed the short wide round tub basket with the diagonal/chevron weave and two small upright side handles into position 10. Can you confirm it’s the chevron-pattern tub (not the tall cylindrical hamper with loop handles)? If yes, please describe basket 11.

[11:11:24] director: Yes—basket 10 is the chevron-pattern tub (not the tall cylinder). 

Basket 11: the VERY shallow round tray/platter basket with a single arched handle; the base has a clear spiral/coil pattern and the sides are low/flat. Please confirm when placed.

[11:11:37] matcher: Placed the very shallow round tray/platter with the single arched handle and obvious spiral/coil base into position 11. Just to confirm: it’s the flattest round platter-like one with the spiral base (not a deeper round handled basket), right? Ready for basket 12.

[11:11:52] director: Yes—basket 11 is the flattest round tray with the spiral base.

Basket 12: a small rectangular wicker basket with ONE arched handle that’s offset, plus a distinctive diagonal support/strap running from the handle down to the opposite rim (asymmetrical look). No lid. Please confirm when placed.

[11:12:09] matcher: Placed the small rectangular basket with the offset arched handle and the diagonal support/strap down to the opposite rim into position 12. Just confirming: it’s the asymmetrical one with that diagonal brace, right? If yes, we’re ready to submit.

transcript:
[11:12:24] director: Basket 1 (top-left): a very shallow round/oval tray basket with a single pale/cream arched handle; the base shows a clear tight spiral/coil pattern, and the sides are low like a serving tray (not a deep bucket). Confirm when you’ve got it.

[11:12:38] matcher: Placed the very shallow round tray/platter basket with the pale handle and tight spiral/coil base into position 1. Just to confirm: it’s the very flat round tray with the spiral base and one handle (not a deeper oval market basket), right? Ready for basket 2.

[11:12:53] director: Yes, basket 1 is the very flat spiral-base tray with one pale handle.

Basket 2: a small BOXy rectangular wicker basket with straight sides and a very regular square/grid weave pattern; one arched handle that looks like a braided/twisted rope (medium thickness, not the super-chunky rope handle). Please confirm when placed.

[11:13:10] matcher: I think I found it and placed the small boxy rectangular basket with the very regular square/grid weave and a medium braided/twisted arched handle in position 2. Can you confirm it’s the squat tight grid-weave one (not the green/teal slatted rectangle and not the taller open-lattice rectangle)?

[11:13:23] director: Yes—basket 2 is the squat tight grid-weave rectangle (not the green/teal one and not the open-lattice one).

Basket 3: the novelty bird/duck basket in SIDE view—rounded lidded body with a pointed beak sticking out to the right, a small eye, and a built-in loop handle over the back/top. Confirm when placed.

[11:13:39] matcher: Placed the side-view duck/bird novelty basket (beak pointing right, small eye, loop handle over the back/top) into position 3. Just confirming: it’s the side-profile bird/duck, not the front-facing cat-face container, correct? Ready for basket 4.

[11:13:52] director: Yes, basket 3 is the side-profile duck/bird (not the cat face).

Basket 4: the rectangular handled basket with a DISTINCT green/teal body (vertical slat look), natural tan rim/trim, and a scalloped/wavy top edge under the handle. Please confirm when you place it.

[11:14:05] matcher: Placing the rectangular basket with the green/teal vertical-slat body and the scalloped/wavy top edge into position 4. Can you confirm it has the tan rim/handle and no lid? Ready for basket 5.

[11:14:19] director: Confirmed on basket 4: yes, it has the tan rim/handle and no lid.

Basket 5: a deep round tub-shaped basket with TWO short upright side handles (like little ears) and a clear diagonal/chevron weave pattern. No big arched handle over the top. Please confirm when placed.

[11:14:34] matcher: I found the deep round tub basket with the two short upright “ear” side handles and the diagonal/chevron weave, and I’m placing it in position 5. To confirm: the handles are short and upright (not a big top handle), and the weave is clearly chevron/diagonal, right? Ready for basket 6.

[11:14:47] director: Yes—basket 5 is the chevron/diagonal tub with two short upright side handles (no big top handle).

Basket 6: a deep round bucket basket with a warm orange-brown tight weave and a VERY dark (almost black) thick arched handle over the top; there’s a darker rim band at the top. Confirm when placed.

[11:15:02] matcher: Placed the deep warm orange-brown bucket basket with the very dark thick arched handle and darker rim band into position 6. Can you confirm it’s the uniform warm orange-brown one (not the dark gray/black with a pale band, and not a lighter tan basket)?

[11:15:15] director: Yes—basket 6 is the uniform warm orange-brown bucket with the near-black thick arched handle (not the dark gray one with a pale band).

Basket 7: a tall straight-sided CYLINDRICAL hamper basket, medium brown, open top, with TWO small loop handles on opposite sides right near the rim (no big arched handle over the top). Confirm when placed.

[11:15:29] matcher: Placed the tall straight-sided cylindrical hamper basket with two small loop handles near the rim into position 7. Can you confirm it’s the tallest cylinder (not the shorter tub with ear-like handles)? Ready for basket 8.

[11:15:41] director: Confirmed—basket 7 is the tallest cylinder with two small loop handles near the rim.

Basket 8: a boxy rectangular picnic basket with ONE tall center handle, and a hinged lid/flap that’s clearly propped open/raised (you can see the opening). Please confirm when placed.

[11:15:56] matcher: Placed the boxy rectangular picnic basket with the tall center handle and the lid propped open into position 8. Just to confirm: it’s the one with the lid clearly open/raised (not a closed box and not an open-top rectangle), with the handle centered over the opening, right? Ready for basket 9.

[11:16:06] director: Yes—basket 8 is the rectangular picnic basket with the lid clearly propped open and the handle centered.

Basket 9: the front-facing novelty CAT-face lidded basket—round, with whiskers and two little ears on top, no handle, and it stands on tiny feet/pegs. Please confirm when placed.

[11:16:21] matcher: Placed the front-facing cat-face lidded basket (whiskers, two ears, tiny feet, no handle) into position 9. Just confirming: it’s the cat head (not the side-view duck/bird with the beak pointing right), correct? Ready for basket 10.

[11:16:34] director: Yes—basket 9 is the cat head. 

Basket 10: a small light cream/tan OVAL “boat” basket with ONE arched handle; it has a darker brown rim and distinctive reddish-brown wrap bands on the handle at BOTH attachment points. Confirm when placed.

[11:16:47] matcher: Placed the small cream/tan oval “boat” basket with the darker brown rim and the reddish-brown wrap bands at both handle attachment points into position 10. Are the reddish wrap bands clearly visible on both sides where the handle meets the rim (and it’s the small boat-shaped oval, not a rounder deeper basket)?

[11:17:00] director: Yes—basket 10 has the reddish wrap bands on BOTH handle attachment points and is the small boat-shaped oval.

Basket 11: the compact very dark gray/black oval bucket basket with ONE dark arched handle, and a distinct pale cream/whitish band wrapping around the LOWER third near the base (two-tone). Please confirm when placed.

[11:17:18] matcher: Placed the compact very dark gray/black oval bucket basket with the single dark arched handle and the pale cream band around the lower third into position 11. Can you confirm it’s the dark one with the light band near the bottom (not the warm orange-brown bucket or the lighter brown bucket)? Ready for basket 12.

[11:17:33] director: Confirmed—basket 11 is the dark gray/black oval with the pale band near the bottom.

Basket 12: the DARK rectangular handled basket—low and wide, with a thick dark arched handle and a very dark brown/black woven body, plus a lighter tan strip/band along the bottom edge (two-tone). Please confirm when placed.

[11:17:47] matcher: Placed the dark low-wide rectangular basket with the thick dark arched handle and the lighter tan band along the bottom into position 12. Just to confirm: it’s the dark rectangular one with the pale strip at the very bottom (not the dark oval bucket with the pale band), right? If yes, I’m ready to submit.

transcript:
[10:50:22] director: Basket 1: a novelty wicker basket shaped like a duck/bird in side view—low oval body with a little head on the RIGHT (eye + small beak). There’s a small handle opening on top near the back. Let me know when you’ve got that one placed.

[10:50:36] matcher: Placed Basket 1 in position 1 as the side-view duck/bird wicker basket with the head on the right and a small top opening. Just to confirm: it’s the side-view bird/duck (not the round front-facing animal), right? Ready for Basket 2.

[10:50:49] director: Yes—basket 1 is the side-view duck/bird (not the round front-facing animal). 

Basket 2: a squat, very round brown wicker basket with a thick, dark (almost black) arched handle; looks like a little cauldron with a narrow rim band. No lid. Confirm when you’ve selected it.

[10:51:04] matcher: I’ve placed Basket 2 as the very round, cauldron-like brown basket with the thick dark/black arched handle. Can you confirm the handle is much darker than the body (almost black), and it’s more spherical than a wide open bowl? Ready for Basket 3.

[10:51:21] director: Confirmed on basket 2: the handle is much darker (nearly black) and the basket is very round/spherical.

Basket 3: a rectangular picnic-style wicker basket with a hinged lid that’s flipped open to the RIGHT, and a tall vertical handle/strap rising from the center. More boxy than the other baskets. Let me know when you’ve got it.

[10:51:33] matcher: I think I found it—the boxy picnic basket with the lid flipped open to the right and a tall center handle/strap. I’m placing that in position 3. Just to confirm: it’s the one with a single flap lid opened to the right (not the flat rectangular tray/hamper)?

[10:51:44] director: Yes—basket 3 is the boxy picnic basket with a single hinged lid flipped open to the RIGHT, plus that tall central strap/handle rising up. It’s not an open-top tray/hamper.

[10:51:55] matcher: Got it—basket 3 is confirmed. Please describe Basket 4 (shape, handle(s), color/pattern, and whether it has a lid).

[10:52:09] director: Basket 4: a light tan/cream oval basket with a single arched handle; the weave has pale/whitish bands, and there are small reddish wrap accents where the handle meets the rim. It’s kind of boat-shaped/asymmetrical (one side higher), and it has NO lid. Confirm when you’ve got it.

[10:52:24] matcher: I’ve placed Basket 4 as the light cream oval/boat-shaped basket with the single arched handle and the little reddish wraps where the handle meets the rim. Quick confirm: the reddish wraps are just at the two handle attachment points, and the basket is mostly light/cream with pale bands (not a two-tone dark/light body), right? Ready for Basket 5.

[10:52:38] director: Confirmed on basket 4: the reddish wraps are only at the two handle attachment points, and the whole basket is mostly light/cream with pale bands (not a dark two-tone).

Basket 5: a small, straight-sided rectangular brown basket with a very thick braided/rope-like arched handle. The body has a chunky square-weave look and it’s open-top (no lid). Let me know when you’ve selected it.

[11:40:04] matcher: Great—position 6 is set. Can you describe basket 7 (shape, handle type/number, and any distinctive color/pattern or lid) so I can place it in the next empty slot?

[11:41:00] matcher: Placed the rectangular tan picnic/hamper with the flat CLOSED lid and a single arched handle into position 8. Just to confirm: it’s the lidded box with the lid fully shut (not the one with the lid flipped open), right? Ready for basket 9.

[11:41:28] matcher: Placed the front-facing cat head basket (painted eyes/whiskers, little ears, on small feet) into position 9. Can you confirm it’s the cat with feet (not the side-profile bird/duck)? Ready for basket 10.

[11:41:55] matcher: Placed the small oval/boat-shaped basket with pale whitish sides, tan rim/base, and the little reddish wraps at both handle attachment points into position 10. Can you confirm the reddish wraps are visible on both sides where the handle meets the rim?

[11:42:39] director: Yes—basket 11’s handle is a plain smooth arc (not twisted) and the tote is more squared-off than round.

Basket 12: a short, round open tub/bucket basket with TWO small loop handles right on the rim (no single arched handle over the top). It’s uniformly brown wicker with a thick rolled rim. Confirm when you’ve placed it in position 12.

[11:42:55] matcher: I’m not fully sure which one you mean for position 12. I see a round tub with thick ear-like side handles and chevron weave, and another tall cylinder with small loop tabs. [11:43:08] director: Yes—basket 12 has TWO small loop/tab handles right on the rim, and the side weave is uniform (no chevron/diagonal). It’s a short round tub with a thick rolled rim—NOT the chevron ear-handle tub (basket 5) and NOT the tall cylinder hamper (basket 7).

[11:43:23] matcher: Got it—I’m placing the short round open tub with the thick rolled rim and two small loop/tab handles (uniform weave, no chevron) into position 12. Just to confirm: the handles are small tabs right on the rim and it’s clearly shorter than the tall cylinder hamper, correct? If yes, I’m ready to submit the full sequence.

[21:27:32] director: Basket 8: squat oval basket with a single arch handle; dark gray-brown wicker sides with lighter/whitish horizontal bands near the bottom (striped look). More “modern” dark tone compared to most. Confirm when placed.

[21:30:25] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical basket (like a hamper) with straight vertical sides and two small loop handles near the rim (one on each side). No big arch handle—more of an open slat/vertical weave in medium-dark brown. Confirm when placed. 

[21:30:40] matcher: got it 

[21:30:43] director: Basket 10: very shallow round tray-like basket with a single arched handle; the base has a clear spiral coil pattern, and the color is light tan with a reddish-brown section on one side. Confirm when placed. 

[21:31:42] matcher: got it 

[21:31:45] director: Basket 11: chunky deep round basket with **two** small ear-like side handles (not an arch); thick braided texture, medium brown, with a slightly flared top and dense weave. Confirm when placed. 

[21:32:26] matcher: ok 

[21:32:28] director: Basket 12: rounded-square/oval basket with a single arch handle; uniform light-to-medium brown, with fairly open vertical slats visible inside and a smooth rim—more “bucket” shape but squarer than basket 2. Confirm when placed. 

[21:35:16] matcher: all set 

[21:35:24] director: Great—basket 12 is the squarish bucket-style basket with one arch handle, medium-light brown, smooth rim, and open vertical slats visible inside (not the one with the very dark handle, not the shallow lattice rectangle, and not the greenish or dark-gray striped one).

transcript:
[21:18:21] director: Basket 1: a novelty wicker basket shaped like a duck/bird in side profile—low wide oval body with a pointed beak-like front and a small blue eye; there’s a curved lid/handle piece over its back. Let me know when you’ve got it. 

[21:21:46] matcher: Note: My "u" key often does not work. Please forgive the vowel's absence across my responses. I don't always catch the glitch. 

[21:21:52] director: Basket 1: a novelty wicker basket shaped like a duck/bird in side view—squat oval body with a pointed beak at the front and a small blue eye; there’s a curved lid/handle over the back. Confirm when placed. 

[21:22:56] matcher: Regretfully,, I do not see a bird-like basket. I see two rabbit baskets. Neither have a handle or blue eyes. 

[21:23:05] director: Let me clarify basket 1: it’s not a front-facing rabbit/cat head. It’s a side-profile duck/bird—low oval body with a pointed beak sticking out on the right, and only ONE visible small blue eye. There’s also a curved lid/handle arching over its back. If you don’t have any side-profile animal at all, tell me what animal-shaped options you do see. 

[21:24:56] matcher: I have two clear rabbits: (1) one is facing me and the other 92) is facing to the right. Basket (2) only shows one eye. No other baskets appear like an animal. 

[21:25:05] director: Ok—let’s move to basket 2: a deep, round bucket-style basket with a single thick arched handle that’s VERY dark brown/near-black. The body is medium brown with tight weave and a darker band right under the rim. Please confirm when you’ve selected it. 

[21:27:06] matcher: Would the basket part have light horizontal borders across the top and bottom? If so, (1) how many? and (2) what color? 

[21:27:17] director: For basket 2: it does NOT have light horizontal border stripes at the top/bottom. The key is the thick arched handle that’s very dark brown/near-black, on a deep round bucket-shaped basket with a darker band just under the rim. 

[21:29:28] matcher: I spotted one; bt can yo please confirm that the top horizontal border (i.e., "rim") is the same color brown as the basket part and not "darker"? 

[21:29:36] director: Yes—on basket 2 the rim/top band reads a bit darker than the main woven body (not the same tone). The handle is the darkest element: a thick near-black arch on a deep round bucket basket. 

[21:31:45] matcher: The description sounds like the one I am thinking; however, the entire basket and rim are objectively the same color. The single handle is clearly "near-black." 

[21:31:53] director: Basket 3: a light-tan rectangular picnic basket with a tall central handle/strap. The key detail: the lid is open—one top flap is flipped up to the RIGHT, so you can see the basket’s interior. Confirm when you’ve got it. 

[21:33:23] matcher: With no basket entered into slot #1, I am unable to input basket #2 into its slot...assuming the basket color discrepancy is subjective. What basket should I put into slot #1? 

[21:33:32] director: For slot #1: choose the ONLY animal basket that’s in SIDE PROFILE (facing right), with a pointed beak/muzzle sticking out and just ONE visible blue bead-like eye. It’s not the front-facing animal head (two eyes) — that front-facing one is a different basket. 

[21:34:36] matcher: Done. Next! 

[21:34:39] director: Basket 4: a small oval wicker basket with a single handle; the handle is wrapped with light material and has little reddish/pink accents where it attaches. The basket body is pale/tan and the shape is like a shallow boat. Confirm when placed. 

[21:36:17] matcher: Does the handle attach to the basket in four locations or two? 

[21:36:19] director: Basket 4 handle attaches in TWO main places (one on each side), not four; look for the light-wrapped handle with small reddish/pink bits near those side attachments. 

[21:39:02] matcher: I do not have a basket with two handle connections "with small reddish/pink bits." The basket that meets that criteria has for connections. Please describe the handle's orientation. 

[21:39:10] director: Basket 4: the handle is one tall arch that goes left-to-right over the LONG direction of the shallow oval basket (like a little canoe). The handle itself looks pale/wrapped, and there are small reddish/pink accent bits near where the handle meets the sides. The basket body is light tan and shallow. 

[21:40:28] matcher: Does the basket contain borders across the top and bottom? If so, please describe their color? 

[21:40:31] director: Basket 4: no strong contrasting top/bottom border stripes—overall it’s a light tan weave. The most distinctive thing is the pale wrapped handle with those small reddish/pink accents near the side attachments. 

[21:42:26] matcher: The basket is one color while the handle is lighter? 

[21:42:29] director: Yes—basket 4 has a light tan body, and the handle is even lighter/paler (looks wrapped), with small reddish/pink accents near where it attaches. 

[21:43:34] matcher: And the handle orientation is literally left to right, not SE to NW? 

[21:43:36] director: Correct—basket 4 handle is a normal upright arch that spans left-to-right over the basket (not a diagonal SE-to-NW handle). 

[21:45:23] matcher: The basket body itself shows no contrast in color? 

[21:45:25] director: Right—basket 4’s body is basically one light tan color without obvious contrasting bands; the handle being paler is the main contrast. 

[21:47:35] matcher: If the basket itself is "narrow," I simply do not have a basket that meets this criteria. No basket with left to light handles includes red-pink material near the connection points. 

[21:47:42] director: Basket 5: a low, rectangular tray-shaped wicker basket in uniform medium brown, with one big thick handle that looks like a twisted rope arching over the center. Please confirm when you’ve placed it. 

[21:50:19] matcher: I have only one rectanglar basket remaining; it has two handles. I chose it to move forward. 

[21:50:26] director: Clarify basket 5: it’s a LOW rectangular tray/bread basket with ONE big central arch handle, and that handle looks like a thick twisted rope. Not the rectangular one with two side handles. Can you look again for the single twisted-rope handle? 

[21:52:08] matcher: No other rectangular baskets are available to me. It is clear that my inventory is intentionally missing ones that are in yours, thus leading to these constant discrepancies. 

[21:52:15] director: Basket 1 (top-left): pick the ONLY animal basket in side profile facing right—low oval body with a pointed beak/muzzle sticking out, just ONE visible small blue bead eye, and a curved lid/handle hump over its back (not the front-facing animal head with two eyes). 

[21:53:00] matcher: Basket 6, please. 

[21:53:07] director: Basket 6: the front-facing animal-face basket—round dome/lidded body with TWO small blue bead eyes, a pink/red nose and whisker lines on the woven face (looks like a cat/rabbit head), sitting on little feet. Confirm when placed. 

[21:53:24] matcher: Done 

[21:53:32] director: Basket 7: a boxy rectangular basket with a single arched handle; the body is distinctly green/teal (vertical slats/weave) while the rim and handle are warm medium-brown. There’s a braided/scalloped trim along the top edge. Confirm when placed. 

[21:54:32] matcher: Done 

[21:54:35] director: Basket 8: squat oval basket with one handle; overall it’s grayish/dark wicker with noticeable white-painted/bleached horizontal bands near the bottom (and some lighter accents), giving it a two-tone gray + white look. Confirm when placed. 

[21:56:12] matcher: Does the rim contain two horizontal colors that vary from the main darkish color of the basket section? 

[21:56:14] director: Basket 8: yes—around the top rim there are lighter/whitish accents compared with the darker gray body, and the strongest light banding is near the bottom. Single handle, squat oval shape. 

[21:56:42] matcher: Done 

[21:56:45] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical wicker basket (like a small hamper) with TWO small loop handles near the top rim (not a big arch). Uniform medium-brown and fairly vertical-sided. Confirm when placed. 

[21:57:54] matcher: Two meet this description. Is the height excessively tall or "average"? 

[21:57:56] director: Basket 9 is the more excessively tall, straight-sided cylinder—hamper-like—taller than “average” baskets, with two small loop handles right at the top. 

[21:58:27] matcher: Done 

[21:58:30] director: Basket 10: a shallow round basket/tray with a single handle; the base has a clear spiral weave pattern, and the handle is a pale smooth arch. Overall light tan, low and platter-like. Confirm when placed. 

[22:00:19] matcher: What is the handle orientation: (1) left to right, (2) nearly front to back, or (3) SW to NE? 

[22:00:21] director: Basket 10 handle runs left-to-right across the round shallow tray (a typical upright arch over the diameter), not front-to-back or diagonal. 

[22:04:15] matcher: I have only one left to right low-lying basket with a weaved handle. 

[22:04:18] director: Basket 11: a deep, open-top round basket with TWO short ear-like handles (one on each side), no tall arch. It’s chunky, honey-brown, and the weave has a darker zigzag/diagonal band around the lower half. Confirm when placed. 

[22:05:00] matcher: Basket 10, is the handle weaved or smooth? 

[22:05:02] director: Basket 10’s handle is smooth/pale (not heavily braided), like a light-wrapped cane arch over a shallow round tray with a spiral base. 

[22:06:38] matcher: Basket 10, is the handle wrapped without palpable weaving? 

[22:06:40] director: Yes—basket 10 handle looks wrapped/smooth rather than obviously woven/braided. 

[22:09:25] matcher: I only had one other two-handled basket. to select. Basket 12, please. 

[22:09:29] director: Basket 12: a medium brown oval basket with ONE tall arch handle; the top rim forms a wavy/scalloped “M” shape with two little peaks at the front (like a gentle double-dip). More open weave on the sides than most. Confirm when placed. 

[22:11:25] matcher: Is the entire basket one distinct color? 

[22:11:27] director: Yes—basket 12 is essentially one uniform medium-brown color (no strong contrasting bands); the standout is the single arch handle plus that wavy/scalloped rim shape. 

[22:12:23] matcher: Does the handle connect to the basket in only two locations? 

[22:12:24] director: Yes—basket 12’s single arch handle connects in two spots, one on each side. 

[22:16:17] matcher: Rephrased, is the left handle positioned slightly south of west and the right handle lightly north of east? 

[22:16:19] director: Yes—those attachment points are slightly offset like that because of the wavy rim: left side a bit lower, right side a bit higher. 

[22:17:29] matcher: Basket 4, is the basket shape spherical (more round than average) or conventional? 

[22:17:37] director: Basket 4 is a conventional shallow OVAL “canoe” basket, not spherical/round. It’s low and boat-shaped with a single upright arch handle that’s paler/looks wrapped; there are small reddish/pink accent bits right at the handle’s side attachment points. 

[22:18:31] matcher: We're done. Clicking submit. 

[22:18:38] director: Sounds good—if anything looks off after submit, we can adjust. Key reminder: basket 1 is the ONLY side-profile animal facing right with a pointed beak/muzzle and just ONE blue bead eye (not the front-facing animal head with two eyes).

transcript:
[19:00:39] director: Basket 1: a novelty animal basket shaped like a duck/bird in side view—light tan wicker, squat oval body with a top opening, and a head on the right with a small pointed beak and a little blue eye. Let me know when you’ve found/placed it. 

[19:01:05] matcher: I placed it! 

[19:01:10] director: Basket 2: a round, bucket-shaped woven basket with a thick arched handle that’s very dark brown/black. The body has a tight small weave and a lighter tan band right near the rim. Confirm when placed. 

[19:02:05] matcher: I placed it! 

[19:02:08] director: Basket 3: a rectangular picnic-style basket with a hinged lid that’s open/tilted up. It has a tall central vertical handle rising from the middle, and the lid appears attached on the right side. The weave is light tan. Confirm when placed. 

[19:02:26] matcher: Placed! 

[19:02:28] director: Basket 4: an oval, shallow basket with a single arched handle; looks like whitewashed wicker with tan accents. The handle has small colored wraps/bands (reddish) near where it meets the sides. Confirm when placed. 

[19:03:17] matcher: Are the tan accents on the top of the basket part? like the rim of it I guess I'd say. 

[19:03:20] director: Yes—Basket 4 has a lighter/whitish body but a more tan rim/top edge, plus those small reddish wraps on the handle near the sides. 

[19:03:33] matcher: Placed it! 

[19:03:36] director: Basket 5: a small rectangular basket with a high arched handle made of twisted rope-like wicker. The basket body is darker golden-brown with a chunky square weave; overall looks like a sturdy little market basket. Confirm when placed. 

[19:04:52] matcher: Is the chunkier weaved part a slightly different color than the rest? 

[19:04:54] director: Yes—on Basket 5 the main body looks a bit darker/denser than the handle, and the weave is chunky like little squares/blocks. The handle is the lighter twisted-rope style. 

[19:05:46] matcher: On the handle you can see the weave twisted around it? Its not just straight rope right? 

[19:05:49] director: Correct—Basket 5’s handle looks like two strands of wicker twisted together (braided/rope-like), not a smooth straight rod. 

[19:06:05] matcher: Placed! 

[19:06:08] director: Basket 6: another novelty animal—this one is a round, lidded “cat face” basket viewed from above/front. Light straw color, with a face (eyes, nose, whiskers) on the lid and two little ear points at the top. It sits on small feet. Confirm when placed. 

[19:06:43] matcher: Placed! 

[19:06:46] director: Basket 7: a rectangular tote basket with a wide arched handle. Distinctive two-tone: the body is dark greenish/teal weave, while the rim and handle are warm orange-brown wicker with a braided band along the top edge. Confirm when placed. 

[19:07:03] matcher: Placed! 

[19:07:10] director: Basket 8: a squat, oval/rectangular basket with a single handle; overall grey-brown wicker with lighter whitish stakes showing in the weave. Has a darker band around the top rim and a medium-height arched handle. Confirm when placed. 

[19:07:45] matcher: Does the weave kind of look thick like sticks in a way? 

[19:07:47] director: Yes—Basket 8 has a chunkier “stick-like” weave with visible thicker ribs, and the coloring alternates grey-brown with lighter whitish vertical pieces. 

[19:08:22] matcher: Placed! 

[19:08:25] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical hamper-like basket, medium brown, with two small loop handles near the top (one on each side). The weave is vertical and it’s noticeably taller than the others—like a basket bin. Confirm when placed. 

[19:09:05] matcher: Does it look like it has two handles that are doubled up? Almost like it has 4 handles? 

[19:09:07] director: Yes—each side handle on Basket 9 looks like a doubled loop (so it can read like 4 small loops total), sitting right near the top rim. 

[19:09:15] matcher: Placed! 

[19:09:18] director: Basket 10: a shallow, round tray-like basket with a single arched handle. Very light tan, with a spiral coil pattern on the base (concentric rings), and the handle attaches at opposite sides of the round tray. Confirm when placed. 

[19:10:03] matcher: Is the inside of the basket and the handle lighter than the outside of the basket? 

[19:10:06] director: Yes—Basket 10 has a slightly darker/reddish outer side wall, while the interior base and the handle look lighter straw/tan. 

[19:10:16] matcher: Placed! 

[19:10:18] director: Basket 11: a deep, round tub basket with no tall handle—just two small side ear-handles. It’s a warm brown wicker with a diagonal chevron-like weave around the sides, and the side handles stick up like short horns. Confirm when placed. 

[19:11:21] matcher: Is it bigger than the one you described as a basket bin? 

[19:11:23] director: No—Basket 11 is shorter and wider (a tub). Basket 9 is the tallest “bin/hamper” cylinder. Basket 11 just looks chunky and deep with those two small ear-handles. 

[19:12:11] matcher: Placed 

[19:12:13] director: Basket 12: a medium brown oval basket with a single arched handle, but the rim has two raised peaks/notches—like an “M” shape at the top edges where the handle meets. The sides are more open slatted weave compared to some others. Confirm when placed. 

[19:13:01] matcher: Does it have white and tan weave? 

[19:13:04] director: No—Basket 12 is pretty uniformly medium brown. The one with more whitish/tan mixed weave is Basket 8. Basket 12’s key cue is that wavy/M-shaped rim with two high points under the handle. 

[19:13:38] matcher: The "M" shape, is that all the way around the rim like a design? 

[19:13:41] director: It’s mainly at the front/back edges of the rim—two raised points (left and right) with a dip in the middle, giving that “M/wavy” silhouette when you look at the top edge. It’s not a continuous pattern all the way around. 

[19:14:42] matcher: Oh, you mean the weave itself looks like an M? 

[19:14:44] director: Not the weave pattern—it's the rim outline/shape of the basket opening: the top edge rises into two points (where the handle attaches) with a lower dip between them, making an “M” profile. 

[19:15:25] matcher: Placed 

[19:15:30] director: Great—basket 1 is the duck/bird-shaped wicker basket in side view: oval body with an open top and a head on the right with a pointed beak and a little blue eye.
