transcript:
[19:15:57] director: Basket 1 (top-left): a light tan rectangular picnic basket with a hinged lid that’s flipped open/standing up, and a tall upright handle rising from the center. It’s the only one with an open lid. Tell me when you’ve placed it. 

[19:16:15] matcher: Placed 

[19:16:20] director: Basket 1: light tan rectangular picnic basket with a hinged lid propped open, and a tall central upright handle rising from the middle. It’s the only one with an open lid. Confirm when placed. 

[19:16:45] matcher: I placed it 

[19:16:50] director: Basket 1 (top-left): light tan rectangular picnic basket with a hinged lid propped open and a tall upright handle in the middle. Only one with an open lid. Confirm when placed. 

[19:16:55] matcher: What is basket 2? 

[19:17:02] director: Basket 2: the tall cylindrical hamper/bin basket, medium-brown, open top, with two small doubled loop handles right near the top rim (can look like 4 little loops). Much taller than the others. Let me know when it’s placed. 

[19:17:38] matcher: Does the weave look like chevron style, or is it horizontal? 

[19:17:45] director: Basket 2 is NOT chevron—its weave reads more straight/vertical and banded. The key is it’s a tall cylinder/hamper with small doubled loop handles right at the top rim (can look like 4 little loops total). 

[19:18:04] matcher: Placed 

[19:18:09] director: Basket 1 (top-left): the light tan rectangular picnic basket with a hinged lid propped open and a tall upright handle rising from the center. It’s the only one with an open lid—confirm when placed. 

[19:18:20] matcher: What is basket 3? 

[19:18:27] director: Basket 3: the round, bowl/bucket-shaped basket with a very dark (almost black) thick arched handle; the body is brown with a lighter tan band right under the rim. Confirm when you’ve placed it. 

[19:19:03] matcher: Is the weave vertical or horizontal? 

[19:19:06] director: Basket 3’s weave looks mostly tight and horizontal/diagonal around the bowl, not vertical staves. The standout is the very dark thick handle over a rounded bowl body with that lighter band near the top. 

[19:19:50] matcher: Is the woven material almost like a stick? Like really thick? 

[19:19:53] director: No—Basket 3 is a finer, tight weave (not thick stick-like). The “stick-like/chunky” one is the grey-brown basket we called Basket 8 earlier. 

[19:20:16] matcher: And horizontal you mean side to side, not up and down? 

[19:20:19] director: Yes—side-to-side bands wrapping around the bowl (not up-and-down vertical ribs). 

[19:20:59] matcher: Does the band right before the handle look like it has an arrow like design almost? Like a pointed pattern 

[19:21:02] director: No, Basket 3’s light band is pretty plain—just a lighter strip below the rim. The arrow/chevron-looking weave is on a different basket (the tub basket with small side handles). 

[19:21:20] matcher: Placed, what is basket 4? 

[19:21:29] director: Basket 4: the small, shallow market basket with a very high arched handle that looks like twisted rope/braided wicker (two strands). The body weave is chunky and square/block-like, in golden-brown. Confirm when placed. 

[19:22:00] matcher: Placed, what is basket 5? 

[19:22:08] director: Basket 5: the grey-to-blackish woven basket with a medium arched handle and a darker band around the top rim; you can see lighter whitish vertical stakes in the weave (kind of thick stick-like). More boxy/rectangular than the round ones. Confirm when placed. 

[19:23:06] matcher: Is the rim, bottom, and handle brown and the body is a greyish green color almost? 

[19:23:15] director: Basket 5: yes—handle and rim are brownish/grey-brown, and the body reads grey/charcoal with lighter whitish vertical stakes showing; plus there’s a darker band right at the top rim. It’s NOT the teal-bodied one with orange-brown rim/handle (that’s basket 7). 

[19:24:55] matcher: I dont feel like I'm seeing this one. The only one that looks like it has a grey body almost looks teal as well. 

[19:25:04] director: For basket 5: look for the *grey/charcoal* handled basket (not the teal one). It has a darker band right along the top rim, and you can clearly see lighter whitish vertical ribs/stakes in the weave—kind of chunky stick-like. The handle and rim are grey-brown, but there’s no bright orange-brown trim like the teal basket has. Do you see that one? 

[19:25:44] matcher: And you said its more rectangular? 

[19:25:48] director: Yes—basket 5 is more boxy/rectangular (like a little tote), not a perfect round bowl. The teal one (basket 7) is also rectangular, but has very obvious orange-brown rim/handle and a braided trim; basket 5 is greyer/charcoal with whitish vertical stakes and a dark top band. 

[19:26:20] matcher: Does it have red spots at the base of the handle and white spots near the middle/top of the handle? 

[19:26:24] director: No—that description (red wraps at the handle base) matches basket 11, the whitish oval basket with tan rim and little reddish wraps. Basket 5 has no red accents; it’s grey/charcoal with whitish vertical stakes and a dark band at the top rim. 

[19:27:31] matcher: Does it have a handle? 

[19:27:35] director: Yes—basket 5 has a single medium-height arched handle. If you’re not seeing it, it might be getting confused with similar grey/white baskets in your distractors; focus on the dark band right at the top rim plus those whitish vertical stakes. 

[19:28:35] matcher: Oh, is it the one with a tan handle, band, and bottom? And the top of the weave right before the rim is white? And between the white and bottom there is a dark piece seperating the two colors? 

[19:28:43] director: Yes—if you’re seeing one with a tan-ish handle/rim and a darker strip just under the rim plus some pale/whitish vertical stakes in the side weave, that’s Basket 5. Key check: it has NO red wraps on the handle (those are on basket 11), and it’s more grey/charcoal than the obvious teal-with-orange-trim basket 7. 

[19:29:10] matcher: Placed, what is basket 6? 

[19:29:17] director: Basket 6: the novelty animal basket shaped like a duck/bird in side view—light tan wicker, oval body with a top opening, and a head on the right with a pointed beak and a small blue eye. Confirm when placed. 

[19:29:36] matcher: Placed, what is basket 7? 

[19:29:43] director: Basket 7: the rectangular tote with a dark green/teal woven body, but a clearly orange-brown rim and matching orange-brown arched handle. There’s a braided/rope trim running along the top edge. Confirm when you’ve placed it. 

[19:30:05] matcher: Placed, what is basket 8? 

[19:30:13] director: Basket 8: the shallow, round tray-like basket with a single arched handle and a very clear spiral/concentric coil pattern on the base. The interior base + handle are lighter straw, while the outer side wall is a bit darker/reddish. Confirm when placed.

transcript:
[19:34:36] director: Basket 1 (top-left): a rectangular tote basket with a dark green/teal woven body, but a clearly orange-brown rim and matching orange-brown arched handle; there’s a braided/rope trim running along the top edge. Let me know when you’ve placed it.

[19:36:58] director: On Basket 3, the noticeable wraps are the reddish ones at the handle bases. [19:37:53] director: More reddish-brown—small dark red/maroon accents, not bright fire-engine red.

[19:38:45] director: Yes—the basket’s body weave is very light/whitish overall (whitewashed look), with the rim more tan.

[19:39:28] director: Right—no distinct “white wrap bands” as a feature; the standout is the reddish-brown wraps at the handle bases.

The key accents are the *small reddish-brown wraps at the two handle attachment points* (where the handle meets the sides). If the handle also has a lighter/whitish section near the top, that’s fine—but the must-have is pale body + tan rim + red-brown wraps at the bases. 

[19:40:18] matcher: Placed, what is number 4?

[22:31:21] director: Basket 1: a novelty wicker basket shaped like a mouse/rat—round body with a pointed snout sticking out to the right, two little ears on top (one like a loop/handle), and small bead-like eyes. Let me know when you’ve found/placed that one. 

[22:32:35] matcher: done 

[22:32:40] director: Basket 2: a deep, rounded bucket-style wicker basket with a very dark brown/black thick arched handle; medium-brown tight weave and a subtle horizontal band right under the rim. Confirm when placed.

[22:35:13] director: Basket 2 clarification: choose the *darker* overall one with a *narrower* band just below the rim (thin strip), and the handle is the darkest element—almost black—smooth and thick. The lighter one with the bigger band is not it. 

[22:35:27] matcher: done 

[22:35:29] director: Basket 3: a rectangular, lidded picnic-style basket with a hinged lid propped open to the right, and a tall central vertical handle that rises between lid and base. It’s tan wicker with a boxy shape. Confirm when placed. 

[22:35:54] matcher: done 

[22:35:56] director: Basket 4: an oval basket with a single arched handle; light tan wicker but with distinct white-painted bands/stripes around the body, plus small reddish wrap accents where the handle meets the rim. Confirm when placed. 

[22:36:46] matcher: done 

[22:36:49] director: Basket 5: a shallow, rectangular basket with a very thick twisted-rope looking handle arching high; darker honey-brown weave with a chunky checkered/blocked texture. No lid. Confirm when placed. 

[22:38:28] matcher: I see a rectangular basket but it has a lid 

[22:38:31] director: Let me clarify basket 5: it’s the *rectangular open* basket (no lid) with a *single thick twisted* handle that looks like two strands braided/spiraled. The rim is low and the body has a chunky square “woven blocks” look. The rectangular one with a lid is a different basket (basket 3). 

[22:39:20] matcher: does it have a brown woven material on the body 

[22:39:23] director: Yes—basket 5 is all brown wicker on the body, kind of a golden/honey brown, with that chunky blocky weave; the key is the thick twisted handle and the low, open rectangular shape. 

[22:39:39] matcher: done 

[22:39:42] director: Basket 6: a round, lidded wicker basket shaped like a cat face—on top you see a cat’s eyes, nose, whiskers, and two small ears; it sits on little feet. Very light/tan straw color. Confirm when placed. 

[22:40:06] matcher: done 

[22:40:09] director: Basket 7: a rectangular basket with a tall arched handle and a two-tone look—greenish/teal vertical weaving on the sides with a warm orange-brown rim and handle. The top edge has a braided trim. Confirm when placed. 

[22:40:28] matcher: done 

[22:40:31] director: Basket 8: a small squat oval basket with a dark brown/gray handle and darker body; the weave includes alternating light/cream and dark bands, giving a striped two-tone appearance near the bottom. Confirm when placed. 

[22:41:12] matcher: does it have a tan band around the top and bottom 

[22:41:15] director: Yes—basket 8 has lighter tan/cream bands, especially a pale band near the base and some lighter weaving mixed in; the overall basket and handle are darker (gray-brown) and it’s a short, rounded oval shape. 

[22:41:25] matcher: done 

[22:41:28] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical wicker basket, like a hamper, with two small loop handles near the top rim. Medium brown, vertical slat-like weave, open top. Confirm when placed. 

[22:41:46] matcher: done 

[22:41:49] director: Basket 10: a very shallow, round tray-like basket with a single thin handle arching over it; light tan with a spiral “coiled” pattern in the base (concentric rings). Confirm when placed. 

[22:42:21] matcher: done 

[22:42:24] director: Basket 11: a large, round, deep basket with two small side handles (ear-like) built into the rim; darker tan/brown with a diagonal weave pattern and thick rim. No single top handle. Confirm when placed. 

[22:43:22] matcher: are there 2additional baskets inside 

[22:43:25] director: Basket 11: it’s just the one big basket—no extra baskets nested inside. The “two shapes” you may be noticing are the two *side handles* that stick up from the rim (one on each side). 

[22:43:40] matcher: done 

[22:43:42] director: Basket 12: a medium oval basket with a single arched handle; light reddish-brown wicker, and the rim has two pointed “peaks”/raised corners (an M-shaped rim profile). More open weave on the sides. Confirm when placed. 

[22:44:42] matcher: does it have a braided band with slightly higher sides 

[22:44:45] director: Yes—basket 12 has a braided-looking rim band, and the sides come up fairly high for an oval basket; the key is that rim with two raised points/peaks and the single central handle. 

[22:45:07] matcher: one solid color 

[22:45:09] director: Basket 12 should look mostly like one solid light reddish-brown tone overall (not strongly two-toned/striped). The weave is fairly uniform; the standout is the single handle plus the two peaked rim corners. 

[22:45:32] matcher: done

transcript:
[22:32:17] director: Basket 1: the light tan wicker basket shaped like a bird/duck seen from the SIDE—pointed beak/snout sticking out to the right, two small bead-like eyes, and an oval opening/lid on the top-back (not the round cat-face one). Confirm when you’ve got it. 

[22:33:07] matcher: is it a lighter or darker colored 

[22:33:14] director: It’s the lighter honey‑tan one (not dark). Side-profile bird/duck with a pointed beak to the right and an oval opening on the top/back. 

[22:33:24] matcher: got it 

[22:33:30] director: Basket 2: a deep, rounded bucket-style basket with a wide open top and a very dark (almost black) thick arched handle; the body is medium brown with tight small weave and a darker rim band at the top. Let me know when you’ve placed it. 

[22:34:10] matcher: got it 

[22:34:13] director: Basket 3: a rectangular picnic hamper with a fitted lid and a tall vertical strap/handle in the center; the lid is flipped open to the right side (hinged), showing the inside. Light golden wicker, boxy shape. Confirm when set. 

[22:34:46] matcher: I don't see anything with a lid 

[22:34:50] director: Let me clarify basket 3: it’s the only boxy rectangular basket that has a hinged top—one half is standing open/up on the right, and there’s a tall upright center handle/strap rising from the middle (like a picnic hamper). It’s light golden wicker. If you still don’t see a lid, look for the rectangular one with the “flap” piece sticking up on the right side. 

[22:36:38] matcher: yeah, still no lid or hinge I have several rectangular baskets though that have handles 

[22:36:46] director: Basket 3 is the ONLY rectangular “picnic hamper” where a lid/flap is visibly OPEN: there’s a vertical strap handle in the center, and on the RIGHT side a top panel is sticking up like an open lid, showing the inside. It’s light golden wicker (not green like basket 7, and not an open-top rectangle like basket 5). Can you spot that raised flap on the right? 

[22:38:24] matcher: No, I can't,,, I though maybe I got 2 wrong but I checked it and it's round 

[22:38:28] director: Ok—keep basket 2 as the round one with the very dark/black handle. For basket 3, in your pool it might look like a rectangular basket with an unusually tall center handle PLUS an extra diagonal/angled piece rising on the right (the lid). The “lid” piece can resemble a second handle leaning to the right. Choose the light tan rectangular one where there are TWO upright elements: the main center handle straight up, and another piece slanting up on the right edge. 

[22:39:27] matcher: OK is there a lighter color woven into the basket near the top 

[22:39:36] director: For basket 3: no, it’s pretty uniformly light tan/golden—no distinct lighter band woven near the top. Key cue is the TWO uprights: a tall straight center strap handle, plus another piece rising/leaning up on the right side (that’s the open lid panel), unlike the green rectangle (basket 7) or the single-arch open basket (basket 5). 

[22:40:33] matcher: Ok, I chose the one that most looks like you've described 

[22:40:40] director: Basket 4: a pale beige/whitish wicker basket that’s elongated like a little boat/oval sling, with a single arched handle that looks wrapped (and has small reddish accents where it attaches). More light-colored than most others. Confirm when placed. 

[22:41:40] matcher: Oh shoot, I've made a mistake, I've found 3, can I go back and place it 

[22:41:46] director: Yes—go back and replace basket 3. It’s the light tan rectangular picnic-hamper with a tall straight center strap handle AND a second upright piece on the right that’s actually the lid flipped open (diagonal/leaning up). It should look like it has an open top with that right-side flap raised. 

[22:42:18] matcher: And you said it's half open, rith 

[22:42:20] director: Yes—basket 3 has the lid partially open: the right-side top panel is lifted up/leaning outward, while the main body stays rectangular. The tall center strap handle stands straight up in the middle. 

[22:42:30] matcher: Got it, now onto 4 

[22:42:33] director: Basket 4: a light cream/whitish wicker basket shaped like a long oval “canoe,” with one arched handle; the handle has wrapped sections and small reddish/brown tie points where it meets the rim. The basket body is the palest in the top row. Confirm when set. 

[22:43:36] matcher: palest in the top row 

[22:43:38] director: Yes—basket 4 is very pale/cream compared to the others on the top row (lighter than the brown basket 2, lighter than the golden hamper 3, and not the animal-face baskets 1 or 6). 

[22:43:59] matcher: and it has a handle? 

[22:44:01] director: Yes—one single arched handle over the top. The basket itself is an elongated oval/boat shape and very light cream. 

[22:44:06] matcher: ok, got it 

[22:44:09] director: Basket 5: a shallow rectangular open-top basket, medium brown, with a thick twisted-rope style handle arching high. The sides have a chunky checker/brick weave, and it’s wider than it is tall. Confirm when placed. 

[22:45:38] matcher: open topped? 

[22:45:41] director: Yes—basket 5 is open at the top (no lid). It’s a low, rectangular tray-like basket with that big twisted handle over it. 

[22:45:43] matcher: handle? 

[22:45:44] director: Single handle, arched over the center—looks like a thick twisted rope made of wicker. 

[22:46:13] matcher: rectangular? 

[22:46:15] director: Yes, rectangular base and straight-ish sides; it’s a low open rectangle with the high twisted handle. 

[22:46:41] matcher: Really pretty darker weave and chunky weave at top? 

[22:46:43] director: Yes—that matches basket 5: medium-to-darker brown overall, with a chunky, blocky weave especially around the upper band, and the thick twisted handle. 

[22:46:58] matcher: got i 

[22:47:01] director: Basket 6: the round lidded basket that looks like a cat face from above—light tan circular lid with a face (nose/whiskers) and two small ear shapes, plus two bead-like eyes. It sits on little feet. Confirm when placed. 

[22:47:56] matcher: OK I may have mixed 1 and 5 up, I think 1 looks more like a rabbit and 5 like a cat 

[22:48:00] director: Basket 1 is the SIDE-profile animal (bird/duck/rabbit-like) with a pointed snout/beak sticking out to the right and an oval opening on the top/back. Basket 6 is the TOP-view round “cat face” with whiskers and ears. Basket 5 is just a plain rectangular open basket with a twisted handle—no animal face at all. 

[22:48:20] matcher: ok let me change that 

[22:48:23] director: Great—once you’ve swapped, confirm basket 1 is the side-profile snout/beak one, basket 5 is the plain rectangular open basket with twisted handle, and basket 6 is the round cat-face lidded one. 

[22:48:35] matcher: ok i have them fixed 

[22:48:38] director: Basket 7: a rectangular basket with a single arched handle; it has distinct TWO-TONE weaving—green/teal vertical slats in the body with brown/orange wicker framing and a braided band along the top edge. Confirm when placed. 

[22:49:23] matcher: got it 

[22:49:25] director: Basket 8: a small squat oval basket with an arched handle, dark gray-brown wicker with lighter cream/white accents woven through near the base and rim—overall looks “dark” compared to most. Confirm when set. 

[22:50:48] matcher: um are you sure it's not roung 

[22:50:51] director: Yes—basket 8 is rounded/oval (not rectangular). It’s like a small bucket/oval shape with one handle, dark gray-brown weave, and noticeable lighter whitish strands near the bottom edge. 

[22:51:27] matcher: kind of a rounded handle 

[22:51:30] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical basket (like a woven barrel) with two small loop handles near the top rim. Medium brown, vertical reed look, open top. Confirm when placed. 

[22:51:59] matcher: got it 

[22:52:02] director: Basket 10: a shallow round tray basket with a single arched handle; the base shows a spiral coil pattern, and the colors are mixed light tan with some reddish-brown bands. Confirm when set. 

[22:52:29] matcher: 10 has no lid 

[22:52:31] director: Correct—basket 10 has no lid. It’s an open shallow round tray with a handle and a spiral pattern on the bottom. 

[22:52:56] matcher: got it 

[22:52:59] director: Basket 11: a large deep round basket, thick woven, with TWO integrated side handles (no arched bail handle). It’s brown/tan and has a chunky diagonal weave; looks like a sturdy tub. Confirm when placed. 

[22:53:31] matcher: does it appear to have another basket inside of it 

[22:53:34] director: No—basket 11 doesn’t have another basket inside. It’s just a deep tub with two side handles; the interior weave may make it look layered, but there isn’t a separate inner basket. 

[22:54:05] matcher: could I have mixed 9 up 

[22:54:08] director: Possibly: Basket 9 is the tall cylinder/barrel with two small loop handles right at the top rim (and no big side handles). Basket 11 is wider and more tub-like, with larger integrated side handles and a diagonal chunky weave. 

[22:54:27] matcher: well 9 is the tallest 

[22:54:30] director: Yes—basket 9 should be the tallest one in the set: a vertical cylinder/barrel shape with the two small loop handles near the top. 

[22:55:41] matcher: well 9 is tall with two looped handles and you're saying 11 also has looped handles 

[22:55:44] director: Clarifying: basket 11 does NOT have small loop handles at the rim. It has two big integrated SIDE handles (like cutout/rolled handles attached to the sides), and the basket is shorter and wider than 9. Basket 9 is the tall barrel with the small loop handles up at the top edge. 

[22:57:13] matcher: ok, I hope thats right 

[22:57:16] director: Basket 12: a small open-top oval basket with a single arched handle; uniform light-medium brown, smooth tight weave, and it has a slightly “pinched” waist in the middle (narrower midsection than the top rim). Confirm when placed. 

[22:57:41] matcher: got it 

[22:57:48] director: (Basket 1 recap if needed) It’s the light honey-tan animal-shaped basket in SIDE profile, with a pointed snout/beak sticking out to the RIGHT and a small bead-like eye; there’s an oval opening on the top/back—NOT the round cat-face (6) and not the plain rectangular open basket (5). 

[22:58:08] matcher: yes 

[22:58:13] director: Great—then you’re set. Basket 1 is the light honey‑tan SIDE-profile animal with a pointed snout/beak to the RIGHT and a small bead eye, plus an oval opening on the top/back (not the round cat-face 6 and not the plain rectangular handle basket 5).

[23:44:44] director: Basket 1 (top-left): a very shallow, wide oval/round basket like a tray, with one tall arch handle. The base has a really clear spiral/coil pattern, and it’s mostly light straw with a darker brown rim/outer side. Let me know when you’ve placed it. 

[23:45:45] matcher: i got it placed - i don't know what happend last round but we only got 3 right 

[23:45:51] director: Basket 1 (top-left): very shallow, tray-like oval basket with ONE tall arch handle; the inside bottom has a very clear spiral/coil pattern in light straw. Please confirm when it’s placed. 

[23:46:04] matcher: done 

[23:46:08] director: Basket 2: a small squat rectangular basket with a high arch handle; tight “checker”/blocky weave. The handle looks like twisted rope (thicker, braided). Warm medium-brown. Confirm when placed. 

[23:46:38] matcher: what do you mean by squat? 

[23:46:47] director: By “squat” I mean it’s low/short and wider than it is tall (not a deep basket). Basket 2 is a low rectangular basket with a chunky checker weave and a very thick twisted-rope style arch handle. 

[23:47:15] matcher: done 

[23:47:20] director: Basket 3: novelty animal—round lidded basket shaped like a duck/bird, with the head/beak sticking out to the right and a small eye. Has a flat slot-style handle on top. Confirm when placed. 

[23:47:28] matcher: done 

[23:47:32] director: Basket 4: tall cylindrical “barrel” basket, open top, medium brown, with two small loop handles near the rim (one each side). The weave looks like vertical slats. Confirm when placed. 

[23:48:18] matcher: are the loop handles single or double 

[23:48:22] director: They’re single loop handles—one simple loop on each side near the top (not doubled). 

[23:48:39] matcher: done 

[23:48:43] director: Basket 5: a big deep round tub basket with NO top handle; instead it has two integrated side “ear” handles. Medium brown diagonal weave with a thick rolled rim. Confirm when placed. 

[23:49:17] matcher: this one looks like the handles are double correct 

[23:49:22] director: Yes—basket 5’s side “ear” handles look like doubled/thicker loops (each side has a chunky handle that reads like two strands). The key is: no arching top handle at all, just the two side ears on a deep tub. 

[23:49:35] matcher: done 

[23:49:38] director: Basket 6: the cat-face lidded basket—round with the cat’s eyes/whiskers on the lid, and it stands on little feet. Confirm when placed. 

[23:49:45] matcher: done 

[23:49:50] director: Basket 7 (bottom-left): a medium honey-brown rectangular basket with straight sides and a normal smooth arch handle. It has a woven “grid” look, and a noticeable horizontal band near the top; not twisted-rope handle. Confirm when placed. 

[23:51:20] matcher: does it have two handles 

[23:51:24] director: Basket 7 has ONE main arch handle over the top (not two). If you see two small side loops, that’s the tall barrel-style basket, not this one. 

[23:53:44] matcher: how is the basket positioned in the picture- is it at an angle or straight on 

[23:53:48] director: Basket 7 is shown mostly straight-on (front facing), not at a strong angle—so you see the rectangular front clearly with the handle arching up symmetrically. 

[23:55:31] matcher: the horizontal band at the top is there a skinny dark band in the middle of the basket 

[23:55:35] director: No—on basket 7 the standout banding is mainly near the TOP/rim area. There isn’t a skinny dark stripe running across the middle of the basket body. 

[23:55:51] matcher: done 

[23:55:55] director: Basket 8: an oval shallow basket with a single arch handle; two-tone weave (light straw with darker brown accents) and small tie/wrap points where the handle meets the sides. Kind of boat-shaped. Confirm when placed. 

[23:56:48] matcher: does the rim look like its braided 

[23:56:53] director: Yes—basket 8’s rim/top edge has a braided/scalloped look, and the handle rises from that rim with small wrap/tie points at the attachments. 

[23:57:45] matcher: can you see the inside bottom of the basket 

[23:57:49] director: Yes—you can see into basket 8; it’s an open basket and the inside bottom is visible (no lid). The view is slightly angled so the interior shows. 

[23:59:03] matcher: no i mean can you see pretty much the whole bottom and does it have a darker weave in the middle center 

[23:59:08] director: You can see a good portion of the inside bottom, but not perfectly flat like a top-down view. And no—there isn’t a distinct darker patch in the dead center; the interior looks fairly uniform light straw with some darker accents around the weave/rim. 

[00:00:29] matcher: the darker rim and does it have the same dark accent around the outside bottom 

[00:00:33] director: It has darker accents mostly around the rim/upper weave and along some vertical strands; it does NOT have a strong, continuous dark band wrapping the outside bottom. 

[00:02:20] matcher: which basket is more shallow 1 or 9 

[00:02:25] director: Basket 1 is more shallow—it’s the low tray-like one. Basket 9 (later in the sequence) is deeper/taller. 

[00:03:10] matcher: next 

[00:03:21] director: Basket 9: a very light/whitish oval basket shown at an angle, with ONE arch handle. Key tell: there are two darker brown vertical side struts that run up and attach to the handle (and the handle has pale wrap/tape sections). Not the spiral tray. Confirm when placed. 

[00:06:48] matcher: done 

[00:06:53] director: Basket 10: a squat oval basket with one arch handle; dark gray-brown weave with lighter cream/white stripes near the base. Darker rim. Confirm when placed. 

[00:07:39] matcher: done 

[00:07:44] director: Basket 11: a shallow round/oval open basket in warm orange-brown, with one tall arch handle. The weave is more uniform than the spiral tray; you don’t see a big pale spiral dominating the base. Confirm when placed. 

[00:08:06] matcher: done 

[00:08:11] director: Basket 12: a small rectangular basket with a high arch handle and very tight checker/block weave. The handle is thick and twisted-rope/braided looking. (Similar to basket 2—make sure it’s the one with the most pronounced chunky checker pattern.) Confirm when placed. 

[00:09:15] matcher: is the color uniform 

[00:09:20] director: Yes—basket 12 is pretty uniform warm medium-brown overall (no strong two-tone panels). The main standout is the chunky checker weave and the thick twisted-rope handle. 

[00:11:33] matcher: is the rim braided or does it look like it has pieces that are wrapped around the rim 

[00:11:37] director: Basket 12’s rim looks like wrapped/stacked horizontal strands rather than a scalloped braided rim. The braid-like feature is mainly the handle (twisted-rope look), not the rim. 

[00:12:42] matcher: ok im going to submit 

[00:12:54] director: Before you hit submit, quick sanity check on Basket 1: it’s the *shallowest tray-like* oval/round basket with ONE arch handle, and the inside bottom shows a very obvious light-tan *spiral/coil* pattern. 

[00:13:08] matcher: yes 

[00:13:17] director: Basket 1: novelty animal basket shaped like a duck/bird—round lidded body with the head/beak sticking out to the RIGHT (little eye). It has a flat slot-style handle on top (not a tall arch handle). Let me know when it’s placed. 

[00:13:30] matcher: wait what? 

[00:13:41] director: Sorry—my mistake: that last message was wrong. For THIS round, basket 1 is the duck/bird-shaped lidded basket: rounded body with the head/beak sticking out to the RIGHT and a little eye, plus a flat slot-style handle on top (not a tall arch handle). Can you confirm basket 1 is set to the duck? 

[00:13:56] matcher: no its not 

[00:14:09] director: We need to fix basket 1: it should be the DUCK/BIRD-shaped lidded basket—round body with the head/beak sticking out to the RIGHT (little eye), and a flat slot-style handle on top. Please swap basket 1 to that duck and tell me when it’s corrected. 

[00:14:09] matcher: that was basket 3 

[00:14:16] director: You’re right—duck was basket 3 in the earlier run. For the actual target grid at the start of this round: **Basket 1 is the shallow oval tray with a single arch handle and a very clear spiral/coil pattern on the base**. Please keep basket 1 as that spiral tray, and keep the duck/bird as basket 3. Can you confirm basket 1 is the spiral tray and basket 3 is the duck? 

[00:15:00] matcher: yes - do you wanna just double check and make sure we have the right order just give a short litte descritption of all 12 

[00:15:05] director: I can’t do all 12 in one message (game rule: one basket per message). I *can* re-check them with you one-by-one in order. Reconfirm **Basket 1**: shallow oval/round **tray** basket, **one arch handle**, and the base has a very clear **spiral/coil** pattern in light straw. Is that what you have in position 1? 

[00:15:17] matcher: yes 

[00:15:28] director: Basket 2 check: the low/squat rectangular basket with a very chunky checker/block weave, and a thick twisted-rope/braided arch handle (uniform warm brown). Do you have that in position 2? 

[00:15:53] matcher: yes 

[00:16:02] director: Basket 1: the novelty duck/bird-shaped lidded basket—rounded body with the head and beak sticking out to the RIGHT (small eye). It has a flat slot-style handle on top, not a tall arch handle. Confirm when you’ve placed it. 

[00:16:06] matcher: yes 

[00:16:15] director: Basket 3: the novelty duck/bird-shaped lidded basket—round body with a head and beak sticking out to the RIGHT (tiny eye). It has a flat slot-style handle on top (not an arched handle). Do you have that in position 3? 

[00:16:35] matcher: yes 

[00:16:45] director: Basket 1: the novelty duck/bird-shaped lidded basket—round body with the head/beak sticking out to the RIGHT (you can see a little eye). It has a flat slot-style handle on top, not a tall arch handle. Please confirm when you’ve placed it.

[14:37:23] director: Basket 3: a rectangular picnic-style basket with a tall vertical center handle, and a hinged lid that’s flipped open to the right side (you can see the lid standing up). More boxy than the others. Confirm when set?

[14:38:27] director: Basket 4: an oval, slightly boat-shaped light basket with a single arched handle; the handle has little colored bands/wraps on it (reddish/brown accents). The basket weave looks pale with some darker horizontal stripes. Please confirm when placed. 

[14:41:01] matcher: got it 

[14:41:03] director: Basket 5: a small rectangular, low basket with a high arched handle; the handle is twisted/rope-like and the basket has a tight blocky weave. Overall medium honey-brown, no lid. Confirm? 

[14:43:25] matcher: got it 

[14:43:28] director: Basket 6: another animal basket—front-facing cat head/round body. Very pale straw color, with two bead-like eyes, a little nose and whisker lines; it sits on small feet and looks like a round cat face. Please confirm. 

[14:43:46] matcher: got it 

[14:43:53] director: Basket 7: a wide rectangular basket with a single smooth arch handle, but the body is two-tone—dark green/olive woven sides with a tan/orange rim and base. It’s the only one with green coloring. Confirm when you’ve placed it. 

[14:48:17] matcher: Ok. I've made a mistake. The one I put at 5 should be here. I hope they let us change our choices. 

[14:48:24] director: No worries—yes, switch if you can. Now basket 8: a small stout oval basket in gray/charcoal wicker, with a dark smooth arched handle and a couple lighter whitish bands around the lower part of the sides. Confirm when set. 

[14:58:26] matcher: got it 

[14:58:28] director: Basket 9: a tall cylindrical basket (like a woven barrel) with two small loop handles near the top rim. It’s upright and much taller than it is wide. Confirm when placed. 

[14:58:50] matcher: got it 

[14:58:52] director: Basket 10: a shallow, round platter-like basket with a single arched handle over the top; the base shows a spiral coil pattern in the weave. Very low and wide compared to others. Confirm? 

[15:00:26] matcher: Is the handle more north and south or east and west? 

[15:00:33] director: For basket 10, the single handle runs left-to-right across the round shallow tray (east–west). Key tell: it’s the flattest, platter-like one with a clear spiral weave on the base. 

[15:01:01] matcher: got it 

[15:01:10] director: Basket 11: a round tub/bucket-shaped wicker basket with NO over-the-top handle—instead it has two thick ‘ear’ handles on the left and right sides. The sides have a strong diagonal/chevron weave and it’s a darker brown. Confirm when placed. 

[15:03:41] matcher: got it 

[15:03:43] director: Basket 12: a medium oval basket with a single arched handle and a distinctive wavy/scalloped rim edge on the front. Light brown overall, with more open vertical slats visible inside. Please confirm when set.

[15:53:40] director: Hi, the first basket looks like a rabbit with no legs. 

[15:53:47] matcher: For position 1, I see two rabbit baskets: one is a long sideways rabbit with a handle on top, and the other is a round rabbit face with little feet. When you say “rabbit with no legs,” do you mean the long sideways one with the handle on its back?

[15:54:15] matcher: Got it—no handle. For position 1, is it the long, horizontal rabbit-shaped basket (side profile), not the round rabbit face one with little feet? 

[15:54:34] director: Correct! 

[15:54:39] matcher: Placing the side-profile rabbit basket with no handle in position 1. Just to confirm: it’s the lying-down rabbit, not the round rabbit-face one with little feet. What’s basket #2 like? 

[15:55:44] director: Yes! The second basket is all one color and has a rounded bottom and rounded handle on top. 

[15:55:49] matcher: For position 2, I think it’s the rounded, bowl-shaped basket with a single arched handle. Is the handle noticeably dark/black compared to the body, or about the same color? 

[15:57:02] director: The handle may appear darker. You can tell the basket is round in shape, whereas the other basket with a similar description looks like the basket is oval. 

[15:57:08] matcher: For position 2, I think it’s the very round, bulb-shaped basket with the darker handle (not the more oval-shaped one). Can you confirm the basket body is almost perfectly circular from the front, with a dark brown/black-ish handle? 

[15:58:11] director: To me the handle looks the same color, but the lighting in the photo makes it look slightly darker than the bottom. 

[15:58:17] matcher: Okay, for basket #2 I’m going with the very round bowl-shaped one with the single arched handle (the one that reads circular rather than oval). Please describe basket #3. 

[15:59:15] director: The third basket looks like a picnic basket that closes on top but can be opened from each side. It's a rounded rectangular box with a single handle. 

[15:59:24] matcher: For position 3, I think I found the classic picnic basket with two lids that meet in the middle under one handle. Can you confirm it’s a split-top (two hinged flaps), not a flat suitcase-style lidded box? 

[15:59:46] director: That's correct! 

[15:59:52] matcher: Placing the rounded rectangular picnic basket with the two split top flaps and a single handle in position 3. Just to double-check: it has two separate top flaps meeting in the middle (not a single flat lid), right? What’s basket #4 like? 

[16:02:09] director: Yes, that's right. The fourth basket has two colors - a medium brown and a very light or white brown. It has two color rings on the bottom, with the darker color as the first ring and the lighter color on top. The it has a medium brown rim around the top. It has a single handle that starts as two separate strands at the rim of the basket and joins into one at the top. 

[16:02:17] matcher: For position 4, I think it’s the two-tone basket with a darker bottom and a light/whitish upper band, plus the handle that splits into two where it attaches and merges at the top. Can you confirm there’s a clear horizontal band between the dark bottom and light top (and it’s kind of an oval shape rather than perfectly round)? 

[16:03:51] director: It is oval, but I don't know what you mean about the horizontal band. Where the handle joins, there's a white ring on each side. 

[16:03:58] matcher: I think I found it: an oval, two-tone basket with a darker brown lower half and a pale/cream upper band, plus a single handle that attaches in two points with little white rings at the sides. Does the light part form a clear band wrapping around the upper half (not just a couple light spots), and is the basket more oval/boat-shaped than round? 

[16:04:32] director: Yes, it is.

[19:20:17] matcher: For position 8, I think it’s the round basket with the darker gray-brown handle (with some lighter strands) and the banded body: brown rim, lighter tan band, dark band, then tan again at the bottom. Does it also have that slightly scalloped/wavy bottom edge?

[19:21:46] director: Yes. 

[19:21:52] matcher: Locked in basket 8 as the round basket with the dark mixed-color handle and the tan/dark/tan horizontal bands. Please describe basket 9 now—does it have a handle (single arch / side handles / none) and what shape is the body? 

[19:23:38] director: Basket 9 is very tall compared to the previous ones. It is a light brown sort of color. The handles are two small ones on the left and right ends of the circle top opening. [19:23:46] matcher: For position 10, I think it’s the very tall cylindrical light-brown basket with two small loop handles near the rim (around 2 and 8 o’clock) and those rectangular cutout openings near the top. Can you confirm that’s the one?

[19:24:15] matcher: Locked in the round banded basket with the dark mixed-color handle as position 8. Just confirming: for basket 9, do you mean the very tall cylindrical light-brown one with two small loop handles near the rim and rectangular cutout openings near the top?

[19:25:49] matcher: Confirmed—tall cylindrical basket with the little rectangular window cutouts is position 9. Please describe basket 10 now (shape and handle/no-handle, any standout pattern).

[19:31:13] matcher: For basket 11, I think it’s the open tub-style basket with crisscross/diagonal weave and multiple small loop handles—two bigger ones flaring out plus two smaller inner ones. Can you confirm it has no top arch handle and has four side loop handles total?

[19:35:02] matcher: I placed the brown purse/handbag-shaped basket with the single arch handle, slightly angled (left side closer), and the rim dipping a bit in the center into position 12. Just to confirm: does it have that scalloped/wavy bottom edge and a darker interior visible?

transcript:
[19:38:49] director: Hello, position 1 should be the basket with like a picnic basket look. The left half is closed while the right half is open. The latch or covering is leaning diagonally from the center handle to the right edge of the basket top. 

[19:38:56] matcher: For position 1, I think it’s the rectangular picnic-style basket with the center handle and a lid/flap leaning diagonally up to the right, with the left side closed and the right side open. Can you confirm it has that hinged flap with a visible darker interior (not the flat rectangular tray-style basket)? 

[19:39:51] director: It has a darker interior visible. 

[19:39:58] matcher: Placing the rectangular picnic-style basket with the center handle and the diagonal flap leaning to the right (dark interior visible) into position 2. Just to double-check: is it a deep picnic basket (not the shallow flat tray with side rails)? 

[19:43:20] director: Basket 2 is a tall, cylinder shaped basket. It has two small handles on the left and right sides of its top rim. There are these little window like cutouts on the front and back sides of the basket. They are located at the top part of the basket. 

[19:46:41] director: Hello, are you still there? 

[19:46:53] matcher: Yes—I’m here. For position 1, I’m placing the rectangular picnic-style basket with the center handle and the diagonal flap leaning to the right (dark interior showing). Just confirming: it’s the deeper picnic basket with that diagonal flap, not the shallow flat rectangular tray, right? After that, for basket 2 (tall cylinder with two small side handles and window cutouts), I’m ready for any extra detail if needed. 

[19:47:36] director: Yes, those details are correct for both. 

[19:47:45] matcher: Great—then I’m locking in: Position 1 = the deep rectangular picnic-style basket with the center handle and the diagonal flap leaning to the right (dark interior visible). For Position 2, I’m placing the tall cylindrical basket with two small loop handles near the rim and the rectangular cutout ‘windows’ near the top. Just to confirm those window cutouts are near the top, right? Then please describe basket 3. 

[19:49:38] director: Basket 3 is the smooth looking basket. It has a bit of extra smooth texture to it compared to the others. It has a circular bowl shape. There is an overarching handle going from across the top of it. The handle is a darker black-brown color compared to the body. There is like a thin up and down string along the top edge of the basket. 

[19:49:48] matcher: For position 3, I think it’s the round bucket/bowl basket with the very tight smooth weave and the noticeably dark (almost black-brown) arched handle, plus that little vertical stitch detail around the rim. Can you confirm the body is a warm medium-brown and it’s not one of the banded/two-tone baskets? 

[19:50:45] director: Yes it looks like a warm medium-brown and doesn't have a two tone color band like a lot of the others. 

[19:50:56] matcher: I can match basket 3 as the smooth, tight-weave round bowl with the darker black-brown arched handle and the little vertical stitch detail along the rim. Before I place it: it sounds like we’re restarting the order—should I move the deep picnic basket to position 1 and the tall cylinder with window cutouts to position 2 (then place this smooth round one as position 3)? 

[19:51:37] director: Yes, picnic basket in position 1. Tall cylinder in position 2. And this latest one in position 3. 

[19:51:50] matcher: Done—I’ve moved things to match: position 1 is the deep rectangular picnic basket with the center handle and diagonal flap leaning right; position 2 is the tall cylinder with two small side handles and window cutouts; position 3 is the smooth tight-weave round bowl with the dark handle. Please describe basket 4 next (shape/handle and any distinctive pattern or colors). 

[19:54:04] director: Basket 4 has a light brown color. It has an overarching arch going on top of it. The shape below the arch has a more extended rectangle shape with round edges. The center band of the basket body is a darker brown. There are 4 rows of these bands. 

[19:54:14] matcher: For position 4, I think it’s the rounded-rectangle handled basket that’s two-tone, with a darker brown horizontal band across the middle (looks like several dark rows). Is the upper part a lighter/cream weave with a darker brown base, and the handle a warm honey-brown? 

[19:55:20] director: That is correct. 

[19:55:28] matcher: Placing the two-tone rounded-rectangle handled basket (cream/light upper weave, darker brown base, dark brown horizontal band in the middle with multiple rows) into position 4. Just to confirm: the top half is noticeably cream/white and the bottom is darker brown, right? Describe basket 5 next. 

[19:56:35] director: No, the top half is not cream white, it is all a honey-brown like color except for the central dark bands. The dark bands are like a muddy brown. The bands are like little rectangles going across horizontally in the middle. 

[19:56:44] matcher: Got it—I may have mis-set position 4. In my view there are two similar handled baskets: one has a cream/white upper weave with a darker brown base, and another is mostly honey-brown with just a muddy-brown band of small rectangular blocks across the middle (about 4 rows). For this one you mean the mostly honey-brown one with ONLY the muddy-brown block band, no cream/white at all—right? If yes, I’ll correct position 4 accordingly. 

[19:57:26] director: Yes, the mostly honey brown and only muddy brown one. 

[19:57:36] matcher: Thanks—I'll correct position 4 to the mostly honey-brown handled basket with only the muddy-brown rectangular-block band across the middle (no cream/white upper weave). Can you confirm that’s right for basket 4? Then please describe basket 5. 

[20:00:34] director: Basket 5 has 3 main colors for its body. The top rim is wood, like mahogany maybe brown. Below that is a lighter, tan color. The bottom edge has roughly the same color as it. The third layer is the thickest and is a dark brown-black color. There are these little white circle ends between the top rim and the tan layer below it. And the darker brown layer has these vertical columns as well. The handle is a darker black color as well. 

[20:00:44] matcher: For basket 5, I think it’s the round basket with the dark (almost black) handle and the banded body: mahogany-brown rim, tan band, thick very dark middle band, then tan again at the bottom, with little light/white weave-ends right under the rim. Can you confirm it’s that banded one (not the uniformly medium-brown smooth basket)? 

[20:01:25] director: Yes, it has multiple bands of colors. Not one uniform color. 

[20:01:36] matcher: Locked basket 5 as the round banded one with the dark handle (mahogany rim, tan band, thick dark middle, tan bottom). Please describe basket 6 next—shape, handle/no handle, and any distinctive details/patterns? 

[20:03:01] director: Basket 6 is the rabbit shaped basket looking to the right. The ear extends from its head towards the back of its body. There is one black bead for its eye. And its body looks like an oval egg shape. The color is a darker yellow/wheat color. 

[20:03:09] matcher: For position 6, I think it’s the side-profile rabbit basket facing right (single black bead eye, ear extending back, egg-shaped body). Can you confirm it’s the side-view one with only one eye visible (not the front-facing bunny with two eyes/feet)? 

[20:03:25] director: Yes the side view one. 

[20:03:33] matcher: Placing the side-profile rabbit basket facing right (single black bead eye, ear extending back, darker wheat color) into position 6. Just to confirm: it’s the side-profile rabbit (one eye visible), not the round front-facing bunny with two eyes and little feet/pegs, right? 

[20:05:13] director: Basket 7 is the front facing rabbit. I don't know if there are other bunny looking ones. But this one is a lighter shade of color comnpared to position 6. It has its feet at the bottom of the basket. And it looks at the viewers. The head makes it look like it is looking upwards a little bit. 

[20:05:21] matcher: For position 7, I think it’s the front-facing bunny basket (lighter tan than the side-view one), with two dot eyes and little feet/pegs at the bottom, looking slightly upward. Can you confirm it has two visible eyes and those little feet/pegs underneath (not the side-profile rabbit)? 

[20:15:02] director: Basket 8's handle has like a tiny little bump on the bottom part of on the right half. Close to the center of it. 

[20:15:11] matcher: For basket 8: I think it’s the small round brown handled basket where the handle has a little nub/bump on the underside near the middle-right. Can you confirm it also has a slightly wavy/scalloped bottom edge (and darker interior)? 

[20:16:49] director: The interior is mostly the same light color as the central band of its outside. It does not look dark. 

[20:16:58] matcher: For position 8, I think it’s the small purse/handbag-shaped brown basket with a single arch handle—there’s a little bump on the underside of the handle near the middle-right and the inside looks fairly light (not dark). Can you confirm it’s that purse-shaped one (with the slightly wavy/scalloped bottom edge)? 

[20:17:56] director: It is not purse shaped. It looks like a circle pan shape. 

[20:18:05] matcher: For position 8, do you mean the shallow circular “pan/saucepan” basket with one arched handle and a visible spiral/rings pattern in the base (viewed kind of from above)? If yes, I’ll place that one; if not, is it deeper like a bowl? 

[20:18:44] director: Yes, let us go with that one. 

[20:18:52] matcher: Placing the shallow circular “pan/saucepan” basket with a single arched handle (the one you can see the spiral/rings base) into position 8. Just to confirm: you can clearly see that spiral/rings weave on the bottom inside, right? Please describe basket 9 next. 

[20:22:20] director: Basket 9 's dark bands look like a dark green color if zooming in. 

[20:22:28] matcher: For position 9, are you referring to the rectangular basket with the dark green-ish horizontal bands/striped body and a tan braided rim with a single arch handle (no lid)? If yes I’ll place that; if not, is basket 9 round or rectangular? 

[20:22:41] director: Mixed with some lighter green. 

[20:22:50] matcher: For basket 9, I’m thinking it’s the rectangular basket with the greenish (dark + lighter green) horizontal striping and a tan braided rim/arched handle. Can you confirm it’s rectangular/boxy (not round), and has that tan braided edge along the top? 

[20:23:09] director: Yes that one. 

[20:23:18] matcher: Just to align us: I’m filling position 9 now—do you confirm basket 9 is the tall cylindrical one with two small loop handles near the rim and the rectangular window cutouts near the top? If yes, I’ll lock it in and we can go to basket 10. 

[20:31:43] director: Hello are you still there? 

[20:31:54] matcher: Yes, I’m here. I think we got crossed up: for position 9, do you mean the rectangular basket with the green-ish horizontal striping and tan braided rim/single arch handle (no lid), or the tall cylindrical basket with the little window cutouts? Which one is basket 9? 

[20:32:35] director: The first one with the green is position 9. Please just confirm it as that for position 9. 

[20:32:43] matcher: Confirmed—I'm placing the rectangular basket with the green-ish horizontal striping (dark + lighter green) and tan braided rim/arched handle into position 9. Can you confirm it has a single arch handle (not small side loop handles) and no cutout window holes near the rim? Then please describe basket 10. 

[20:33:21] director: Basket 10 is like a shorter version of basket 2. Just the height. Its inside is visible and we see like two noticeable ring "layers". One is near the top edge. The next one is just a little below it. This one has multiple handles on its left and right sides. Basket 10 does not have a single overarching handle. Its overall color is a lightish brown. 

[20:33:29] matcher: For position 10, I think it’s the shorter open tub/cylinder basket with the crisscross/diagonal weave and multiple small loop handles on the sides (no top arch handle). Does it have four loop handles total (two larger outer and two smaller inner) around the rim? 

[20:33:37] director: Yes. 

[20:33:47] matcher: Got it—basket 10 is the open tub/cylinder with four side loop handles (no top arch handle). Do you want me to move that open tub into position 10 (it’s currently placed at position 11 on my board)? Also please describe basket 11 next, and then basket 12 so I can finish the last slots. 

[20:35:10] director: Basket 11 is the one with the top half of its body having very noticeabler white bands on it. The top edge sort of extends over the the rest of the body. 

[20:35:19] matcher: For position 11, I think it’s the tilted/open oval basket where the top half has several bright white horizontal bands and the rim kind of overhangs. Does it have those four separate uprights that come up from the sides/corners and join into the handle at the top (rather than a normal single-piece handle)? 

[20:35:39] director: Yes it does. 

[20:35:49] matcher: Great—then for position 11 I’m selecting the open oval basket with the bright white horizontal bands on the body and the handle made from four uprights that converge at the top. Please describe basket 12 next (shape/handle and any standout details). 

[20:36:37] director: Basket 12 is the purse shaped basket. It has a tall looking body. The handles are on the left and right ends. Moving out from the handle bases the edges curves downwards a bit to form a very wide V. 

[20:36:46] matcher: For position 12, I think it’s the tall handbag/purse-shaped basket with two small side loop handles (left and right) and a rim that dips down to a low center forming a wide V. [20:41:10] director: Hello, the first basket is the basket with the dark green bands in the middle section. The top and bottom edges of the body are a brown color. It has an overarching handle going from left to right.

[20:41:43] director: Yes it is rectangular shaped and no lid. 

[20:41:52] matcher: I see the rectangular basket with dark green horizontal bands and tan/brown rim/bottom, with one arch handle and no lid. Just to confirm—are we restarting the sequence for this round, and should I place this as position 1 (replacing what’s currently in position 1 on my board)? 

[20:42:53] director: Yes, we are restarting the sequence as the instructions have shown. This basket should be in position 1.Basket 2 has the very noticeable white bands on the top half of its basket body. It has an overarching handle with the ends splitting into twos.

For basket 2: do you mean the light oval basket with several bright white horizontal bands on the upper body, and the handle formed by four uprights that split into two on each side before meeting at the top?

Just confirming for position 1: it’s the green-banded one (not the darker gray/black-banded rounder one), right? 

[20:44:08] director: Basket 3 is the lighter colored rabbit one facing the viewer. It looks like it is looking up a bit. It has 3 red strings for its whiskers on each side.

[20:45:04] matcher: Perfect—positions 1–3 are confirmed. Please describe basket 4 next (shape, handle type, and any distinctive colors/patterns).

[20:45:24] matcher: Great—basket 3 is locked in as the front-facing bunny. Please describe basket 4: what shape is it (round/oval/rectangular), does it have a handle or lid, and any distinctive bands/patterns/colors? 

[20:45:25] director: Basket 4 is the bunny looking to the right. The side-profile rabbit with one eye. 

[20:45:36] matcher: For position 4, I’m placing the side-profile rabbit facing right (one eye visible). Can you confirm it’s the side-view bunny (not the front-facing one with two eyes and little feet/pegs)? Then please describe basket 5.

[20:45:57] matcher: Confirmed position 4 as the side-profile rabbit facing right (one eye visible). Please describe basket 5—does it have a lid, what shape is it (round/rectangular/tall cylinder), and any standout colors/bands/white stripes or other unique details? 

[20:47:05] director: Basket 5 is the purse shaped basket. Please get this one right. The body of the purse is tall. It has an overarching handle. The space below the handle looks very much like a top half of a circle. The top edge of this purse shape basket goes down at the center though. So the handle parts are a bit taller than the front and back centers. The color is overall a brown color with a bit of a red hue to it. 

[20:47:14] matcher: For position 5, I think it’s the tall purse/handbag-shaped brown basket with a single arch handle and a rim that dips down in the center. Does it have that wavy/scalloped bottom edge and a kind of peanut-shaped opening?

[20:47:54] matcher: For position 5, I’m looking for the tall handbag/purse-shaped brown basket with one arched handle and a rim that dips down in the center (but with a smooth bottom edge—no scallops—and not a peanut-shaped opening). [20:48:07] director: Yes that is correct. 

[20:48:15] matcher: Placing the tall purse/handbag-shaped brown basket with a single continuous arch handle and a rim that dips down at the center (smooth bottom edge, not peanut-shaped) into position 5. [20:48:54] matcher: Got it—position 5 is the tall reddish-brown purse/handbag basket with a single arch handle, smooth bottom edge, and the rim dipping at the center (not the wavy/scalloped one). Please describe basket 6 next: shape, handle type, and any distinctive bands/stripes/cutouts or lid?

[20:51:07] matcher: For position 7, I’m selecting the smooth, tight-weave round bowl basket with the single very dark (almost black) arched handle. Can you confirm the body is a uniform medium-brown with no multi-color horizontal bands?

[20:52:05] matcher: Great—position 7 is locked as the smooth tight-weave round bowl basket with the very dark single arch handle. Please describe basket 8 next (shape, handle type, and any distinctive colors/bands/patterns or lid/cutouts).

[20:52:43] matcher: For position 8, I think it’s the round basket with the brown rim, a tan band, a thick dark brown-black middle band, and tan again at the bottom—with those little light/white weave-ends right under the rim. [20:54:25] director: Yes that is correct. 

[20:54:33] matcher: Placing the round banded basket (brown rim, tan band, thick dark brown-black middle band, tan bottom) with the very dark arched handle into position 8. Just to confirm: the handle is very dark/black and the dark middle band is the widest section, right? Please describe basket 9 next. 

[20:55:01] director: Yes correct. Basket 9 is the one with a square-rectanglular shaped handle. 

[20:55:09] matcher: For basket 9: do you mean the small basket whose handle opening looks more like a rounded rectangle/square (less of a semicircle), and it’s a low rectangular/rounded-rectangle body with no lid? Or is it the deeper picnic basket with a diagonal flap? 

[20:55:11] director: The height of the handle from its body looks like it is longer than the body height. 

[20:55:19] matcher: For position 9, I think it’s the low rounded-rectangle basket with the more square/rounded-rectangle handle opening, where the handle is taller than the body. Can you confirm the weave looks like a little lattice/grid and there’s no lid (so it’s not the two-tone picnic basket)? 

[20:55:42] director: The body is a low rectangle. And yes correct. 

[20:55:52] matcher: Placing the small low rectangular basket with the more squared/rounded-rectangle handle opening (handle taller than the body, no lid) into position 9. Just to confirm: it’s the lattice-weave one and not the picnic basket with the diagonal flap, right? Please describe basket 10 next. 

[20:56:30] director: Basket 10 is another cylinder shaped basket. This one is shorter than the taller one previously discussed. This one has multiple handles pointing outwards from its body. It has two noticeable rings in its interior view. 

[20:56:39] matcher: For position 10, I think it’s the shorter open tub/cylinder basket with multiple small side loop handles flaring outward and you can see two interior ring layers near the top. Can you confirm it’s the one with four loop handles total (two bigger outer + two smaller inner), and not the tall cylinder with the little window cutouts near the rim? 

[20:56:49] director: Yes that is correct. 

[20:56:59] matcher: Great—then for position 10 I’m placing the shorter cylindrical open tub basket with four side loop handles (two bigger outer + two smaller inner) and the two visible interior ring layers. Just confirming: it’s the open tub with the diagonal/crisscross weave and no overhead handle, right? Please describe basket 11 next. 

[20:57:40] director: Basket 11 is the flat looking basket. It looks like a stir fry pan with an overarching light brown handle above it. The inside center looks like a brown square with ring shapes around it. The top and bottom edges of the body is a brown color. 

[20:57:49] matcher: For basket 11, I think it’s the shallow pan/wok-like basket with one light-brown arch handle and the spiral/rings base with that darker square-ish center. Can you confirm it has a single centered arch handle (not side loop handles) and that spiral base is clearly visible? 

[20:58:09] director: Yes that is correct. 

[20:58:20] matcher: Got it. For position 11 I’m selecting the shallow pan/wok-like basket with the single light-brown arch handle and the visible spiral/rings base with the darker square-ish center. Just to confirm: it’s very shallow and shot from above so the spiral base is obvious, right? Please describe basket 12 next. 

[20:58:48] director: Basket 12 is the picnic looking one. The left half of its top is closed. The right half is open and and we can see a dark inside. The top part of the cover is forming a diagonal line with the handles, which are located in the center. 

[20:58:58] matcher: I have basket 12 as the deep picnic-style basket with the center handle and the diagonal flap, where the left half is closed and the right half is open showing a dark interior. Before I lock it in: should I place that picnic basket in position 12 now (and keep position 5 as the tall reddish purse basket with a smooth bottom edge)? 

[20:59:17] director: Yes that is correct. 

[20:59:26] matcher: Confirming: we’re restarting this round, and you want the rectangular basket with dark green horizontal bands (tan/brown rim and base), single arch handle, no lid placed in position 1—correct? 

[20:59:36] director: yes 

[20:59:47] matcher: Confirmed—position 1 is the rectangular basket with dark green horizontal bands in the middle, tan/brown rim and base, a single arch handle, and no lid. Please continue with the next basket description when ready. 

[21:00:32] director: Please refer to the previous messages. We have finished. Please send it for final confirmation. 

[21:00:47] matcher: Here’s the full order we arrived at: 1) rectangular basket with dark green horizontal bands, tan rim/base, single arch handle, no lid 2) light oval basket with bright white bands and the split/four-upright handle 3) front-facing light bunny with red whiskers and little feet/pegs 4) side-profile bunny facing right (one eye) 5) tall reddish-brown purse basket with single arch handle and a center dip on the rim (smooth bottom edge) 6) tall cylinder with two small side handles and window cutouts near the top 7) smooth tight-weave round bowl with very dark arch handle (uniform brown body) 8) round banded basket: brown rim, tan band, thick dark middle band, tan bottom, dark handle, little white weave-ends under rim 9) low rectangular basket with the more squared/rounded-rectangle handle opening (handle taller than body), no lid 10) shorter open tub cylinder with four side loop handles (no overhead handle), two interior ring layers 11) shallow pan/wok basket with single arch handle and visible spiral/rings base with darker square-ish center 12) deep picnic basket with center handle and diagonal flap (left closed, right open, dark interior) Please confirm position 5 is the smooth-bottom (not wavy) purse basket—then I’m ready to submit. 

[21:03:28] director: I confirm 

[21:03:37] matcher: Confirmed—position 5 is the smooth-bottom purse basket. I’m ready to submit the full 12-basket sequence as listed.

(e.g., tall cylinder with 2 small loop handles vs round bucket with 1 arch handle vs shallow tray vs rectangular/oval). Any stripes/bands?

[20:53:38] director: yes the bucket 

[20:53:50] matcher: Great—locking basket #6 as the deep round bucket with the single smooth dark arch handle. Now please describe basket #7 (shape + handle type + any standout pattern/stripe/animal detail) so I can pick the right one.

Light brown wicker weaved with white, very open weave, no lid. [20:18:27] director: Correct the obvious white open weave, I think you got it. 

[20:18:33] matcher: Great—#4 set as the small shallow oval with the obvious white open weave and thin arched handle. Please describe basket #5 (shape, handle type, and any distinctive colors/patterns or lid).

[16:07:32] director: yes. 6 is bucket shaped. It has a darker brown/black handle which is smooth. It has a smooth rim and a tightly woven body. The rim is shiny. It is all brown

[16:08:27] director: yes. 7 is a very tall round basket. It has tiny ear shaped handles on the sides. There are window like openings around the top. It is all one color brown.

[16:12:57] director: 9 is a bunny seen from the front. It has small feet on the bottom and black button eyes and small ears folded forward 

[16:13:07] matcher: We still need basket #8 described before I can place #9. Can you describe basket #8 (shape/handle/lid/colors) so I can fill the next empty slot? Then for #9, just confirming: it’s the front-facing bunny with black button eyes and little feet visible, with small ears folded forward and no lid seam—right? 

[16:13:51] director: 8 is a picnic basket with a lid and the right side of the basket's lid is open. It has thick woven handle and it is all one color 

[16:14:01] matcher: Got it—basket #8 sounds like the classic picnic basket with a thick center handle and the right lid flap propped open. I’m placing that into position 8. Can you confirm it’s the split-lid picnic basket (not the flat trunk-style lidded box)? Then we can place basket #9 (the front-facing bunny with feet).

[16:15:12] matcher: For basket #10, I’m selecting the oval/oblong basket with the split handles (two attachment points per side) that meet at the top, with two white wrap bands holding them together and a white weave section near the top. [16:19:12] director: 12 is a small round basket with a black handle. It has a light tan rim and base with a woven black center with ribbing/bonig woven into it. 

[16:19:23] matcher: I can place #10 once I confirm one detail: is the white weave ONLY near the top (a band under the rim), and do the split handles meet at the top with two white wrap bands? [16:21:30] matcher: For basket #2, I’m going to place the open oval basket with ONE tall braided/twisted handle and that flat rim with visible lashing loops (handle/rim slightly lighter than the darker body). [16:22:00] director: yes. 12 is a small round basket with a black handle. It has a light tan rim and base with a woven black center with ribbing/bonig woven into it. 

[16:22:13] matcher: For basket #12, I think it’s the small round two-tone one with a very dark/black smooth handle, a light tan rim and base, and a dark ribbed middle band. If yes, we’re fully filled and ready to submit. 

[16:22:19] director: that is all the baskets. please submit your selections 

[16:22:33] matcher: I’m placing the deep oval honey-brown basket with the smooth handle, darker interior, and rope-twisted edging (top and bottom) into position 12. We now have all 12 positions filled, so I’m ready to submit—please confirm that rim dips lower on the long sides and the twisted edging is on both top and bottom.

transcript:
[19:22:18] director: Rabbit goes first. Round, brown with large handle next. Folding basket next. Brown and white rectangular next. Shallow brown rectangular basket next. Mouse face at the end. Next is light and dark brown rectangular basket. Next is round tricolor. Then tall wastebasket. Light, shallow, round basket next. Round basket with double handles is next. Last is the tall basket with sloped opening. 

[19:22:29] matcher: Placing the rabbit-shaped lidded basket in position 1. Quick check: it’s the side-profile rabbit with ears (not the round mouse-face one), right? If yes, describe basket #2 (round brown with large handle). 

[19:23:15] director: Yes. Round, brown with large handle next. 

[19:23:23] matcher: For position 2, I think it’s the deep round brown basket with the thick dark arched handle. Does it have that noticeably dark handle and a darker rim at the top edge? 

[19:23:58] director: The handle is thicker and darker, yes. 

[19:24:07] matcher: Got it—placing the deep round brown basket with the thick dark handle in position 2. Next basket is the folding basket—can you confirm if it’s the flat, lidded/tray-like one that looks like it collapses? 

[19:24:23] director: Correct 

[19:24:31] matcher: For position 3, I think the folding basket is the flat rectangular one with the two long bar handles/rails along the sides. Is that the one (not the picnic basket with a hinged lid)? 

[19:25:15] director: Position 3 is the picnic basket with hinged lid 

[19:25:26] matcher: Placing the picnic basket with the hinged lid in position 3. To confirm: does it have a single arched handle/strap over the top (flip-up lid), not the flat folding tray with side rails? 

[19:26:05] director: yes 

[19:26:14] matcher: Okay, position 3 locked in as the picnic basket with the hinged lid and single arched handle. For position 4 (brown and white rectangular), is it the two-tone basket with one arched handle and a light upper band over a darker lower half? 

[19:26:30] director: yes 

[19:26:41] matcher: Placing the two-tone brown-and-white rectangular basket with the single arched handle in position 4. Next is the shallow brown rectangular basket—does it look like a low open tray with no lid and no tall handle? 

[19:27:26] director: No lid, but yes a tall handle 

[19:27:38] matcher: For position 5: is it the shallow rectangular basket with a tall arched handle that has greenish/olive weaving and a tan rim/handle (with a slightly wavy/scalloped band near the top), rather than a plain honey-brown one? 

[19:28:01] director: yes 

[19:28:11] matcher: Placing the shallow rectangular basket with the tall handle and greenish/olive weave in position 5. Next is the mouse-face one—just to confirm, is it the round lidded basket with the mouse face on top (eyes and whiskers)? 

[19:28:40] director: yes 

[19:28:50] matcher: Placing the round lidded basket with the mouse face (eyes/whiskers looking forward) in position 6. Confirm it’s the full face-on mouse head, basically a round ball shape? If yes, please describe the next one (light and dark brown rectangular). 

[19:29:32] director: Yes. Next is two-tone, deep rectangular basket 

[19:29:49] matcher: For position 7, I think it’s the deep two-tone rectangular basket with a single arched handle (light top, darker lower half). Does it have that kind of scalloped darker band around the middle, and no lid? 

[19:30:05] director: yes 

[19:30:17] matcher: Placing the deep two-tone rectangular basket with the single arched handle (light top, darker bottom) into position 7. Next should be the round tricolor basket—can you describe its colors/pattern (e.g., striped bands vs a geometric motif)? 

[19:31:30] director: Small light band, then large dark band, then small light band on bottom 

[19:31:38] matcher: For position 8, I think it’s the small round basket with a very dark handle and distinct light–dark–light horizontal bands on the body. Does it have that almost black handle and dark rim at the top? 

[19:32:00] director: yes 

[19:32:12] matcher: Placing the round tricolor basket with the very dark handle (light–dark–light bands) in position 8. After this, is the ‘tall wastebasket’ the cylindrical one with two small side loop handles (no big arch handle)? 

[19:32:36] director: yes, tallest of the bunch 

[19:32:51] matcher: Placing the tallest cylindrical wastebasket with two small side loop handles in position 9. Just to confirm: it’s the tall cylinder with the two little loop handles near the top (not the shorter open round bin or the tall lidded trunk), right? What’s basket #10 (light, shallow, round)? 

[19:33:35] director: yes. #10 is shallowest of the bunch with dark rim and light body 

[19:33:47] matcher: For position 10, I think it’s the very shallow round open tray with a light body and a darker rim (no tall arched handle), with a spiral weave on the base. Can you confirm it has no arched handle and looks like a low platter/tray? 

[19:34:26] director: It has a tall arched, light colored handle 

[19:34:36] matcher: For position 10, I think it’s the shallow round basket with a tall thin light-colored handle and a darker rim/band at the top. Can you confirm it’s wide/low (not deep) and has just that single tall arch handle? 

[19:34:48] director: yes 

[19:35:00] matcher: Great—position 10 is set as the shallow round basket with the tall thin light handle. For position 11 (the round basket with double handles), do you mean it has two short loop handles on the sides (and no big arched handle over the top)? 

[19:35:34] director: Yes, each handle on each side has two handles for a total of four 

[19:35:42] matcher: Placing the open round brown bin with four small loop handles (two per side) into position 11. Please confirm it’s not the tall cylindrical wastebasket we used earlier, and then describe the last basket (#12: tall basket with sloped opening). 

[19:36:31] director: Correct. Next is deep basket with short arched handle and sloped opening 

[19:36:44] matcher: For position 12, I think it’s the deep oval basket with a single short arched handle and an uneven/sloped rim (one side higher). Can you confirm it’s that asymmetrical rim basket (not a shallow tray or a tall-handled one)?
