transcript:
[19:38:55] director: picnic basket first 

[19:39:06] matcher: For position 1, I’m selecting the rectangular picnic basket with the hinged lid and a single arched handle over the top (not the flatter folding tray with side rails). Can you confirm that’s the right picnic basket, and then describe basket #2? 

[19:39:32] director: Yes. Tall wastebasket next 

[19:39:42] matcher: Placing the tallest cylindrical wastebasket in position 2. Quick confirm: it has two small loop handles near the top (not the shorter open bin with four loops), right? What’s basket #3? 

[19:40:08] director: Yes. Round, deep basket with dark handle 

[19:40:16] matcher: For position 3, I think it’s the deep round bucket-shaped basket with the noticeably thick dark handle (and darker rim). Can you confirm the handle is very dark/thick compared to the body, and the basket is deep like a pail? 

[19:40:45] director: Yes 

[19:40:56] matcher: Placing the deep round basket with the very thick dark handle in position 3. Next basket description please (position 4). 

[19:41:29] director: Rectangular. Tall handle. Handle and rim light. Body dark 

[19:41:40] matcher: For position 4, I think it’s the rectangular basket with a tall tan/light handle and light rim, with a darker green/olive body (and a braided band near the top). Is that the one? 

[19:42:21] director: No. Body, handle and rim are all brown 

[19:42:31] matcher: For position 4, do you mean the small squat rectangular basket with a thick twisted/rope-style arched handle, all in brown tones? Or is it a larger deeper rectangle with a smoother (not rope-twisted) handle? Any detail on the weave/pattern would help. 

[19:43:19] director: Handle is braided like rope. basket is shallow 

[19:43:28] matcher: For position 4, I’m going with the shallow rectangular basket that has the thick braided/rope-style arched handle (all brown). Can you confirm the sides aren’t green/olive, and then describe basket #5? 

[19:44:19] director: Yes, no olive color. #5 is the olive color basket with the three alternating bands: light, dark, light 

[19:44:32] matcher: For position 5, I’m selecting the olive/greenish rectangular basket with the tall arched handle and the three horizontal bands (light–dark–light). Can you confirm it’s rectangular with a tan braided rim (not an oval two-tone basket)? Then please describe basket #6. 

[19:45:06] director: Yes. #6 is rabbit. 

[19:45:16] matcher: Placing the rabbit-shaped lidded basket in position 6. Confirm it’s the side-profile rabbit with ears (not the round mouse-face one), and then tell me basket #7. 

[19:45:39] director: Yes. #7 is mouse with whiskers 

[19:45:52] matcher: Placing the mouse-face basket with whiskers in position 7. Quick confirm: it’s the round lidded one with the mouse face looking up/front (eyes, nose, whiskers), not the rabbit side-profile. What’s basket #8? 

[19:46:24] director: Yes. #8 is shallowest round basket with tall light handle 

[19:46:35] matcher: For position 8, I’m placing the very shallow round basket with the tall thin light-colored handle. Does it have that spiral-coil base and a very thin pale handle (vs a thicker wrapped handle)? If yes, please describe basket #9. 

[19:47:33] director: Yes. #9 is rectangular and deep with light handle and rim, dark body and then a light bottom trim 

[19:47:43] matcher: For position 9, I think it’s the deep two-tone rectangular basket with a pale handle and rim, a darker middle body, and a thin pale strip along the very bottom edge (no lid). Can you confirm that bottom light trim is clearly visible? 

[19:48:38] director: Yes. #10 is four handled round deep basket 

[19:48:47] matcher: Placing the deep round open basket with four small loop handles (two on each side) in position 10. Just to confirm it’s the open bin with four loops (no big arched handle over the top). What’s basket #11? 

[19:50:05] director: Yes. #11 is two toned rectangular. Handle has four attachment points at basket 

[19:50:17] matcher: For position 11, I think it’s the two-tone rectangular basket where the arched handle is supported/attached at four points (two on each side). Is it the whitish/tan basket with that braced handle, rather than the deeper two-tone rectangular with a simpler handle? 

[19:50:40] director: Yes. #12 is deep basket with sloped rim 

[19:50:51] matcher: Placing the deep basket with the sloped/uneven rim and a single short arched handle in position 12. Can you confirm the rim is clearly higher on one side (asymmetric opening) and it’s a uniform medium-brown (not white/striped)? 

[19:51:04] director: yes 

[19:51:13] matcher: Confirmed. I’ve got all 12 positions filled—should I submit the sequence now?

transcript:
[19:52:04] director: Two toned deep basket with light handle and rim and light band at bottom 

[19:52:15] matcher: For position 1, I think it’s the deep two-tone rectangular basket with the pale handle/rim and a thin pale band along the very bottom edge. Can you confirm the bottom light trim is clearly visible all the way across (and it’s definitely rectangular, not oval)? 

[19:53:08] director: All correct. Not sure if rectangular or oval because it is a side view 

[19:53:18] matcher: I think I found that one: the deep two‑tone basket with the pale handle/rim and a thin pale band at the very bottom. From my view it looks like the oval/boat-shaped one (side view) rather than a boxy rectangle—does it also have a tiny reddish accent on the handle? 

[19:53:58] director: Not that I can tell 

[19:54:10] matcher: Okay—I’m going to place the deep two-tone basket with the light handle/rim and the thin light band at the bottom in position 2. Can you confirm if the overall silhouette is more oval/boat-shaped (curved sides) rather than boxy-rectangular? 

[19:54:38] director: Probably curved sides 

[19:54:51] matcher: Got it—then I’ll keep that deep two-tone, curved/oval basket with the light handle/rim and thin light bottom trim in position 2. What’s basket #3 (shape + any standout details)? 

[19:55:22] director: #3 mouse 

[19:55:31] matcher: For position 1, I’m placing the round lidded mouse basket with the whiskers/face on top. Can you confirm it’s the mouse (not the side-profile rabbit), and then describe basket #4? 

[19:55:49] director: Yes. #4 rabbit 

[19:56:01] matcher: Placing the side-profile rabbit-shaped lidded basket with ears in position 4. Can you confirm it’s the rabbit (not the round mouse-face lid with whiskers), and then describe basket #5? 

[19:56:18] director: Yes. #5 Deep basket, sloped rim 

[19:56:30] matcher: For position 5, I think it’s the deep basket with the clearly sloped/uneven rim and a single short arched handle (uniform brown). Is the rim definitely higher on one side, with no white/olive banding? 

[19:57:04] director: Yes. #6 is tall wastebasket 

[19:57:15] matcher: Placing the tallest cylindrical wastebasket in position 6. Does it have two small loop handles near the top (not four loops and no big arched handle)? What’s basket #7? 

[19:57:50] director: Yes. #7 is deep, round basket with dark smooth handle 

[19:58:03] matcher: For position 3, I’m selecting the deep round basket with the smooth dark arched handle (pail/bucket shape). Can you confirm the handle is smooth (not braided like rope) and there are no light/dark horizontal bands on the body? Then what’s basket #8? 

[19:59:22] director: Yes. #8 is two tone body: light rim, dark body and light band on bottom. Handle is dark and braided like rope 

[19:59:32] matcher: For position 7, I think #8 is the two-tone basket with a dark braided/rope arched handle plus a light rim and a thin light band at the very bottom. Is it more boxy-rectangular with straighter sides (not oval), and does the handle attach at the corners? 

[20:00:03] director: basket is round 

[20:00:14] matcher: Got it—so it’s a ROUND basket with a dark braided/rope handle, plus a light rim and a thin light band at the very bottom. I think I see that one; can you confirm it has clear light–dark–light horizontal banding on the body (not a smooth all-brown body)? 

[20:01:29] director: yes. #9 is oval and shallow with large handle. Body is dark. Handle and rim are light 

[20:01:42] matcher: I’m still filling position 7: for #8, I’m looking for a ROUND basket with a dark braided/rope handle and clear light–dark–light horizontal banding (light rim + thin light band at the very bottom). Can you confirm the handle is braided/rope (not smooth) and the striping is very distinct? Once confirmed I’ll lock it in, then we’ll go to #9 (oval/shallow, dark body with light rim/handle). 

[20:03:02] director: #8 is correct. 

[20:03:16] matcher: Great—I’m locking #8 into position 7 as the round basket with the dark braided/rope handle and the light–dark–light banding. For basket #9 (oval/shallow, dark body with light rim/handle), does it have two small upright supports that connect the rim to the handle? 

[20:03:51] director: zno 

[20:04:03] matcher: Okay for basket #9 (oval + shallow, dark body with light rim/handle, and NO extra upright supports): is it the squat oval basket with a single big arch handle and a darker interior (not the greenish rectangular one and not the lighter two-tone oval with a golden handle)? 

[20:04:04] director: Sorry. No. 

[20:04:15] matcher: I’m stuck on basket #9. Is it the oval shallow basket that’s clearly two‑tone (lighter upper band over darker lower) with a golden/yellowish handle, or is the whole body mostly dark with just a light rim/handle? Any extra detail on the side weave/pattern would help. 

[20:06:05] director: #9 is oval and shallow. Handle and rim are light golden colored. Body is darker brown. handle is braided like rope 

[20:06:15] matcher: For position 8, I’m selecting the oval shallow basket with the light golden braided/rope handle and light rim, and a darker brown body. Can you confirm the handle is clearly straw-golden and rope-braided (not a smooth dark handle), and then describe basket #10? 

[20:06:49] director: Yes. #10 is deep round basket with four handles 

[20:07:02] matcher: For position 2, I’m placing the deep round open basket with four small loop handles (two per side). Just to confirm: it’s the shorter open bin with FOUR loops, not the tall cylindrical wastebasket with only two loops near the top—right? What’s basket #11? 

[20:08:02] director: Yes. #11 is the shallowest basket. It's round and light colored with circular pattern in the bottom 

[20:08:13] matcher: For position 9, I think it’s the very shallow round light-colored tray basket with a spiral/circular pattern in the base and no tall arched handle. Can you confirm there’s no upright handle at all (just an open shallow platter)? 

[20:08:45] director: No, it has a tall arched light-colored handle 

[20:08:59] matcher: Got it—so #11 is the shallow round light basket WITH a tall pale arched handle and a visible spiral/circular base. Is the handle very thin and light-colored (almost like a single narrow strip), and the basket is extremely shallow like a platter? 

[20:09:19] director: Yes. 

[20:09:30] matcher: Placing the very shallow round light basket with the tall thin pale arched handle and the spiral/circular base pattern into position 9. Can you confirm the handle is very thin and pale (not a thicker wrapped handle)? What’s basket #12? 

[20:09:55] director: Yes. #12 is picnic basket 

[20:10:06] matcher: For position 10, I’m placing the rectangular picnic basket with the hinged lid (the lidded one, not an open-top basket). Can you confirm it’s the hinged-lid picnic basket, and then tell me what basket #1 is (we may need to revisit since I’ve been filling out of order)? 

[20:10:41] director: #12 is the picnic basket. 

[20:10:52] matcher: I currently have positions 1–10 filled, and positions 11–12 are still empty in my sequence. Can you describe basket #11 next (shape + handles + any standout color/pattern) so I can place it in position 11? 

[20:11:25] director: #11 is round, shallow with circular pattern and tall arched light colored handle 

[20:11:36] matcher: Placing the very shallow round basket with the tall thin pale arched handle and the circular/spiral base pattern into position 11. Can you confirm the handle is that thin, light straw-colored arch? What’s basket #12 (the last empty position) again? 

[20:11:50] director: Yes. #12 is the picnic basket 

[20:12:03] matcher: I still have position 9 empty. I’m going to place the oval, shallow basket with the light golden braided/rope handle and light rim but darker brown body there. Can you confirm the handle is definitely that golden rope-braid (not smooth/dark)? 

[20:12:21] director: Correct. 

[20:12:31] matcher: Placing the rectangular picnic basket with the hinged lid into position 12. Just to confirm: it’s the picnic basket with the flip/hinged lid (not the flat folding tray with side rails), right? 

[20:12:41] director: yes 

[20:12:51] matcher: I’m at the remaining empty slot (position 10) in our current Round 3 sequence. Can you re-describe the basket for position 10 (shape + handle style + any lid/pattern/colors) so I can select the right one? 

[20:13:13] director: #10 is the round deep basket with four handles 

[20:13:27] matcher: Placing the deep round open basket with FOUR small loop handles (two per side) into position 10. Just to confirm, it’s the shorter one with four loops—not the tall cylindrical wastebasket with only two loops near the top—right? If that’s correct, we should be ready to submit.
